text.NRSV: These are the words that Moses spoke to all Israel beyond the Jordan—in the wilderness, on the plain opposite Suph, between Paran and Tophel, Laban, Hazeroth, and Di-zahab.
text.Rotherham: These, are the words, which Moses spake unto all Israel, over the Jordan,—in the wilderness in the waste plain over against Suph, between Paran and Tophel, and Laban and Hazeroth and Di-zahab:
text.KJV: 1:1 These bee the woordes which Moses spake vnto all Israel, on this side Iordane in the wildernes, in the plaine ouer against the Red sea, betweene Paran, and Tophel, and Laban, and Hazeroth, and Dizahab.
text.NABRE: 1:1 I. First Address Chapter 1 - Introduction. These are the words that Moses spoke to all Israel beyond the Jordan in the wilderness, in the Arabah, opposite Suph, between Paran and Tophel, Laban, Hazeroth, and Dizahab.

text.NRSV: (By the way of Mount Seir it takes eleven days to reach Kadesh-barnea from Horeb.)
text.Rotherham: eleven days from Horeb, by way of Mount Seir, as far as Kadesh-barnea.
text.KJV: 1:2 (There are eleuen daies iourney from Horeb, by the way of mount Seir, vnto Kadesh Barnea.)
text.NABRE: 1:2 It is a journey of eleven days from Horeb to Kadesh-barnea by way of the highlands of Seir.

text.NRSV: In the fortieth year, on the first day of the eleventh month, Moses spoke to the Israelites just as the Lord had commanded him to speak to them.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in the fortieth year, in the eleventh month, on the first of the month, that Moses spake unto the sons of Israel, according to all that Yahweh had given him in charge for them;
text.KJV: 1:3 And it came to passe in the fourtieth yeere, in the eleuenth moneth, on the first day of the moneth, that Moses spake vnto the children of Israel, according vnto all that the Lord had giuen him in commandement vnto them:
text.NABRE: 1:3 In the fortieth year, on the first day of the eleventh month, Moses spoke to the Israelites according to all that the LORD had commanded him to speak to them,

text.NRSV: This was after he had defeated King Sihon of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon, and King Og of Bashan, who reigned in Ashtaroth and in Edrei.
text.Rotherham: after he had smitten Sihon, king of the Amorites, who dwelt in Heshbon,—and Og, king of Bashan, who dwelt in Ashtaroth, in Edrei:
text.KJV: 1:4 After hee had slaine Sihon the King of the Amorites, which dwelt in Heshbon, and Og the King of Bashan, which dwelt at Astaroth, in Edrei.
text.NABRE: 1:4 after he had defeated Sihon, king of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon, and Og, king of Bashan, who reigned in Ashtaroth and in Edrei.

text.NRSV: Beyond the Jordan in the land of Moab, Moses undertook to expound this law as follows:
text.Rotherham: over the Jordan, in the land of Moab, did Moses take in hand to expound this law, saying:—
text.KJV: 1:5 On this side Iordane, in the land of Moab, began Moses to declare this law, saying,
text.NABRE: 1:5 Beyond the Jordan, in the land of Moab, Moses undertook to explain this law:

text.NRSV: The Lord our God spoke to us at Horeb, saying, “You have stayed long enough at this mountain.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh our God, spake unto us in Horeb, saying, Long enough, have ye dwelt in this mountain:
text.KJV: 1:6 The Lord our God spake vnto vs in Horeb, saying, Ye haue dwelt long ynough in this mount:
text.NABRE: 1:6 Departure from Horeb. The LORD, our God, said to us at Horeb: You have stayed long enough at this mountain.

text.NRSV: Resume your journey, and go into the hill country of the Amorites as well as into the neighboring regions—the Arabah, the hill country, the Shephelah, the Negeb, and the seacoast—the land of the Canaanites and the Lebanon, as far as the great river, the river Euphrates.
text.Rotherham: Turn ye and set yourselves forward, and enter into the hill country of the Amorites, and into all the places near, in the plain, in the mountain, and in the lowland, and in the south, and in the coast of the sea,—the land of Canaan and the Lebanon, as far as the great river, the river Euphrates.
text.KJV: 1:7 Turne you, and take your iourney, and goe to the mount of the Amorites, and vnto all the places nigh thereunto, in the plaine, in the hills, and in the vale, and in the South, and by the sea side, to the land of the Canaanites, and vnto Lebanon, vnto the great riuer, the riuer Euphrates.
text.NABRE: 1:7 Leave here and go to the hill country of the Amorites and to all the surrounding regions, the Arabah, the mountains, the Shephelah, the Negeb and the seacoast—the land of the Canaanites and the Lebanon as far as the Great River, the Euphrates.

text.NRSV: See, I have set the land before you; go in and take possession of the land that I swore to your ancestors, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give to them and to their descendants after them.”
text.Rotherham: See! I have set before you the land,—enter and possess the land, which, Yahweh sware unto your fathers to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give unto them, and unto their seed, after them.
text.KJV: 1:8 Behold, I haue set the land before you: Goe in, and possesse the land, which the Lord sware vnto your fathers, Abraham, Isaac, and Iacob, to giue vnto them, and to their seed after them.
text.NABRE: 1:8 See, I have given that land over to you. Go now and possess the land that the LORD swore to your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, to give to them and to their descendants after them.

text.NRSV: At that time I said to you, “I am unable by myself to bear you.
text.Rotherham: So I spake unto you, at that time saying, I am unable, by myself, to carry you.
text.KJV: 1:9 ¶ And I spake vnto you at that time, saying, I am not able to beare you my selfe alone:
text.NABRE: 1:9 Appointment of Elders. At that time I said to you, “I am unable to carry you by myself.

text.NRSV: The Lord your God has multiplied you, so that today you are as numerous as the stars of heaven.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh your God hath multiplied you, and here ye are to-day, like the stars of the heavens for multitude.
text.KJV: 1:10 The Lord your God hath multiplied you, and beholde, you are this day as the starres of heauen for multitude.
text.NABRE: 1:10 The LORD, your God, has made you numerous, and now you are as numerous as the stars of the heavens.

text.NRSV: May the Lord, the God of your ancestors, increase you a thousand times more and bless you, as he has promised you!
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, the God of your fathers, be adding unto you the like of you, a thousand times,—and bless you, as he hath spoken unto you!
text.KJV: 1:11 (The Lord God of your fathers make you a thousand times so many moe as ye are, and blesse you as he hath promised you.)
text.NABRE: 1:11 May the LORD, the God of your ancestors, increase you a thousand times over, and bless you as he promised!

text.NRSV: But how can I bear the heavy burden of your disputes all by myself?
text.Rotherham: How should I carry by myself the fatigue of you and the burden of you and your controversies?
text.KJV: 1:12 How can I my selfe alone beare your cumbrance, and your burden, and your strife?
text.NABRE: 1:12 But how can I, by myself, bear the weight, the contentiousness of you?

text.NRSV: Choose for each of your tribes individuals who are wise, discerning, and reputable to be your leaders.”
text.Rotherham: Set forth for you—men wise and discerning, and known to your tribes,—that I may appoint them to be heads over you.
text.KJV: 1:13 Take ye wise men, and vnderstanding, and knowen among your tribes, and I will make them rulers ouer you.
text.NABRE: 1:13 Provide wise, discerning, and reputable persons for each of your tribes, that I may appoint them as your leaders.”

text.NRSV: You answered me, “The plan you have proposed is a good one.”
text.Rotherham: And ye answered me,—and said, Good, is the word which thou hast spoken to be done.
text.KJV: 1:14 And ye answered me, and saide, The thing which thou hast spoken, is good for vs to doe.
text.NABRE: 1:14 You answered me, “What you have proposed is good.”

text.NRSV: So I took the leaders of your tribes, wise and reputable individuals, and installed them as leaders over you, commanders of thousands, commanders of hundreds, commanders of fifties, commanders of tens, and officials, throughout your tribes.
text.Rotherham: So I took heads for your tribes wise men and known, and placed them as heads over you, captains of thousands, and captains of hundreds, and captains of fifties and captains of tens, and overseers for your tribes.
text.KJV: 1:15 So I tooke the chiefe of your tribes, wise men, and knowen, and made them heads ouer you, captaines ouer thousands, and captaines ouer hundreds, and captaines ouer fifties, and captaines ouer tennes, and officers among your tribes.
text.NABRE: 1:15 So I took the leaders of your tribes, wise and reputable, and set them as leaders over you, commanders over thousands, over hundreds, over fifties and over tens, and other tribal officers.

text.NRSV: I charged your judges at that time: “Give the members of your community a fair hearing, and judge rightly between one person and another, whether citizen or resident alien.
text.Rotherham: And I charged your judges, at that time, saying,—Ye are to hear between your brethren and shall judge righteously, between each man and his brother and his sojourner.
text.KJV: 1:16 And I charged your Iudges at that time, saying, Heare the causes betweene your brethren, and iudge righteously betweene euery man and his brother, & the stranger that is with him.
text.NABRE: 1:16 I charged your judges at that time, “Listen to complaints among your relatives, and administer true justice to both parties even if one of them is a resident alien.

text.NRSV: You must not be partial in judging: hear out the small and the great alike; you shall not be intimidated by anyone, for the judgment is God’s. Any case that is too hard for you, bring to me, and I will hear it.”
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not take note of faces in judgment—so the small as the great, shall ye hear, ye shall not shrink from the face of any man, for judgment, unto God, belongeth,—but the thing which is too hard for you, shall ye bring near unto me, and I will hear it.
text.KJV: 1:17 Ye shall not respect persons in iudgement, but you shall heare the small aswell as the great: you shall not bee afraid of the face of man, for the iudgment is Gods: and the cause that is too hard for you, bring it vnto me, and I will heare it.
text.NABRE: 1:17 In rendering judgment, do not consider who a person is; give ear to the lowly and to the great alike, fearing no one, for the judgment is God’s. Any case that is too difficult for you bring to me and I will hear it.”

text.NRSV: So I charged you at that time with all the things that you should do.
text.Rotherham: So I commanded you at that time all the things which ye should do.
text.KJV: 1:18 And I commanded you at that time all the things which ye should doe.
text.NABRE: 1:18 Thus I charged you, at that time, with all the things you were to do.

text.NRSV: Then, just as the Lord our God had ordered us, we set out from Horeb and went through all that great and terrible wilderness that you saw, on the way to the hill country of the Amorites, until we reached Kadesh-barnea.
text.Rotherham: And when we brake up from Horeb, we came through all that great and terrible desert which ye saw, by way of the hill-country of the Amorites, as Yahweh our God commanded us,—and we entered in as far as Kadesh-barnea.
text.KJV: 1:19 ¶ And when wee departed from Horeb, we went through all that great and terrible wildernes, which you saw by the way of the mountaine of the Amorites, as the Lord our God commanded vs: and wee came to Kadesh Barnea.
text.NABRE: 1:19 The Twelve Scouts. Then we set out from Horeb and journeyed through that whole vast and fearful wilderness that you have seen, in the direction of the hill country of the Amorites, as the LORD, our God, had commanded; and we came to Kadesh-barnea.

text.NRSV: I said to you, “You have reached the hill country of the Amorites, which the Lord our God is giving us.
text.Rotherham: Then said I unto you,—Ye are come in as far as the hill-country of the Amorites, which Yahweh our God, is giving unto us.
text.KJV: 1:20 And I said vnto you, Ye are come vnto the mountaine of the Amorites, which the Lord our God doth giue vnto vs.
text.NABRE: 1:20 I said to you, “You have come to the hill country of the Amorites, which the LORD, our God, is giving us.

text.NRSV: See, the Lord your God has given the land to you; go up, take possession, as the Lord, the God of your ancestors, has promised you; do not fear or be dismayed.”
text.Rotherham: See thou—Yahweh thy God hath set before thee the land,—go up—take possession as Yahweh the God of thy fathers hath spoken unto thee, do not fear, neither be dismayed.
text.KJV: 1:21 Behold, the Lord thy God hath set the land before thee: Goe vp, and possesse it, as the Lord God of thy fathers hath said vnto thee: Feare not, neither be discouraged.
text.NABRE: 1:21 See, the LORD, your God, has given this land over to you. Go up and take possession of it, as the LORD, the God of your ancestors, has promised you. Do not fear or be dismayed.”

text.NRSV: All of you came to me and said, “Let us send men ahead of us to explore the land for us and bring back a report to us regarding the route by which we should go up and the cities we will come to.”
text.Rotherham: And ye came near unto me all of you, and said Let us send men before us, that they may search out for us the land,—and bring us back word, the way by which we must go up, and the cities whereinto we must enter,
text.KJV: 1:22 ¶ And ye came neere vnto mee euery one of you, and said, We will send men before vs, and they shall search vs out the land, and bring vs word againe, by what way we must goe vp, and into what cities we shall come.
text.NABRE: 1:22 Then all of you approached me and said, “Let us send men ahead to spy out the land for us and report to us on the road we should follow and the cities we will come upon.”

text.NRSV: The plan seemed good to me, and I selected twelve of you, one from each tribe.
text.Rotherham: And the thing, was good in mine eyes,—so I took from among you twelve men, one man for each tribe.
text.KJV: 1:23 And the saying pleased mee well: and I tooke twelue men of you, one of a tribe.
text.NABRE: 1:23 Agreeing with the proposal, I took twelve men from your number, one from each tribe.

text.NRSV: They set out and went up into the hill country, and when they reached the Valley of Eshcol they spied it out
text.Rotherham: And they turned and went up the hill-country, and entered in as far as the ravine of Eshcol, and explored it.
text.KJV: 1:24 And they turned and went vp into the mountaine, and came vnto the valley of Eshcol, and searched it out.
text.NABRE: 1:24 They set out into the hill country as far as the Wadi Eshcol, and explored it.

text.NRSV: and gathered some of the land’s produce, which they brought down to us. They brought back a report to us, and said, “It is a good land that the Lord our God is giving us.”
text.Rotherham: And they took in their hand of the fruit of the country, and bare it down unto us, and brought us back word, and said, Good is the land, which Yahweh our God, is giving unto us.
text.KJV: 1:25 And they tooke of the fruit of the land in their handes, and brought it downe vnto vs, and brought vs worde againe, and said, It is a good lande which the Lord our God doeth giue vs.
text.NABRE: 1:25 Then, taking along some of the fruit of the land, they brought it down to us and reported, “The land the LORD, our God, is giving us is good.”

text.NRSV: But you were unwilling to go up. You rebelled against the command of the Lord your God;
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless ye were not willing to go up,—but rebelled against the bidding of Yahweh, your God;
text.KJV: 1:26 Notwithstanding, ye would not goe vp, but rebelled against the commandement of the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 1:26 Threats of Revolt. But you refused to go up; you defied the command of the LORD, your God.

text.NRSV: you grumbled in your tents and said, “It is because the Lord hates us that he has brought us out of the land of Egypt, to hand us over to the Amorites to destroy us.
text.Rotherham: and murmured in your tents and said, Because Yahweh hated us, hath he brought us forth out of the land of Egypt,—to deliver us into the hand of the Amorites, to destroy us.
text.KJV: 1:27 And ye murmured in your tents and said, Because the Lord hated vs, he hath brought vs forth out of the land of Egypt, to deliuer vs into the hand of the Amorites, to destroy vs:
text.NABRE: 1:27 You set to murmuring in your tents, “Out of hatred for us the LORD has brought us out of the land of Egypt, to deliver us into the power of the Amorites and destroy us.

text.NRSV: Where are we headed? Our kindred have made our hearts melt by reporting, ‘The people are stronger and taller than we; the cities are large and fortified up to heaven! We actually saw there the offspring of the Anakim!’”
text.Rotherham: Whither, are, we, to go up? Our own brethren, have made our heart melt saying—A people greater and taller than we, cities large and walled-up into the heavens,—yea, moreover sons of Anakim, have we seen there.
text.KJV: 1:28 Whither shall wee goe vp? our brethren haue discouraged our heart, saying, The people is greater and taller then we, the cities are great, and walled vp to heauen, and moreouer we haue seene the sonnes of the Anakims there.
text.NABRE: 1:28 What shall we meet with up there? Our men have made our hearts melt by saying, ‘The people are bigger and taller than we, and their cities are large and fortified to the sky; besides, we saw the Anakim there.’”

text.NRSV: I said to you, “Have no dread or fear of them.
text.Rotherham: Then I said unto you,—Ye must not tremble, nor be afraid of them:
text.KJV: 1:29 Then I said vnto you, Dread not, neither be afraid of them.
text.NABRE: 1:29 But I said to you, “Have no dread or fear of them.

text.NRSV: The Lord your God, who goes before you, is the one who will fight for you, just as he did for you in Egypt before your very eyes,
text.Rotherham: Yahweh your God, who is going before you, he, will fight for you,—according to all that he did with you in Egypt, before your eyes;
text.KJV: 1:30 The Lord your God which goeth before you, he shall fight for you, according to all that hee did for you in Egypt before your eyes:
text.NABRE: 1:30 The LORD, your God, who goes before you, is the one who will fight for you, just as he acted with you before your very eyes in Egypt,

text.NRSV: and in the wilderness, where you saw how the Lord your God carried you, just as one carries a child, all the way that you traveled until you reached this place.
text.Rotherham: also in the desert which thou hast seen, where Yahweh thy God carried thee as a man will carry his son, throughout all the way that ye journeyed, until ye entered as far as, this place.
text.KJV: 1:31 And in the wildernes, where thou hast seene how that the Lord thy God bare thee, as a man doth beare his sonne, in all the way that ye went, vntil ye came into this place.
text.NABRE: 1:31 as well as in the wilderness, where you saw how the LORD, your God, carried you, as one carries his own child, all along your journey until you arrived at this place.”

text.NRSV: But in spite of this, you have no trust in the Lord your God,
text.Rotherham: But in this thing, ye were putting no trust in Yahweh your God;
text.KJV: 1:32 Yet in this thing ye did not beleeue the Lord your God,
text.NABRE: 1:32 Despite this, you would not trust the LORD, your God,

text.NRSV: who goes before you on the way to seek out a place for you to camp, in fire by night, and in the cloud by day, to show you the route you should take.”
text.Rotherham: who was going before you in the way—to search out for you a place, where ye might encamp,—in the fire by night, that ye might get sight of the way wherein ye should go, and in the cloud, by day.
text.KJV: 1:33 Who went in the way before you to search you out a place to pitch your tents in, in fire by night, to shew you by what way ye should goe, and in a cloud by day.
text.NABRE: 1:33 who journeys before you to find you a place to camp—by night in the fire, and by day in the cloud, to show you the way to go.

text.NRSV: When the Lord heard your words, he was wrathful and swore:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh heard the voice of your words,—and was wroth and sware—saying:
text.KJV: 1:34 And the Lord heard the voice of your words, and was wroth, and sware, saying,
text.NABRE: 1:34 When the LORD heard your words, he was angry, and took an oath:

text.NRSV: “Not one of these—not one of this evil generation—shall see the good land that I swore to give to your ancestors,
text.Rotherham: Surely, not one of these men, this wicked generation,—shall see the good land, which I sware to give unto your fathers:
text.KJV: 1:35 Surely there shall not one of these men of this euill generation see that good land, which I sware to giue vnto your fathers:
text.NABRE: 1:35 Not a single one of this evil generation shall look upon the good land I swore to give to your ancestors,

text.NRSV: except Caleb son of Jephunneh. He shall see it, and to him and to his descendants I will give the land on which he set foot, because of his complete fidelity to the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: saving Caleb son of Jephunneh, he, shall see it, and unto him, will I give the land through which he hath trodden, and unto his sons,—because he hath wholly followed Yahweh.
text.KJV: 1:36 Saue Caleb the sonne of Iephunneh, he shall see it, and to him will I giue the land that he hath troden vpon, and to his children, because hee hath wholly followed the Lord.
text.NABRE: 1:36 except Caleb, son of Jephunneh. He shall see it, for to him and to his descendants I will give the land he trod upon, because he has fully followed the LORD.

text.NRSV: Even with me the Lord was angry on your account, saying, “You also shall not enter there.
text.Rotherham: Even with me, did Yahweh show himself angry, for your sakes, saying,—Even thou, shalt not enter therein.
text.KJV: 1:37 Also the Lord was angry with me for your sakes, saying, Thou also shalt not goe in thither.
text.NABRE: 1:37 The LORD was angered against me also on your account, and said, You shall not enter there either,

text.NRSV: Joshua son of Nun, your assistant, shall enter there; encourage him, for he is the one who will secure Israel’s possession of it.
text.Rotherham: Joshua son of Nun, who standeth before thee, he, shall enter in thither, him, strengthen thou, for he, shall cause Israel to inherit it.
text.KJV: 1:38 But Ioshua the sonne of Nun, which standeth before thee, he shall goe in thither. Encourage him: for he shall cause Israel to inherite it.
text.NABRE: 1:38 but Joshua, son of Nun, your attendant, shall enter. Encourage him, for he is the one who is to give Israel its possession.

text.NRSV: And as for your little ones, who you thought would become booty, your children, who today do not yet know right from wrong, they shall enter there; to them I will give it, and they shall take possession of it.
text.Rotherham: And your little ones, of whom ye said they should become, a prey, and your sons who to-day know not good and evil, they, shall enter in thither,—and unto them, will I give it, and they, shall possess it.
text.KJV: 1:39 Moreouer, your litle ones, which ye said should be a pray, and your children, which in that day had no knowledge betweene good and euil, they shall goe in thither; and vnto them will I giue it, and they shall possesse it.
text.NABRE: 1:39 Your little ones, who you said would become plunder, and your children, who as yet do not know good from evil—they shall enter there; to them I will give it, and they shall take possession of it.

text.NRSV: But as for you, journey back into the wilderness, in the direction of the Red Sea.”
text.Rotherham: But as for you, turn ye, for your part,—and set forward towards the desert, by way of the Red Sea.
text.KJV: 1:40 But as for you, turne ye, and take your iourney into the wildernesse, by the way of the Red sea.
text.NABRE: 1:40 But as for yourselves: turn back and proceed into the wilderness on the Red Sea road.

text.NRSV: You answered me, “We have sinned against the Lord! We are ready to go up and fight, just as the Lord our God commanded us.” So all of you strapped on your battle gear, and thought it easy to go up into the hill country.
text.Rotherham: Then responded ye and said unto me—We have sinned against Yahweh, We, ourselves, will go up, and fight, according to all that Yahweh our God hath commanded us. And when ye had girded on every man his weapons of war, ye foolishly went up towards the hill-country.
text.KJV: 1:41 Then ye answered, and said vnto mee, Wee haue sinned against the Lord, we will goe vp and fight, according to all that the Lord our God commanded vs. And when ye had girded on euery man his weapons of warre, yee were ready to goe vp into the hill.
text.NABRE: 1:41 Unsuccessful Invasion. In reply you said to me, “We have sinned against the LORD. We will go up ourselves and fight, just as the LORD, our God, commanded us.” And each of you girded on his weapons, making light of going up into the hill country.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to me, “Say to them, ‘Do not go up and do not fight, for I am not in the midst of you; otherwise you will be defeated by your enemies.’”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto me—Say unto them Ye shall not go up, nor fight, for I am not in your midst,—lest ye should be smitten before your enemies.
text.KJV: 1:42 And the Lord said vnto me, Say vnto them, Goe not vp, neither fight, for I am not among you: least ye be smitten before your enemies.
text.NABRE: 1:42 But the LORD said to me, Warn them: Do not go up and fight—for I will not be in your midst—lest you be beaten down before your enemies.

text.NRSV: Although I told you, you would not listen. You rebelled against the command of the Lord and presumptuously went up into the hill country.
text.Rotherham: So I spake unto you howbeit ye hearkened not,—but rebelled against the bidding of Yahweh, and presumptuously went up the hill-country.
text.KJV: 1:43 So I spake vnto you, and you would not heare, but rebelled against the commandement of the Lord, and went presumptuously vp into the hill.
text.NABRE: 1:43 I gave you this warning but you would not listen. You defied the LORD’s command and arrogantly went off into the hill country.

text.NRSV: The Amorites who lived in that hill country then came out against you and chased you as bees do. They beat you down in Seir as far as Hormah.
text.Rotherham: Then came forth the Amorites who were dwelling in that country, to meet you, and they chased you, as do, bees; and struck you down in Seir, as far as Hormah.
text.KJV: 1:44 And the Amorites which dwelt in that mountaine, came out against you, and chased you, as Bees doe, and destroyed you in Seir, euen vnto Hormah.
text.NABRE: 1:44 Then the Amorites living in that hill country came out against you and put you to flight the way bees do, cutting you down in Seir as far as Hormah.

text.NRSV: When you returned and wept before the Lord, the Lord would neither heed your voice nor pay you any attention.
text.Rotherham: So then ye returned and wept before Yahweh,—but Yahweh hearkened not unto your voice, nor gave ear unto you.
text.KJV: 1:45 And ye returned and wept before the Lord; but the Lord would not hearken to your voyce, nor giue eare vnto you.
text.NABRE: 1:45 On your return you wept before the LORD, but the LORD did not listen to your voice or give ear to you.

text.NRSV: After you had stayed at Kadesh as many days as you did,
text.Rotherham: And ye abode in Kadesh many days,—how many were the days that ye abode [there] !
text.KJV: 1:46 So yee abode in Kadesh many dayes, according vnto the dayes that ye abode there.
text.NABRE: 1:46 That is why you had to stay as long as you did at Kadesh.

text.NRSV: Then we turned back and journeyed into the wilderness, in the direction of the Red Sea, as the Lord had told me, and skirted Mount Seir for many days.
text.Rotherham: Then turned we, and set forward towards the desert by way of the Red Sea, as Yahweh, spake unto me; and we encompassed Mount Seir many days.
text.KJV: 2:1 Then we turned, and tooke our iourney into the wildernesse, by the way of the Red sea, as the Lord spake vnto mee: and wee compassed mount Seir many dayes.
text.NABRE: 2:1 Chapter 2 - Northward Along Edom. Then we turned and proceeded into the wilderness on the Red Sea road, as the LORD had told me, and circled around the highlands of Seir for a long time.

text.NRSV: And the Lord spoke to me, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto me, saying:
text.KJV: 2:2 And the Lord spake vnto me, saying,
text.NABRE: 2:2 Finally the LORD said to me,

text.NRSV: You have been skirting this hill country long enough; turn north,
text.Rotherham: Long enough, have ye encompassed this mountain,—turn yourselves north, wards.
text.KJV: 2:3 Yee haue compassed this mountaine long enough: turne you Northward.
text.NABRE: 2:3 You have wandered round these highlands long enough; turn and go north.

text.NRSV: and command the people: You are about to cross the territory of your kindred, the descendants of Esau, who live in Seir. Though they will be afraid of you, be very careful
text.Rotherham: And, the people, command thou saying, Ye are about to pass through the boundary of your brethren the sons of Esau, who are dwelling in Seir,—and should they be afraid of you, take ye good heed to yourselves:
text.KJV: 2:4 And commaund thou the people, saying, Ye are to passe through the coast of your brethren the children of Esau, which dwell in Seir, and they shall be afraid of you: take ye good heed vnto your selues therefore.
text.NABRE: 2:4 Command the people: You are now about to pass through the territory of your relatives, the descendants of Esau, who live in Seir. Though they are afraid of you, be very careful

text.NRSV: not to engage in battle with them; for I will not give you any of their land, no, not so much as a foot’s length; since I have given Mount Seir to Esau as a possession.
text.Rotherham: do not engage in strife with them, for I will not give you of their land, so much as place for the tread of a foot-sole,—for as a possession to Esau, have I given Mount Seir.
text.KJV: 2:5 Meddle not with them, for I will not giue you of their land, no not so much as a foote breadth, because I haue giuen mount Seir vnto Esau for a possession.
text.NABRE: 2:5 not to come in conflict with them, for I will not give you so much as a foot of their land, since I have already given Esau possession of the highlands of Seir.

text.NRSV: You shall purchase food from them for money, so that you may eat; and you shall also buy water from them for money, so that you may drink.
text.Rotherham: Food, shall ye buy of them with silver so shall ye eat,—yea even water, shall ye buy of them with silver so shall ye drink.
text.KJV: 2:6 Ye shall buy meat of them for money, that ye may eat, and yee shall also buy water of them for money, that yee may drinke.
text.NABRE: 2:6 You shall purchase from them with money the food you eat; even the water you drink you shall buy from them with money.

text.NRSV: For the Lord your God has blessed you in all your undertakings; he knows your going through this great wilderness. These forty years the Lord your God has been with you; you have lacked nothing.’
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh thy God, hath blessed thee in all the work of thy hand, he hath considered thy traversing of this great desert,—these forty years, hath Yahweh thy God been with thee, thou hast lacked, nothing.
text.KJV: 2:7 For the Lord thy God hath blessed thee, in all the workes of thy hand: hee knoweth thy walking thorow this great wildernesse: these fourtie yeres the Lord thy God hath bene with thee, thou hast lacked nothing.
text.NABRE: 2:7 Surely, the LORD, your God, has blessed you in all your undertakings; he has been concerned about your journey through this vast wilderness. It is now forty years that the LORD, your God, has been with you, and you have lacked nothing.

text.NRSV: So we went past our kindred, the descendants of Esau who live in Seir, past the Arabah, past Ezion-geber. And we turned and went in the direction of the wilderness of Moab.
text.Rotherham: So we passed on, a way from our brethren the sons of Esau who dwell in Seir, from the way of the waste plain, from Elath, and from Ezion-geber,—and we turned and passed on by way of the desert of Moab.
text.KJV: 2:8 And when we passed by from our brethren the children of Esau, which dwelt in Seir, thorow the way of the plaine from Elath, and from Ezion-Gaber, wee turned and passed by the way of the wildernesse of Moab.
text.NABRE: 2:8 So we passed by our relatives, the descendants of Esau who live in Seir, leaving behind us the Arabah route, Elath, and Ezion-geber. Along Moab. Then we turned and passed on toward the wilderness of Moab.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said to me, ‘Do not harass Moab or engage them in battle, for I will not give you any of their land as a possession, since I have given Ar to the descendants of Lot for a possession.’
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto me—Do not lay siege to Moab, nor engage in strife with them in battle,—for I will not give unto thee of this land as a possession, for unto the sons of Lot, have I given Ar, as a possession.
text.KJV: 2:9 And the Lord said vnto mee, Distresse not the Moabites, neither contend with them in battell: for I wil not giue thee of their land for a possession, because I haue giuen Ar vnto the children of Lot for a possession.
text.NABRE: 2:9 And the LORD said to me, Do not show hostility to the Moabites or engage them in battle, for I will not give you possession of any of their land, since I have given Ar to the descendants of Lot as their possession.

text.NRSV: (The Emim, a large and numerous people, as tall as the Anakim, had formerly inhabited it.
text.Rotherham: the Emim, formerly dwelt therein,—a people great and many and tall, like the Anakim.
text.KJV: 2:10 The Emims dwelt therein in times past, a people great, and many, and tall, as the Anakims:
text.NABRE: 2:10 (Formerly the Emim lived there, a people great and numerous and as tall as the Anakim;

text.NRSV: Like the Anakim, they were reckoned as Rephaim, but the Moabites called them Emim.
text.Rotherham: Giants, used, they also, to be accounted like the Anakim,—but the Moabites, called them Emim.
text.KJV: 2:11 Which also were accounted giants, as the Anakims, but the Moabites call them Emims.
text.NABRE: 2:11 like the Anakim they are considered Rephaim, though the Moabites call them Emim.

text.NRSV: Formerly the Horites lived in Seir, but the descendants of Esau dispossessed them and destroyed them and settled in their place, as Israel did in the land that the Lord gave them as their possession.)
text.Rotherham: And in Seir, dwelt the Horim aforetime, but, the sons of Esau, dispossessed them, and destroyed them from before them, and dwelt in their stead,—as did Israel unto the land of his own possession, which Yahweh had given unto them.
text.KJV: 2:12 The Horims also dwelt in Seir beforetime, but the children of Esau succeeded them when they had destroyed them from before them, & dwelt in their stead, as Israel did vnto the land of his possession, which the Lord gaue vnto them.
text.NABRE: 2:12 In Seir, however, the former inhabitants were the Horites; the descendants of Esau dispossessed them, clearing them out of the way and dwelling in their place, just as Israel has done in the land of its possession which the LORD gave it.)

text.NRSV: ‘Now rise up, set out on your journey, and cross the Wadi Zered.’ So we crossed the Wadi Zered.
text.Rotherham: Now, arise, and pass ye over the ravine of Zered. So we passed over the ravine of Zered.
text.KJV: 2:13 Now rise vp, said I, and get you ouer the brooke Zered: and we went ouer the brooke Zered.
text.NABRE: 2:13 Now get ready to cross the Wadi Zered. So we crossed the Wadi Zered.

text.NRSV: The time from our leaving Kadesh-barnea until we crossed the Wadi Zered was thirty-eight years, until the whole generation that had done evil in the sight of the Lord had perished from the camp, as the Lord had sworn to them.
text.Rotherham: Now, the days in which we journeyed from Kadesh-barnea, as far as where we crossed the ravine of Zered, were thirty-eight years,—until all the generation of the men of war were consumed out of the midst of the camp, as Yahweh had sworn unto them.
text.KJV: 2:14 And the space in which we came from Kadesh Barnea, vntill we were come ouer the brooke Zered, was thirtie and eight yeeres; vntill all the generation of the men of warre were wasted out from among the hoste, as the Lord sware vnto them.
text.NABRE: 2:14 Now thirty-eight years had elapsed between our departure from Kadesh-barnea and the crossing of the Wadi Zered; in the meantime the whole generation of soldiers had perished from the camp, as the LORD had sworn they should.

text.NRSV: Indeed, the hand of the Lord was against them, to root them out from the camp, until all had perished.
text.Rotherham: Yea, even the hand of Yahweh was against them, to destroy them out of the midst of the camp,—until they were consumed.
text.KJV: 2:15 For indeed the hand of the Lord was against them, to destroy them from among the hoste, vntill they were consumed.
text.NABRE: 2:15 Indeed the LORD’s own hand was against them, to rout them from the camp completely.

text.NRSV: And as soon as all the warriors had perished from the people,
text.Rotherham: So it came to pass when all the men of war were consumed by dying, out of the midst of the people,
text.KJV: 2:16 ¶ So it came to passe, when all the men of warre were consumed and dead from among the people,
text.NABRE: 2:16 Along Ammon. When at length death had put an end to all the soldiers among the people,

text.NRSV: the Lord spoke to me, saying,
text.Rotherham: then spake Yahweh unto me, saying:
text.KJV: 2:17 That the Lord spake vnto me, saying,
text.NABRE: 2:17 the LORD said to me,

text.NRSV: ‘Today you are going to cross the boundary of Moab at Ar.
text.Rotherham: Thou, art passing, to-day, the boundary of Moab even Ar;
text.KJV: 2:18 Thou art to passe ouer thorow Ar, the coast of Moab, this day.
text.NABRE: 2:18 You are now about to leave Ar and the territory of Moab behind.

text.NRSV: When you approach the frontier of the Ammonites, do not harass them or engage them in battle, for I will not give you any of the land of the Ammonites as a possession, since I have given it to the descendants of Lot for a possession.’
text.Rotherham: so wilt thou draw near over against the sons of Ammon, do not thou besiege them neither engage in strife with them,—for I will not give of the land of the sons of Ammon unto thee as a possession, for unto the sons of Lot, have I given it as a possession.
text.KJV: 2:19 And when thou commest nigh ouer against the children of Ammon, distresse them not, nor meddle with them: for I will not giue thee of the lande of the children of Ammon any possession, because I haue giuen it vnto the children of Lot for a possession:
text.NABRE: 2:19 As you come opposite the Ammonites, do not show hostility or come in conflict with them, for I will not give you possession of any land of the Ammonites, since I have given it to the descendants of Lot as their possession.

text.NRSV: (That also was reckoned a land of Rephaim. Rephaim formerly lived there—but the Ammonites call them Zamzummim,
text.Rotherham: A land of giants, used, that also, to be accounted,—giants, dwelt therein aforetime, but the Ammonites, called them Zamzummim:
text.KJV: 2:20 (That also was accounted a land of Giants: giants dwelt therein in old time, and the Ammonites call them Zamzummims.
text.NABRE: 2:20 (This also is considered a country of the Rephaim; formerly the Rephaim dwelt there. The Ammonites call them Zamzummim,

text.NRSV: a people as numerous and tall as the Anakim; but the Lord destroyed them so that they could not continue to live there; so they lived in their place instead of them, as they have done to this day.
text.Rotherham: a people great and many and tall, like the Anakim,—but Yahweh destroyed them from before them, and they took their possessions, and dwelt in their stead:
text.KJV: 2:21 A people great, and many, and tall, as the Anakims: but the Lord destroyed them before them, and they succeeded them & dwelt in their stead:)
text.NABRE: 2:21 a people great and numerous and as tall as the Anakim. But these, too, the LORD cleared out of the way for the Ammonites, so that they dispossessed them and dwelt in their place.

text.NRSV: He did the same for the descendants of Esau who live in Seir, when he destroyed the Horites before them; they dispossessed them and settled in their place until today.
text.Rotherham: as he did for the sons of Esau, who are dwelling in Seir,—when he destroyed the Horim from before them, and they took their possessions and have dwelt in their stead, even unto this day.
text.KJV: 2:22 As he did to the children of Esau which dwelt in Seir, when he destroyed the Horims from before them, and they succeeded them, and dwelt in their stead euen vnto this day.
text.NABRE: 2:22 He did the same for the descendants of Esau, who live in Seir, by clearing the Horites out of their way, so that they dispossessed them and dwelt in their place down to the present.

text.NRSV: As for the Avvim, who lived in villages as far as Gaza, the Caphtorim, who came from Caphtor, destroyed them and settled in their place.)
text.Rotherham: The Avvim also, who dwelt in settlements as far as Gaza, Caphtorim, who were coming forth out of Caphtor, destroyed them, and dwelt in their stead.
text.KJV: 2:23 And the Auims which dwelt in Hazerim, euen vnto Azzah, the Caphtorims which came foorth out of Caphtor, destroyed them, and dwelt in their stead.
text.NABRE: 2:23 As for the Avvim, who once lived in villages in the vicinity of Gaza, the Caphtorim, migrating from Caphtor, cleared them away and dwelt in their place.)

text.NRSV: ‘Rise up, set out on your journey, and cross the Wadi Arnon. See, I have given into your hand King Sihon the Amorite of Heshbon, and his land. Begin to take possession of it, and engage him in battle.
text.Rotherham: Arise ye, set forward and cross over the ravine of Arnon, see! I have given into thy hand—Sihon king of Heshbon, the Amorite and his land, begin—take possession,—and engage in strife with him in battle.
text.KJV: 2:24 ¶ Rise ye vp, take your iourney, and passe ouer the riuer Arnon: Behold, I haue giuen into thy hand Sihon the Amorite king of Heshbon, and his land: begin to possesse it, and contend with him in battell.
text.NABRE: 2:24 Defeat of Sihon. Advance now across the Wadi Arnon. I now deliver into your power Sihon, the Amorite king of Heshbon, and his land. Begin to take possession; engage him in battle.

text.NRSV: Today I will begin to put the dread and fear of you on the nations everywhere under heaven, who on hearing the news of you will tremble and be in anguish because of you.’
text.Rotherham: This day, will I begin to extend the dread of thee and the fear of thee over the face of the peoples under all the heavens,—who will hear the report of thee, then will they quake and writhe in pain because of thee.
text.KJV: 2:25 This day will I begin to put the dread of thee, and the feare of thee vpon the nations, that are vnder the whole heauen, who shall heare report of thee, and shall tremble, and be in anguish because of thee.
text.NABRE: 2:25 This day I will begin to put a fear and dread of you into the peoples everywhere under heaven, so that at the mention of your name they will quake and tremble before you.

text.NRSV: So I sent messengers from the wilderness of Kedemoth to King Sihon of Heshbon with an offer of peace, saying,
text.Rotherham: So I sent messengers out of the desert of Kademoth, unto Sihon, king of Heshbon,—with words of peace saying:
text.KJV: 2:26 ¶ And I sent messengers out of the wildernesse of Kedemoth, vnto Sihon king of Heshbon, with wordes of peace, saying,
text.NABRE: 2:26 So I sent messengers from the wilderness of Kedemoth to Sihon, king of Heshbon, with this offer of peace:

text.NRSV: ‘Let me pass through your land by the road. I will go only by the road. I will not turn aside to the right or to the left;
text.Rotherham: I would pass along through thy land, by the road, by the road, will I go,—I will not turn aside, to the right hand or to the left:
text.KJV: 2:27 Let me passe through thy land: I will goe along by the high way, I will neither turne vnto the right hand, nor to the left.
text.NABRE: 2:27 “Let me pass through your country. I will travel only on the road. I will not turn aside either to the right or to the left.

text.NRSV: you shall sell me food for money, so that I may eat, and give me water for money, so that I may drink. Only allow me to pass through on foot,
text.Rotherham: Food for silver, shalt thou sell me so will I eat, and water for silver, shalt thou let me have, so will I drink,—only I would pass through on my feet;—
text.KJV: 2:28 Thou shalt sell me meat for money, that I may eate, and giue me water for money that I may drinke: Only I will passe through on my feet:
text.NABRE: 2:28 The food I eat you will sell me for money, and the water I drink, you will give me for money. Only let me march through,

text.NRSV: just as the descendants of Esau who live in Seir did for me, and the Moabites who live in Ar, until I cross the Jordan into the land that the Lord our God is giving us.’
text.Rotherham: as the sons of Esau, who are dwelling in Seir, did to me, and the Moabites who are dwelling in Ar,—until that I pass over the Jordan, into the land which, Yahweh our God, is giving unto us.
text.KJV: 2:29 As the children of Esau which dwell in Seir, and the Moabites which dwell in Ar, did vnto me, vntill I shall passe ouer Iordan, into the land which the Lord our God giueth vs.
text.NABRE: 2:29 as the descendants of Esau who dwell in Seir and the Moabites who dwell in Ar have done, until I cross the Jordan into the land the LORD, our God, is about to give us.”

text.NRSV: But King Sihon of Heshbon was not willing to let us pass by him, for the Lord your God hardened his spirit and made his heart defiant, in order to give him into your hand, as he has now done.
text.Rotherham: But Sihon king of Heshbon, was not willing, to let us pass along through it,—for Yahweh thy God had suffered him to make his spirit harsh and his heart bold, that he might give him into thy hand—(as [appeareth] this day).
text.KJV: 2:30 But Sihon King of Heshbon would not let vs passe by him: for the Lord thy God hardened his spirit, and made his heart obstinate, that hee might deliuer him into thy hand, as appeareth this day.
text.NABRE: 2:30 But Sihon, king of Heshbon, refused to let us pass through his land, because the LORD, your God, made him stubborn in mind and obstinate in heart that he might deliver him into your power, as indeed he has now done.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to me, ‘See, I have begun to give Sihon and his land over to you; begin now to take possession of his land.’
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto me, See! I have begun to deliver up before thee Sihon and his land,—begin take possession, that thou mayest make a possession of his land.
text.KJV: 2:31 And the Lord said vnto mee, Behold, I haue begun to giue Sihon and his land before thee: begin to possesse, that thou mayest inherit his land.
text.NABRE: 2:31 Then the LORD said to me, Now that I have already begun to give over to you Sihon and his land, begin to take possession.

text.NRSV: Then Sihon came out against us, he and all his people, to battle at Jahaz.
text.Rotherham: So then Sihon came out to meet us he, and all his people, to give battle at Jahaz.
text.KJV: 2:32 Then Sihon came out against vs, he & all his people to fight at Iahaz.
text.NABRE: 2:32 So Sihon and all his people advanced against us to join battle at Jahaz;

text.NRSV: The Lord our God gave him over to us; and we struck him down, along with his sons and his whole people.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh our God delivered him up before us,—and we smote him and his sons, and all his people
text.KJV: 2:33 And the Lord our God deliuered him before vs, and wee smote him, and his sonnes, and all his people.
text.NABRE: 2:33 but since the LORD, our God, had given him over to us, we defeated him and his sons and all his people.

text.NRSV: At that time we captured all his towns, and in each town we utterly destroyed men, women, and children. We left no survivors.
text.Rotherham: And we captured all his cities, at that time, and devoted to destruction every city of males, with the women and the little ones,—we left not remaining a survivor:
text.KJV: 2:34 And we tooke all his cities at that time, and vtterly destroyed the men, and the women, and the litle ones of euery citie, we left none to remaine:
text.NABRE: 2:34 At that time we captured all his cities and put every city under the ban, men, women and children; we left no survivor.

text.NRSV: We took only the livestock as spoil for ourselves, with the plunder of the towns that we captured.
text.Rotherham: only the cattle, made we our prey,—and the spoil of the cities which we captured.
text.KJV: 2:35 Onely the cattell wee tooke for a pray vnto our selues, and the spoyle of the cities, which we tooke:
text.NABRE: 2:35 Our only plunder was the livestock and the spoils of the captured cities.

text.NRSV: From Aroer, which is on the edge of the Wadi Arnon (and from the town in the wadi itself), as far as Gilead, there was not a town too high for us; the Lord our God gave us all of them.
text.Rotherham: From Aroer, which is on the edge of the ravine of Arnon and the city that is in the ravine even as far as Gilead, there was not a fortress that proved too high for us,—the whole, did Yahweh our God deliver up before us.
text.KJV: 2:36 From Aroer, which is by the brinke of the riuer of Arnon, and from the citie that is by the riuer euen vnto Gilead, there was not one citie too strong for vs: the Lord our God deliuered all vnto vs.
text.NABRE: 2:36 From Aroer on the edge of the Wadi Arnon and from the town in the wadi itself, as far as Gilead, no city was too well fortified for us. All of them the LORD, our God, gave over to us.

text.NRSV: You did not encroach, however, on the land of the Ammonites, avoiding the whole upper region of the Wadi Jabbok as well as the towns of the hill country, just as the Lord our God had charged.
text.Rotherham: Only unto the land of the sons of Ammon, didst thou not come near,—all the side of the Jabbok ravine, nor the cities of the hill country, nor any which Yahweh our God had forbidden to us.
text.KJV: 2:37 Onely vnto the land of the children of Ammon thou camest not, nor vnto any place of the riuer Iabbok, nor vnto the cities in the mountaines, nor vnto whatsoeuer the Lord our God forbade vs.
text.NABRE: 2:37 However, just as the LORD, our God, commanded us, you did not encroach upon any of the Ammonite land, neither the region bordering on the Wadi Jabbok, nor the cities of the highlands.

text.NRSV: When we headed up the road to Bashan, King Og of Bashan came out against us, he and all his people, for battle at Edrei.
text.Rotherham: Then turned we and went up, the way of Bashan; and Og king of Bashan, came out to meet us, he, and all his people, to give battle at Edrei.
text.KJV: 3:1 Then we turned, and went vp the way to Bashan: and Og the King of Bashan came out against vs, hee, and all his people to battell at Edrei.
text.NABRE: 3:1 Chapter 3 - Defeat of Og. Then we turned and proceeded up the road to Bashan. But Og, king of Bashan, came out against us with all his people to give battle at Edrei.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to me, “Do not fear him, for I have handed him over to you, along with his people and his land. Do to him as you did to King Sihon of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto me: Do not fear him, for into thy hand, have I delivered him and all his people and his land,—so then thou shalt do unto him, as thou didst unto Sihon king of the Amorites who was dwelling in Heshbon.
text.KJV: 3:2 And the Lord said vnto mee, Feare him not: for I will deliuer him, and all his people, and his land into thy hand, and thou shalt doe vnto him as thou didst vnto Sihon king of the Amorites, which dwelt at Heshbon.
text.NABRE: 3:2 The LORD said to me, Do not be afraid of him, for I have delivered him into your power with all his people and his land. Do to him as you did to Sihon, king of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon.

text.NRSV: So the Lord our God also handed over to us King Og of Bashan and all his people. We struck him down until not a single survivor was left.
text.Rotherham: Then did Yahweh our God deliver into our hand, Og also, king of Bashan and all his people,—so we smote him until there was not left remaining to him a survivor.
text.KJV: 3:3 So the Lord our God deliuered into our hands Og also the King of Bashan, and all his people: and wee smote him vntill none was left to him remayning.
text.NABRE: 3:3 And thus the LORD, our God, delivered into our power also Og, king of Bashan, with all his people. We defeated him so completely that we left him no survivor.

text.NRSV: At that time we captured all his towns; there was no citadel that we did not take from them—sixty towns, the whole region of Argob, the kingdom of Og in Bashan.
text.Rotherham: And we captured all his cities, at that time, there was no fortress which we took not from them,—sixty cities all the region of Argob, the kingdom of Og in Bashan.
text.KJV: 3:4 And we tooke all his cities at that time, there was not a citie which wee tooke not from them; threescore cities, all the region of Argob, the kingdome of Og in Bashan.
text.NABRE: 3:4 At that time we captured all his cities; there was no town we did not take: sixty cities in all, the whole region of Argob, the kingdom of Og in Bashan—

text.NRSV: All these were fortress towns with high walls, double gates, and bars, besides a great many villages.
text.Rotherham: All these, were fortified cities each with a high wall, folding gates and a bar,—besides country towns exceeding many.
text.KJV: 3:5 All these cities were fenced with high walles, gates and barres, beside vnwalled townes a great many.
text.NABRE: 3:5 all these cities were fortified with high walls and gates and bars—besides a great number of unwalled towns.

text.NRSV: And we utterly destroyed them, as we had done to King Sihon of Heshbon, in each city utterly destroying men, women, and children.
text.Rotherham: And we devoted them to destruction, doing unto them as we did unto Sihon king of Heshbon,—devoting to destruction every city of males, the women, and the little ones.
text.KJV: 3:6 And we vtterly destroyed them, as we did vnto Sihon King of Heshbon, vtterly destroying the men, women, and children of euery citie.
text.NABRE: 3:6 As we had done to Sihon, king of Heshbon, so also here we put all the towns under the ban, men, women and children;

text.NRSV: But all the livestock and the plunder of the towns we kept as spoil for ourselves.
text.Rotherham: But all the cattle, and the spoil of the cities, made we our prey.
text.KJV: 3:7 But all the cattell, and the spoile of the cities, we tooke for a pray to our selues.
text.NABRE: 3:7 but all the livestock and the spoils of each city we took as plunder for ourselves.

text.NRSV: So at that time we took from the two kings of the Amorites the land beyond the Jordan, from the Wadi Arnon to Mount Hermon
text.Rotherham: Thus did we at that time take the land out of the hand of the two kings of the Amorites (which was over the Jordan),—from the ravine of Arnon as far as Mount Hermon.
text.KJV: 3:8 And we tooke at that time out of the hand of the two Kings of the Amorites, the land that was on this side Iordan, from the riuer of Arnon, vnto mount Hermon:
text.NABRE: 3:8 And so at that time we took from the two kings of the Amorites beyond the Jordan the territory from the Wadi Arnon to Mount Hermon

text.NRSV: (the Sidonians call Hermon Sirion, while the Amorites call it Senir),
text.Rotherham: Sidonians, call Hermon Sirion,—but, the Amorites, call it Senir.
text.KJV: 3:9 (Which Hermon the Sidonians call Syrion: and the Amorites call it Shenir.)
text.NABRE: 3:9 (the Sidonians call Hermon Sirion and the Amorites call it Senir),

text.NRSV: all the towns of the tableland, the whole of Gilead, and all of Bashan, as far as Salecah and Edrei, towns of Og’s kingdom in Bashan.
text.Rotherham: All the cities of the table-land, and all Gilead, and all Bashan,—unto Salecah, and Edrei,—cities of the kingdom of Og, in Bashan.
text.KJV: 3:10 All the cities of the plaine, and all Gilead, and all Bashan, vnto Salchah, and Edrei, cities of the kingdome of Og in Bashan.
text.NABRE: 3:10 all the towns of the plateau, all of Gilead, and all of Bashan as far as Salecah and Edrei, towns of the kingdom of Og in Bashan.

text.NRSV: (Now only King Og of Bashan was left of the remnant of the Rephaim. In fact his bed, an iron bed, can still be seen in Rabbah of the Ammonites. By the common cubit it is nine cubits long and four cubits wide.)
text.Rotherham: For, only Og, king of Bashan was left remaining of the remnant of the giants, lo! his bedstead, was a bedstead of iron, is not, the same, in Rabbath of the sons of Ammon? nine cubits, the length thereof and four cubits, the breadth thereof, by the fore-arm of a man.
text.KJV: 3:11 For onely Og King of Bashan remained of the remnant of giants; behold, his bedsted was a bedsted of yron: is it not in Rabbath of the children of Ammon? Nine cubites was the length thereof, and foure cubites the breadth of it, after the cubite of a man.
text.NABRE: 3:11 (Og, king of Bashan, was the last remaining survivor of the Rephaim. He had a bed of iron, nine regular cubits long and four wide, which is still preserved in Rabbah of the Ammonites.)

text.NRSV: As for the land that we took possession of at that time, I gave to the Reubenites and Gadites the territory north of Aroer, that is on the edge of the Wadi Arnon, as well as half the hill country of Gilead with its towns,
text.Rotherham: And this land, took we in possession at that time,—from Aroer which is by the ravine of Arnon, and half the hill-country of Gilead and the cities thereof, gave I unto the Reubenites and unto the Gadites;
text.KJV: 3:12 And this land which we possessed at that time, from Aroer which is by the riuer Arnon, and halfe mount Gilead, and the cities thereof, gaue I vnto the Reubenites, and to the Gadites.
text.NABRE: 3:12 Allotment of the Conquered Lands. As for the land we took possession of at that time, I gave Reuben and Gad the territory from Aroer, on the edge of the Wadi Arnon, halfway up into the highlands of Gilead, with its cities.

text.NRSV: and I gave to the half-tribe of Manasseh the rest of Gilead and all of Bashan, Og’s kingdom. (The whole region of Argob: all that portion of Bashan used to be called a land of Rephaim;
text.Rotherham: but the remainder of Gilead, and all Bashan the kingdom of Og, gave I unto the half tribe of Manasseh,—all the region of the Argob with all Bashan. The same, is called, A land of giants.
text.KJV: 3:13 And the rest of Gilead, and all Bashan, being the kingdome of Og, gaue I vnto the halfe tribe of Manasseh: All the region of Argob with all Bashan, which was called the land of Giants.
text.NABRE: 3:13 The rest of Gilead and all of Bashan, the kingdom of Og, I gave to the half-tribe of Manasseh. (The whole Argob region, all that part of Bashan, was once called a land of the Rephaim.

text.NRSV: Jair the Manassite acquired the whole region of Argob as far as the border of the Geshurites and the Maacathites, and he named them—that is, Bashan—after himself, Havvoth-jair, as it is to this day.)
text.Rotherham: Jair, son of Manasseh, took all the region of Argob, as far as the boundary of the Geshurites, and the Maachathites,—and called them after his own name The Bashan of Havvoth-jair unto this day.
text.KJV: 3:14 Iair the sonne of Manasseh tooke all the countrey of Argob, vnto the coastes of Geshuri, and Maachathi; and called them after his owne name, Bashan Hauoth Iair, vnto this day.
text.NABRE: 3:14 Jair, a Manassite, took all the region of Argob as far as the border of the Geshurites and Maacathites, and named them—Bashan, that is—after himself, Havvoth-jair, the name it bears today.)

text.NRSV: To Machir I gave Gilead.
text.Rotherham: And, unto Machir, gave I Gilead;
text.KJV: 3:15 And I gaue Gilead vnto Machir.
text.NABRE: 3:15 To Machir I gave Gilead,

text.NRSV: And to the Reubenites and the Gadites I gave the territory from Gilead as far as the Wadi Arnon, with the middle of the wadi as a boundary, and up to the Jabbok, the wadi being boundary of the Ammonites;
text.Rotherham: And unto the Reubenites and unto the Gadites, gave I—from Gilead even as far as the ravine of Arnon, the middle of the ravine and boundary,—even as far as the Jabbok ravine, the boundary of the sons of Ammon;
text.KJV: 3:16 And vnto the Reubenites, and vnto the Gadites, I gaue from Gilead, euen vnto the riuer Arnon, halfe the valley, and the border, euen vnto the riuer Iabbok, which is the border of the children of Ammon:
text.NABRE: 3:16 and to Reuben and Gad the territory from Gilead to the Wadi Arnon—the middle of the wadi being its boundary—and to the Wadi Jabbok, which is the border of the Ammonites,

text.NRSV: the Arabah also, with the Jordan and its banks, from Chinnereth down to the sea of the Arabah, the Dead Sea, with the lower slopes of Pisgah on the east.
text.Rotherham: the Waste Plain also and the Jordan and boundary,—from Chinnereth, even as far as the sea of the Waste Plain the Salt Sea, under the slopes of Pisgah, on the east.
text.KJV: 3:17 The plaine also, and Iordan, and the coast thereof, from Chinnereth, euen vnto the sea of the plaine, euen the salt sea, vnder Ashdoth Pisgah Eastward.
text.NABRE: 3:17 as well as the Arabah with the Jordan and its banks from Chinnereth to the Salt Sea of the Arabah, under the slopes of Pisgah on the east.

text.NRSV: At that time, I charged you as follows: “Although the Lord your God has given you this land to occupy, all your troops shall cross over armed as the vanguard of your Israelite kin.
text.Rotherham: So I commanded you, at that time saying,—Yahweh your God hath given unto you this land to possess it, armed, shall ye pass over before your brethren the sons of Israel all the sons of valour,
text.KJV: 3:18 ¶ And I commanded you at that time, saying, The Lord your God hath giuen you this land to possesse it: ye shall passe ouer armed before your brethren the children of Israel, all that are meet for the warre.
text.NABRE: 3:18 At that time I charged you: The LORD, your God, has given you this land as your possession. But all your troops equipped for battle must cross over in the vanguard of your fellow Israelites.

text.NRSV: Only your wives, your children, and your livestock—I know that you have much livestock—shall stay behind in the towns that I have given to you.
text.Rotherham: Only, your wives and your little ones and your cattle—I know that ye have much cattle—shall abide in your cities which I have given unto you;
text.KJV: 3:19 But your wiues, and your little ones, and your cattell (for I know that ye haue much cattel) shall abide in your cities, which I haue giuen you:
text.NABRE: 3:19 But your wives and children, as well as your livestock, of which I know you have a large number, shall remain behind in the towns I have given you,

text.NRSV: When the Lord gives rest to your kindred, as to you, and they too have occupied the land that the Lord your God is giving them beyond the Jordan, then each of you may return to the property that I have given to you.”
text.Rotherham: until that Yahweh shall give rest unto your brethren, as well as you, so shall, they too, possess the land which, Yahweh your God, is giving unto them, over the Jordan,—then shall ye return every man unto his possession, which I have given unto you.
text.KJV: 3:20 Untill the Lord haue giuen rest vnto your brethren, as well as vnto you, and vntill they also possesse the land which the Lord your God hath giuen them beyond Iordan: and then shall ye returne euery man vnto his possession, which I haue giuen you.
text.NABRE: 3:20 until the LORD has settled your relatives as well, and they too possess the land which the LORD, your God, will give them on the other side of the Jordan. Then you may all return to the possessions I have given you.

text.NRSV: And I charged Joshua as well at that time, saying: “Your own eyes have seen everything that the Lord your God has done to these two kings; so the Lord will do to all the kingdoms into which you are about to cross.
text.Rotherham: Joshua also, commanded I, at that time saying,—Thine are the eyes, that have seen all that Yahweh your God hath done unto these two kings, thus, will Yahweh do unto all the kingdoms whereunto thou, art passing over:
text.KJV: 3:21 ¶ And I commanded Ioshua at that time, saying, Thine eyes haue seene all that the Lord your God hath done vnto these two Kings: so shal the Lord doe vnto all the kingdomes whither thou passest.
text.NABRE: 3:21 And I charged Joshua as well, “Your own eyes have seen all that the LORD, your God, has done to both these kings; so, too, will the LORD do to all the kingdoms into which you will cross over.

text.NRSV: Do not fear them, for it is the Lord your God who fights for you.”
text.Rotherham: ye shall not fear them,—for, Yahweh your God, he, it is that is fighting for you.
text.KJV: 3:22 Ye shall not feare them: for the Lord your God, he shal fight for you.
text.NABRE: 3:22 Do not fear them, for it is the LORD, your God, who will fight for you.”

text.NRSV: At that time, too, I entreated the Lord, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then sought I Yahweh at that time, for a favour saying:
text.KJV: 3:23 And I besought the Lord at that time, saying,
text.NABRE: 3:23 Moses Excluded from the Promised Land. It was then that I entreated the LORD,

text.NRSV: “O Lord God, you have only begun to show your servant your greatness and your might; what god in heaven or on earth can perform deeds and mighty acts like yours!
text.Rotherham: My Lord Yahweh, thou thyself, hast begun to shew thy servant thy greatness, and thy firm hand,—as to which, what GOD is there, in the heavens or in the earth, that can do according to thy doings, and according to thy mighty deeds?
text.KJV: 3:24 O Lord God, thou hast begun to shew thy seruant thy greatnesse, and thy mighty hand: for what God is there in heauen, or in earth, that can do according to thy workes, and according to thy might?
text.NABRE: 3:24 “Lord GOD, you have begun to show to your servant your greatness and your mighty hand. What god in heaven or on earth can perform deeds and powerful acts like yours?

text.NRSV: Let me cross over to see the good land beyond the Jordan, that good hill country and the Lebanon.”
text.Rotherham: Let me pass over, I pray thee that I may see the good land that is over the Jordan,—this good mountain, and the Lebanon.
text.KJV: 3:25 I pray thee let me goe ouer, and see the good land that is beyond Iordan, that goodly mountaine and Lebanon.
text.NABRE: 3:25 Ah, let me cross over and see the good land beyond the Jordan, that fine hill country, and the Lebanon!”

text.NRSV: But the Lord was angry with me on your account and would not heed me. The Lord said to me, “Enough from you! Never speak to me of this matter again!
text.Rotherham: But Yahweh had been provoked with me for your sakes, and hearkened not unto me,—and Yahweh said unto me, Enough for thee! do not add a word unto me further in this matter.
text.KJV: 3:26 But the Lord was wroth with me for your sakes, and would not heare mee: and the Lord said vnto me, Let it suffice thee, speake no more vnto me of this matter.
text.NABRE: 3:26 But the LORD was angry with me on your account and would not hear me. The LORD said to me, Enough! Speak to me no more of this.

text.NRSV: Go up to the top of Pisgah and look around you to the west, to the north, to the south, and to the east. Look well, for you shall not cross over this Jordan.
text.Rotherham: Ascend the top of the Pisgah, and lift up thine eyes—westward, and northward and southward and eastward—and see with thine own eyes,—for thou shalt not pass over this Jordan.
text.KJV: 3:27 Get thee vp into the top of Pisgah, and lift vp thine eyes Westward, and Northward, and Southward, and Eastward, and beholde it with thine eyes: for thou shalt not goe ouer this Iordan.
text.NABRE: 3:27 Go up to the top of Pisgah and look out to the west, and to the north, and to the south, and to the east. Look well, for you shall not cross this Jordan.

text.NRSV: But charge Joshua, and encourage and strengthen him, because it is he who shall cross over at the head of this people and who shall secure their possession of the land that you will see.”
text.Rotherham: But command thou Joshua and confirm him and embolden him,—for, he, shall pass over before this people, and, he shall cause them to inherit the land which thou shalt see.
text.KJV: 3:28 But charge Ioshua, and encourage him, and strengthen him: for hee shall goe ouer before this people, and he shall cause them to inherite the land which thou shalt see.
text.NABRE: 3:28 Commission Joshua, and encourage and strengthen him, for it is he who will cross at the head of this people and he who will give them possession of the land you are to see.

text.NRSV: So we remained in the valley opposite Beth-peor.
text.Rotherham: So we abode in the valley, over against Beth-peor.
text.KJV: 3:29 So we abode in the valley, ouer against Beth-Peor.
text.NABRE: 3:29 So we remained in the valley opposite Beth-peor.

text.NRSV: So now, Israel, give heed to the statutes and ordinances that I am teaching you to observe, so that you may live to enter and occupy the land that the Lord, the God of your ancestors, is giving you.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, O Israel, hearken thou unto the statutes, and unto the regulations, which, I, am teaching you, to do [them],—to the end ye may live and enter and possess the land, which, Yahweh, the God of your fathers, is giving unto you.
text.KJV: 4:1 Nowe therefore hearken, O Israel, vnto the Statutes, and vnto the Iudgments which I teach you, for to do them, that ye may liue, and goe in and possesse the lande, which the Lord God of your fathers giueth you.
text.NABRE: 4:1 Chapter 4 - Advantages of Fidelity. Now therefore, Israel, hear the statutes and ordinances I am teaching you to observe, that you may live, and may enter in and take possession of the land which the LORD, the God of your ancestors, is giving you.

text.NRSV: You must neither add anything to what I command you nor take away anything from it, but keep the commandments of the Lord your God with which I am charging you.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not add unto the word which, I, am commanding you, neither shall ye take away therefrom,—that ye may keep the commandments of Yahweh your God, which, I, am commanding you.
text.KJV: 4:2 Ye shall not adde vnto the word which I command you, neither shall you diminish ought from it, that ye may keepe the Commaundements of the Lord your God, which I command you.
text.NABRE: 4:2 In your observance of the commandments of the LORD, your God, which I am commanding you, you shall not add to what I command you nor subtract from it.

text.NRSV: You have seen for yourselves what the Lord did with regard to the Baal of Peor—how the Lord your God destroyed from among you everyone who followed the Baal of Peor,
text.Rotherham: Yours are the eyes, that have seen what Yahweh did in Baal-peor, for every man who went after Baal-peor, did Yahweh thy God destroy out of thy midst;
text.KJV: 4:3 Your eyes haue seene what the Lord did because of Baal Peor: for all the men that followed Baal Peor, the Lord thy God hath destroyed them from among you.
text.NABRE: 4:3 You have seen with your own eyes what the LORD did at Baal-peor: the LORD, your God, destroyed from your midst everyone who followed the Baal of Peor;

text.NRSV: while those of you who held fast to the Lord your God are all alive today.
text.Rotherham: but, ye who kept on cleaving unto Yahweh your God, alive, are ye all to-day.
text.KJV: 4:4 But yee that did cleaue vnto the Lord your God, are aliue euery one of you this day.
text.NABRE: 4:4 but you, who held fast to the LORD, your God, are all alive today.

text.NRSV: See, just as the Lord my God has charged me, I now teach you statutes and ordinances for you to observe in the land that you are about to enter and occupy.
text.Rotherham: See! I have taught you statutes, and regulations, as Yahweh my God commanded me,—that ye should do so, in the midst of the land whereinto ye are entering to possess it.
text.KJV: 4:5 Behold, I haue taught you Statutes, and Iudgements, euen as the Lord my God commanded me, that ye should do so, in the land whither ye goe to possesse it.
text.NABRE: 4:5 See, I am teaching you the statutes and ordinances as the LORD, my God, has commanded me, that you may observe them in the land you are entering to possess.

text.NRSV: You must observe them diligently, for this will show your wisdom and discernment to the peoples, who, when they hear all these statutes, will say, ‘Surely this great nation is a wise and discerning people!’
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye observe, and do [them], for that, will be your wisdom, and discernment, in the eyes of the peoples,—who will hear all these statutes, and will say—Nevertheless, a people wise and discerning, is this great nation.
text.KJV: 4:6 Keepe therefore, and doe them; for this is your wisedome and your vnderstanding in the sight of the nations, which shall heare all these statutes, and say, Surely this great nation is a wise and vnderstanding people.
text.NABRE: 4:6 Observe them carefully, for this is your wisdom and discernment in the sight of the peoples, who will hear of all these statutes and say, “This great nation is truly a wise and discerning people.”

text.NRSV: For what other great nation has a god so near to it as the Lord our God is whenever we call to him?
text.Rotherham: For what great nation is there which hath gods nigh unto it,—like Yahweh our God, whensoever we have cried out unto him.
text.KJV: 4:7 For what nation is there so great, who hath God so nigh vnto them, as the Lord our God is in all things, that we call vpon him for?
text.NABRE: 4:7 For what great nation is there that has gods so close to it as the LORD, our God, is to us whenever we call upon him?

text.NRSV: And what other great nation has statutes and ordinances as just as this entire law that I am setting before you today?
text.Rotherham: Or what great nation which hath righteous statutes and regulations, like all this law, which, I, am setting before you to-day?
text.KJV: 4:8 And what nation is there so great, that hath Statutes and Iudgements so righteous, as all this Law which I set before you this day?
text.NABRE: 4:8 Or what great nation has statutes and ordinances that are as just as this whole law which I am setting before you today?

text.NRSV: But take care and watch yourselves closely, so as neither to forget the things that your eyes have seen nor to let them slip from your mind all the days of your life; make them known to your children and your children’s children—
text.Rotherham: Only take thou heed to thyself and keep thy soul diligently, so that thou forget not the things which thine own eyes have seen, and so that they go not out of thy heart, all the days of thy life,—but thou shalt make them known unto thy sons and unto thy sons’ sons:—
text.KJV: 4:9 Onely take heed to thy selfe, and keepe thy soule diligently, lest thou forget the things which thine eyes haue seene, and lest they depart from thy heart all the dayes of thy life: but teach them thy sonnes, & thy sonnes sonnes:
text.NABRE: 4:9 Revelation at Horeb. However, be on your guard and be very careful not to forget the things your own eyes have seen, nor let them slip from your heart as long as you live, but make them known to your children and to your children’s children,

text.NRSV: how you once stood before the Lord your God at Horeb, when the Lord said to me, ‘Assemble the people for me, and I will let them hear my words, so that they may learn to fear me as long as they live on the earth, and may teach their children to do so’;
text.Rotherham: What day thou didst stand before Yahweh thy God, in Horeb, when Yahweh said unto me, Gather unto me the people, that I may let them hear my words,—which they must learn that they may revere me all the days which they are living upon the soil, their children also, must they teach.
text.KJV: 4:10 Specially, the day that thou stoodst before the Lord thy God in Horeb, when the Lord said vnto mee, Gather me the people together, and I will make them heare my wordes, that they may learne to feare mee all the dayes that they shall liue vpon the earth, and that they may teach their children.
text.NABRE: 4:10 that day you stood before the LORD, your God, at Horeb, when the LORD said to me: Assemble the people for me, that I may let them hear my words, that they may learn to fear me as long as they live in the land and may so teach their children.

text.NRSV: you approached and stood at the foot of the mountain while the mountain was blazing up to the very heavens, shrouded in dark clouds.
text.Rotherham: So ye drew near and stood, under the mountain. Now, the mountain, was burning with fire, up to the midst of the heavens,—darkness, cloud, and thick gloom.
text.KJV: 4:11 And ye came neere and stood vnder the mountaine, and the mountaine burnt with fire vnto the midst of heauen, with darkenes, cloudes, and thicke darkenesse.
text.NABRE: 4:11 You came near and stood at the foot of the mountain, while the mountain blazed to the heart of the heavens with fire and was enveloped in a dense black cloud.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord spoke to you out of the fire. You heard the sound of words but saw no form; there was only a voice.
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto you out of the midst of the fire,—a voice of words, ye were hearing, no form, were ye seeing, there was only a voice.
text.KJV: 4:12 And the Lord spake vnto you out of the midst of the fire: ye heard the voyce of the words, but saw no similitude, onely ye heard a voyce.
text.NABRE: 4:12 Then the LORD spoke to you from the midst of the fire. You heard the sound of the words, but saw no form; there was only a voice.

text.NRSV: He declared to you his covenant, which he charged you to observe, that is, the ten commandments; and he wrote them on two stone tablets.
text.Rotherham: Then declared he unto you his covenant which he commanded you to do, the ten words,—and wrote them upon two tables of stone.
text.KJV: 4:13 And he declared vnto you his couenant, which he commanded you to performe, euen ten cōmandements, and he wrote them vpon two tables of stone.
text.NABRE: 4:13 He proclaimed to you his covenant, which he commanded you to keep: the ten words, which he wrote on two stone tablets.

text.NRSV: And the Lord charged me at that time to teach you statutes and ordinances for you to observe in the land that you are about to cross into and occupy.
