text.Rotherham: and thou shalt do according to the sentence which they shall declare unto thee, out of that place, which Yahweh, shall choose,—yea thou shalt observe to do according to all which they shall direct thee:
text.KJV: 17:10 And thou shalt doe according to the sentence which they of that place (which the Lord shall choose) shall shew thee, and thou shalt obserue to do according to all that they enforme thee:
text.NABRE: 17:10 You shall act according to the decision they announce to you in the place which the LORD will choose, carefully observing everything as they instruct you.

text.NRSV: You must carry out fully the law that they interpret for you or the ruling that they announce to you; do not turn aside from the decision that they announce to you, either to the right or to the left.
text.Rotherham: according to the direction wherewith they direct thee and after the judgment which they shall announce to thee, shalt thou do,—thou shalt not turn aside from the sentence which they shall declare unto thee to the right hand or to the left.
text.KJV: 17:11 According to the sentence of the Law which they shall teach thee, and according to the Iudgement which they shall tell thee thou shalt doe: thou shalt not decline from the Sentence which they shall shew thee, to the right hand, nor to the left.
text.NABRE: 17:11 You shall carry out the instruction they give you and the judgment they pronounce, without turning aside either to the right or left from the decision they announce to you.

text.NRSV: As for anyone who presumes to disobey the priest appointed to minister there to the Lord your God, or the judge, that person shall die. So you shall purge the evil from Israel.
text.Rotherham: And, the man who shall do presumptuously by not hearkening unto the priest that standeth to minister there to Yahweh thy God, or unto the judge, that man shall die, and so shalt thou consume the wicked thing out of Israel.
text.KJV: 17:12 And the man that will doe presumptuously, and will not hearken vnto the Priest (that standeth to minister there before the Lord thy God) or vnto the Iudge, euen that man shall die, and thou shalt put away the euill from Israel.
text.NABRE: 17:12 Anyone who acts presumptuously and does not obey the priest who officiates there in the ministry of the LORD, your God, or the judge, shall die. Thus shall you purge the evil from Israel.

text.NRSV: All the people will hear and be afraid, and will not act presumptuously again.
text.Rotherham: And, all the people, shall hear and fear,—and shall not act presumptuously any more.
text.KJV: 17:13 And all the people shal heare, and feare, and doe no more presumptuously.
text.NABRE: 17:13 And all the people, on hearing of it, shall fear, and will never again act presumptuously.

text.NRSV: When you have come into the land that the Lord your God is giving you, and have taken possession of it and settled in it, and you say, “I will set a king over me, like all the nations that are around me,”
text.Rotherham: When thou shalt enter into the land which Yahweh thy God is giving unto thee, and shalt possess it and dwell therein,—and shalt say—I will set over me a king, like all the nations that are round about me,
text.KJV: 17:14 ¶ When thou art come vnto the land which the Lord thy God giueth thee, and shalt possesse it, and shalt dwell therein, and shalt say, I will set a King ouer mee, like as all the nations that are about me:
text.NABRE: 17:14 The King. When you have come into the land which the LORD, your God, is giving you, and have taken possession of it and settled in it, should you then decide, “I will set a king over me, like all the surrounding nations,”

text.NRSV: you may indeed set over you a king whom the Lord your God will choose. One of your own community you may set as king over you; you are not permitted to put a foreigner over you, who is not of your own community.
text.Rotherham: thou shalt, set, over thee as king, him whom Yahweh thy God shall choose,—out of the midst of thy brethren, shalt thou set over thee a king, thou mayest not appoint over thee a man that is a foreigner, who is not thy brother.
text.KJV: 17:15 Thou shalt in any wise set him King ouer thee, whom the Lord thy God shall choose. One from among thy brethren shalt thou set King ouer thee: thou mayest not set a stranger ouer thee, which is not thy brother.
text.NABRE: 17:15 you may indeed set over you a king whom the LORD, your God, will choose. Someone from among your own kindred you may set over you as king; you may not set over you a foreigner, who is no kin of yours.

text.NRSV: Even so, he must not acquire many horses for himself, or return the people to Egypt in order to acquire more horses, since the Lord has said to you, “You must never return that way again.”
text.Rotherham: Moreover he shall not multiply to himself horses, neither shall he cause the people to return to Egypt that he may multiply horses,—when, Yahweh, hath said unto you, Ye shall not again return this way any more.
text.KJV: 17:16 But he shall not multiply horses to himselfe, nor cause the people to returne to Egypt, to the ende that hee should multiply horses: for as much as the Lord hath said vnto you, Yee shall hencefoorth returne no more that way.
text.NABRE: 17:16 But he shall not have a great number of horses; nor shall he make his people go back again to Egypt to acquire many horses, for the LORD said to you, Do not go back that way again.

text.NRSV: And he must not acquire many wives for himself, or else his heart will turn away; also silver and gold he must not acquire in great quantity for himself.
text.Rotherham: Neither shall he multiply to himself wives, that his heart turn not aside,—nor silver and gold, shall he multiply to himself greatly.
text.KJV: 17:17 Neither shall he multiply wiues to himselfe, that his heart turne not away: neither shall hee greatly multiply to himselfe siluer and gold.
text.NABRE: 17:17 Neither shall he have a great number of wives, lest his heart turn away, nor shall he accumulate a vast amount of silver and gold.

text.NRSV: When he has taken the throne of his kingdom, he shall have a copy of this law written for him in the presence of the levitical priests.
text.Rotherham: But it shall be when he sitteth upon the throne of his kingdom, then shall he write for himself a copy of this law upon a scroll, out of that which is before the priests the Levites:
text.KJV: 17:18 And it shall be when he sitteth vpon the Throne of his kingdome, that he shall write him a copy of this Law in a booke, out of that which is before the Priests the Leuites.
text.NABRE: 17:18 When he is sitting upon his royal throne, he shall write a copy of this law upon a scroll from the one that is in the custody of the levitical priests.

text.NRSV: It shall remain with him and he shall read in it all the days of his life, so that he may learn to fear the Lord his God, diligently observing all the words of this law and these statutes,
text.Rotherham: so shall it be with him, and he shall read therein all the days of his life,—that he may learn to revere Yahweh his God, to observe all the words of this law and these statutes to do them:
text.KJV: 17:19 And it shall be with him, and hee shall reade therein all the dayes of his life, that hee may learne to feare the Lord his God, to keep all the words of this Law, and these Statutes, to do them:
text.NABRE: 17:19 It shall remain with him and he shall read it as long as he lives, so that he may learn to fear the LORD, his God, and to observe carefully all the words of this law and these statutes,

text.NRSV: neither exalting himself above other members of the community nor turning aside from the commandment, either to the right or to the left, so that he and his descendants may reign long over his kingdom in Israel.
text.Rotherham: that his heart may not be lifted up above his brethren, and he may not turn aside from the commandment to the right hand or to the left,—that he may prolong his days over his kingdom—he and his sons, in the midst of Israel.
text.KJV: 17:20 That his heart bee not lifted vp aboue his brethren, and that hee turne not aside from the Commandement, to the right hand, or to the left: to the end that hee may prolong his dayes in his kingdome, hee, and his children in the midst of Israel.
text.NABRE: 17:20 so that he does not exalt himself over his kindred or turn aside from this commandment to the right or to the left, and so that he and his descendants may reign long in Israel.

text.NRSV: The levitical priests, the whole tribe of Levi, shall have no allotment or inheritance within Israel. They may eat the sacrifices that are the Lord’s portion
text.Rotherham: Neither the priests the Levites [nor] any of the tribe of Levi, shall have either portion or inheritance with Israel,—the altar-flames of Yahweh and his inheritance, shall they eat.
text.KJV: 18:1 The Priests, the Leuites, and all the tribe of Leui, shall haue no part nor inheritance with Israel: they shall eate the offerings of the Lord made by fire, and his inheritance.
text.NABRE: 18:1 Chapter 18 - Priests. The levitical priests, the whole tribe of Levi, shall have no hereditary portion with Israel; they shall eat the fire offerings of the LORD and the portions due to him.

text.NRSV: but they shall have no inheritance among the other members of the community; the Lord is their inheritance, as he promised them.
text.Rotherham: But inheritance, shall he not have, in the midst of his brethren,—Yahweh, is his inheritance, as he spake unto him.
text.KJV: 18:2 Therefore shall they haue no inheritance among their brethren: the Lord is their inheritance, as he hath said vnto them.
text.NABRE: 18:2 They shall have no heritage among their kindred; the LORD himself is their heritage, as he has told them.

text.NRSV: This shall be the priests’ due from the people, from those offering a sacrifice, whether an ox or a sheep: they shall give to the priest the shoulder, the two jowls, and the stomach.
text.Rotherham: This, therefore shall be the due of the priests from the people from them who offer the sacrifice whether ox or lamb,—there shall be given unto the priest, the shoulder and the two cheeks and the maw:
text.KJV: 18:3 ¶ And this shalbe the Priests due from the people, from them that offer a sacrifice, whether it bee oxe or sheepe: and they shall giue vnto the Priest the shoulder, and the two cheekes, and the maw.
text.NABRE: 18:3 This shall be the due of the priests from the people: those who are offering a sacrifice, whether an ox or a sheep, shall give the priest the shoulder, the jowls and the stomach.

text.NRSV: The first fruits of your grain, your wine, and your oil, as well as the first of the fleece of your sheep, you shall give him.
text.Rotherham: the firstfruit of thy corn thy new wine and thine oil and the first of the fleece of thy flock, shalt thou give unto him.
text.KJV: 18:4 The first fruit also of thy corne, of thy wine, and of thy oyle, and the first of the fleece of thy sheepe, shalt thou giue him.
text.NABRE: 18:4 The first fruits of your grain, your wine, and your oil, as well as the first shearing of your flock, you shall also give him.

text.NRSV: For the Lord your God has chosen Levi out of all your tribes, to stand and minister in the name of the Lord, him and his sons for all time.
text.Rotherham: For of him, hath Yahweh thy God made choice out of all thy tribes,—to stand to minister in the name of Yahweh of him and his sons all the days.
text.KJV: 18:5 For the Lord thy God hath chosen him out of all thy tribes, to stand to minister in the Name of the Lord, him, and his sonnes for euer.
text.NABRE: 18:5 For the LORD, your God, has chosen him out of all your tribes to be in attendance to minister in the name of the LORD, him and his descendants for all time.

text.NRSV: If a Levite leaves any of your towns, from wherever he has been residing in Israel, and comes to the place that the Lord will choose (and he may come whenever he wishes),
text.Rotherham: And when the Levite shall come in out of any of thy gates out of any part of Israel, where he is sojourning,—yea shall come in with all the desire of his soul, into the place which Yahweh shall choose,
text.KJV: 18:6 ¶ And if a Leuite come from any of thy gates out of all Israel, where he soiourned, and come with all the desire of his minde, vnto the place which the Lord shall choose;
text.NABRE: 18:6 When a Levite goes from one of your communities anywhere in Israel in which he has been residing, to visit, as his heart may desire, the place which the LORD will choose,

text.NRSV: then he may minister in the name of the Lord his God, like all his fellow-Levites who stand to minister there before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: then may he minister in the name of Yahweh his God,—like any of his brethren the Levites who are standing there before Yahweh.
text.KJV: 18:7 Then hee shall minister in the Name of the Lord his God, as all his brethren the Leuites doe, which stand there before the Lord.
text.NABRE: 18:7 and ministers there in the name of the LORD, his God, like all his fellow Levites who stand before the LORD there,

text.NRSV: They shall have equal portions to eat, even though they have income from the sale of family possessions.
text.Rotherham: Portion for portion, shall they eat,—apart from his possessions upon his patrimony.
text.KJV: 18:8 They shall haue like portions to eate, beside that which commeth of the sale of his patrimonie.
text.NABRE: 18:8 he shall receive the same portions to eat, along with his stipends and patrimony.

text.NRSV: When you come into the land that the Lord your God is giving you, you must not learn to imitate the abhorrent practices of those nations.
text.Rotherham: When thou art coming into the land which Yahweh thy God is giving unto thee, thou shalt not learn to do according to the abominable doings of those nations.
text.KJV: 18:9 ¶ When thou art come into the land which the Lord thy God giueth thee, thou shalt not learne to doe after the abominations of those nations.
text.NABRE: 18:9 Prophets. When you come into the land which the LORD, your God, is giving you, you shall not learn to imitate the abominations of the nations there.

text.NRSV: No one shall be found among you who makes a son or daughter pass through fire, or who practices divination, or is a soothsayer, or an augur, or a sorcerer,
text.Rotherham: There shall not be found in thy midst—One who causeth his son or his daughter to pass through fire,—Or who useth divination, hidden arts or enchantments Or who muttereth incantations;
text.KJV: 18:10 There shall not be found among you any one that maketh his sonne, or his daughter to passe thorow the fire, or that vseth diuination, or an obseruer of times, or an inchanter, or a witch,
text.NABRE: 18:10 Let there not be found among you anyone who causes their son or daughter to pass through the fire, or practices divination, or is a soothsayer, augur, or sorcerer,

text.NRSV: or one who casts spells, or who consults ghosts or spirits, or who seeks oracles from the dead.
text.Rotherham: Or who bindeth with spells,—Or who asketh of a familiar spirit or an oracle, Or who seeketh unto the dead.
text.KJV: 18:11 Or a charmer, or a consulter with familiar spirits, or a wyzard, or a Necromancer.
text.NABRE: 18:11 or who casts spells, consults ghosts and spirits, or seeks oracles from the dead.

text.NRSV: For whoever does these things is abhorrent to the Lord; it is because of such abhorrent practices that the Lord your God is driving them out before you.
text.Rotherham: For, an abomination unto Yahweh, is everyone who doeth these things,—and on account of these abominations, is Yahweh thy God dispossessing them from before thee.
text.KJV: 18:12 For all that do these things, are an abomination vnto the Lord: and because of these abominations, the Lord thy God doth driue them out from before thee.
text.NABRE: 18:12 Anyone who does such things is an abomination to the LORD, and because of such abominations the LORD, your God, is dispossessing them before you.

text.NRSV: You must remain completely loyal to the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Blameless, shalt thou be with, Yahweh thy God;
text.KJV: 18:13 Thou shalt bee perfite with the Lord thy God.
text.NABRE: 18:13 You must be altogether sincere with the LORD, your God.

text.NRSV: Although these nations that you are about to dispossess do give heed to soothsayers and diviners, as for you, the Lord your God does not permit you to do so.
text.Rotherham: for, these nations whom thou art dispossessing, unto them who use hidden arts and unto diviners, do hearken, but as for thee, not so, doth Yahweh thy God suffer thee.
text.KJV: 18:14 For these nations which thou shalt possesse, hearkened vnto obseruers of times, and vnto diuiners: but as for thee, the Lord thy God hath not suffered thee so to doe.
text.NABRE: 18:14 Although these nations whom you are about to dispossess listen to their soothsayers and diviners, the LORD, your God, will not permit you to do so.

text.NRSV: The Lord your God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among your own people; you shall heed such a prophet.
text.Rotherham: A prophet out of thy midst of thy brethren like unto me, will Yahweh thy God, raise up unto thee—unto him, shall ye hearken:
text.KJV: 18:15 ¶ The Lord thy God will raise vp vnto thee a Prophet from the midst of thee, of thy brethren, like vnto me, vnto him ye shall hearken,
text.NABRE: 18:15 A prophet like me will the LORD, your God, raise up for you from among your own kindred; that is the one to whom you shall listen.

text.NRSV: This is what you requested of the Lord your God at Horeb on the day of the assembly when you said: “If I hear the voice of the Lord my God any more, or ever again see this great fire, I will die.”
text.Rotherham: according to all which thou didst ask of Yahweh thy God, in Horeb, in the day of the convocation saying,—Let me no further hearken unto the voice of Yahweh my God, and this great fire, let me not see any more lest I die.
text.KJV: 18:16 According to all that thou desiredst of the Lord thy God in Horeb, in the day of the assembly, saying, Let mee not heare again the voice of the Lord my God, neither let mee see this great fire any more, that I die not.
text.NABRE: 18:16 This is exactly what you requested of the LORD, your God, at Horeb on the day of the assembly, when you said, “Let me not again hear the voice of the LORD, my God, nor see this great fire any more, or I will die.”

text.NRSV: Then the Lord replied to me: “They are right in what they have said.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto me: They have well said what they have spoken.
text.KJV: 18:17 And the Lord said vnto mee, They haue well spoken that which they haue spoken.
text.NABRE: 18:17 And the LORD said to me, What they have said is good.

text.NRSV: I will raise up for them a prophet like you from among their own people; I will put my words in the mouth of the prophet, who shall speak to them everything that I command.
text.Rotherham: A prophet, will I raise up unto them, out of the midst of their brethren, like unto thee,—and I will put my words in his mouth, so shall he speak unto them whatsoever I shall command him.
text.KJV: 18:18 I will raise them vp a Prophet from among their brethren, like vnto thee, and will put my wordes in his mouth, and hee shall speake vnto them all that I shall command him.
text.NABRE: 18:18 I will raise up for them a prophet like you from among their kindred, and will put my words into the mouth of the prophet; the prophet shall tell them all that I command.

text.NRSV: Anyone who does not heed the words that the prophet shall speak in my name, I myself will hold accountable.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass, that, the man who will not hearken unto my words which he shall speak in my name, I, myself will require it of him.
text.KJV: 18:19 And it shall come to passe, that whosoeuer will not hearken vnto my words, which hee shall speake in my name, I will require it of him.
text.NABRE: 18:19 Anyone who will not listen to my words which the prophet speaks in my name, I myself will hold accountable for it.

text.NRSV: But any prophet who speaks in the name of other gods, or who presumes to speak in my name a word that I have not commanded the prophet to speak—that prophet shall die.”
text.Rotherham: Howbeit the prophet who shall presume to speak a word in my name which I have not commanded him to speak, or who shall speak in the name of other gods, that prophet shall die.
text.KJV: 18:20 But the prophet which shall presume to speake a word in my name, which I haue not commanded him to speake, or that shall speake in the name of other gods, euen that prophet shall die.
text.NABRE: 18:20 But if a prophet presumes to speak a word in my name that I have not commanded, or speaks in the name of other gods, that prophet shall die.

text.NRSV: You may say to yourself, “How can we recognize a word that the Lord has not spoken?”
text.Rotherham: And, when thou shalt say in thy heart,—In what manner shall we know the word which Yahweh hath not spoken!?,
text.KJV: 18:21 And if thou say in thine heart, How shall wee know the word which the Lord hath not spoken?
text.NABRE: 18:21 Should you say to yourselves, “How can we recognize that a word is one the LORD has not spoken?”,

text.NRSV: If a prophet speaks in the name of the Lord but the thing does not take place or prove true, it is a word that the Lord has not spoken. The prophet has spoken it presumptuously; do not be frightened by it.
text.Rotherham: When the prophet shall speak in the name of Yahweh and the word shall not come to pass, neither shall come in, that, is the word which Yahweh, hath not spoken,—presumptuously, hath the prophet spoken it, thou shall not be in dread of him.
text.KJV: 18:22 When a prophet speaketh in the name of the Lord, if the thing follow not, nor come to passe, that is the thing which the Lord hath not spoken, but the prophet hath spoken it presumptuously: thou shalt not bee afraid of him.
text.NABRE: 18:22 if a prophet speaks in the name of the LORD but the word does not come true, it is a word the LORD did not speak. The prophet has spoken it presumptuously; do not fear him.

text.NRSV: When the Lord your God has cut off the nations whose land the Lord your God is giving you, and you have dispossessed them and settled in their towns and in their houses,
text.Rotherham: When Yahweh thy God, shall cut off the nations whose land Yahweh thy God is giving thee,—and thou shalt dispossess them, and dwell in their cities and in their houses,
text.KJV: 19:1 When the Lord thy God hath cut off the nations, whose lande the Lord thy God giueth thee, and thou succeedest them, and dwellest in their cities, and in their houses:
text.NABRE: 19:1 Chapter 19 - Cities of Refuge. When the LORD, your God, cuts down the nations whose land the LORD, your God, is giving you, and you have dispossessed them and settled in their cities and houses,

text.NRSV: you shall set apart three cities in the land that the Lord your God is giving you to possess.
text.Rotherham: three cities, shalt thou set apart for thee,—in the midst of thy land which Yahweh thy God is giving thee to possess.
text.KJV: 19:2 Thou shalt separate three cities for thee in the midst of thy land, which the Lord thy God giueth thee to possesse it.
text.NABRE: 19:2 you shall set apart three cities in the land the LORD, your God, is giving you to possess.

text.NRSV: You shall calculate the distances and divide into three regions the land that the Lord your God gives you as a possession, so that any homicide can flee to one of them.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt prepare for thee a way, and shalt divide into three parts the boundary of thy land which Yahweh thy God shall cause thee to inherit,—and it shall be that every manslayer shall flee thither.
text.KJV: 19:3 Thou shalt prepare thee a way, and diuide the coasts of thy land (which the Lord thy God giueth thee to inherit) into three parts, that euery slayer may flee thither.
text.NABRE: 19:3 You shall measure the distances and divide into three regions the land of which the LORD, your God, is giving you possession, so that every homicide will be able to find a refuge.

text.NRSV: Now this is the case of a homicide who might flee there and live, that is, someone who has killed another person unintentionally when the two had not been at enmity before:
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the case of the manslayer, who shall flee thither and live,—Whoso shall smite his neighbour unwittingly, he, not having hated him aforetime;
text.KJV: 19:4 ¶ And this is the case of the slayer which shall flee thither, that hee may liue: who so killeth his neighbour ignorantly, whom he hated not in time past,
text.NABRE: 19:4 This is the case of a homicide who may take refuge there and live: when someone strikes down a neighbor unintentionally and not out of previous hatred.

text.NRSV: Suppose someone goes into the forest with another to cut wood, and when one of them swings the ax to cut down a tree, the head slips from the handle and strikes the other person who then dies; the killer may flee to one of these cities and live.
text.Rotherham: as when one entereth with his neighbour into a forest, to fell trees, and his hand fetcheth a stroke with the axe, to cut down the tree, and the head flieth off from the handle, and lighteth upon his neighbour, that he die, he, shall flee into one of these cities, and live;
text.KJV: 19:5 As when a man goeth into the wood with his neighbor, to hew wood, and his hand fetcheth a stroke with the axe to cut downe the tree, and the head slippeth from the helue, and lighteth vpon his neighbour that he die, he shall flee vnto one of those cities, and liue:
text.NABRE: 19:5 For example, if someone goes with a neighbor to a forest to cut wood, wielding an ax to cut down a tree, and its head flies off the handle and hits the neighbor a mortal blow, such a person may take refuge in one of these cities and live.

text.NRSV: But if the distance is too great, the avenger of blood in hot anger might pursue and overtake and put the killer to death, although a death sentence was not deserved, since the two had not been at enmity before.
text.Rotherham: lest the blood redeemer pursue the manslayer because his heart is hot, and overtake him because the way is long, and he smite him so as to take away his life,—he not being worthy of death, seeing that he had not been hating him aforetime.
text.KJV: 19:6 Lest the auenger of the blood pursue the slaier, while his heart is hot, and ouertake him, because the way is long, and slay him, whereas he was not worthy of death, in as much as hee hated him not in time past.
text.NABRE: 19:6 Should the distance be too great, the avenger of blood might in hot anger pursue, overtake, and strike the killer dead, even though that one does not deserve the death penalty since there had been no previous hatred;

text.NRSV: Therefore I command you: You shall set apart three cities.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, am I commanding thee saying,—Three cities, shalt thou set apart for thee.
text.KJV: 19:7 Wherefore I command thee, saying, Thou shalt separate three cities for thee.
text.NABRE: 19:7 for this reason I command you: Set apart three cities.

text.NRSV: If the Lord your God enlarges your territory, as he swore to your ancestors—and he will give you all the land that he promised your ancestors to give you,
text.Rotherham: But, if Yahweh thy God should enlarge thy bounds, as he sware unto thy fathers,—and should give thee all the land, which he spake of giving unto thy fathers;
text.KJV: 19:8 And if the Lord thy God enlarge thy coast (as he hath sworne vnto thy fathers) and giue thee all the lande which hee promised to giue vnto thy fathers:
text.NABRE: 19:8 But if the LORD, your God, enlarges your territory, as he swore to your ancestors, and gives you all the land he promised your ancestors he would give,

text.NRSV: provided you diligently observe this entire commandment that I command you today, by loving the Lord your God and walking always in his ways—then you shall add three more cities to these three,
text.Rotherham: because thou dost observe all this commandment to do it which I am commanding thee to-day, to love Yahweh thy God and to walk in his paths all the days, then shalt thou add unto thee yet three cities, unto these three;
text.KJV: 19:9 (If thou shalt keepe all these commandements to doe them, which I command thee this day, to loue the Lord thy God, and to walke euer in his wayes) then shalt thou adde three cities moe for thee, beside these three:
text.NABRE: 19:9 because you carefully observe this whole commandment which I give you today, loving the LORD, your God, and ever walking in his ways, then add three more cities to these three.

text.NRSV: so that the blood of an innocent person may not be shed in the land that the Lord your God is giving you as an inheritance, thereby bringing bloodguilt upon you.
text.Rotherham: that innocent blood may not be shed in the midst of thy land, which Yahweh thy God is giving unto thee as an inheritance,—and so shed-blood be upon thee.
text.KJV: 19:10 That innocent blood be not shed in thy land which the Lord thy God giueth thee for an inheritance, and so blood be vpon thee.
text.NABRE: 19:10 Thus, in the land which the LORD, your God, is giving you as a heritage, innocent blood will not be shed and you will not become guilty of bloodshed.

text.NRSV: But if someone at enmity with another lies in wait and attacks and takes the life of that person, and flees into one of these cities,
text.Rotherham: But, when any man shall be hating his neighbour, and shall lie in wait for him and rise up against him, and smite him so as to take away his life that he dieth,—and shall then flee into one of these cities,
text.KJV: 19:11 ¶ But if any man hate his neighbour and lie in wait for him, and rise vp against him, and smite him mortally that hee die, and fleeth into one of these Cities:
text.NABRE: 19:11 However, if someone, hating a neighbor, lies in wait, attacks, and strikes the neighbor dead, and then flees to one of these cities,

text.NRSV: then the elders of the killer’s city shall send to have the culprit taken from there and handed over to the avenger of blood to be put to death.
text.Rotherham: then shall the elders of his city send and fetch him from thence,—and deliver him into the hand of the blood redeemer that he die;
text.KJV: 19:12 Then the Elders of his citie shall send and fetch him thence, and deliuer him into the hand of the auenger of blood, that he may die.
text.NABRE: 19:12 the elders of the killer’s own city shall send and have the killer taken from there, to be handed over to the avenger of blood and slain.

text.NRSV: Show no pity; you shall purge the guilt of innocent blood from Israel, so that it may go well with you.
text.Rotherham: thine eye shall not look with pity upon him,—so shall thou consume the shedding of innocent blood out of Israel and it shall go well with thee.
text.KJV: 19:13 Thine eye shall not pittie him, but thou shalt put away the guilt of innocent blood from Israel, that it may goe wel with thee.
text.NABRE: 19:13 Do not show pity, but purge from Israel the innocent blood, so that it may go well with you.

text.NRSV: You must not move your neighbor’s boundary marker, set up by former generations, on the property that will be allotted to you in the land that the Lord your God is giving you to possess.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not move back the boundary of thy neighbour, by which they set bounds at first,—in thine inheritance which thou shall receive in the land which Yahweh thy God is giving thee to possess.
text.KJV: 19:14 ¶ Thou shalt not remooue thy neighbours land-marke, which they of old time haue set in thine inheritance, which thou shalt inherite, in the land that the Lord thy God giueth thee to possesse it.
text.NABRE: 19:14 Removal of Landmarks. You shall not move your neighbor’s boundary markers erected by your forebears in the heritage that will be allotted to you in the land the LORD, your God, is giving you to possess.

text.NRSV: A single witness shall not suffice to convict a person of any crime or wrongdoing in connection with any offense that may be committed. Only on the evidence of two or three witnesses shall a charge be sustained.
text.Rotherham: One witness, shall not rise up, against a man for any iniquity, or for any sin, with regard to any sin that he may commit,—at the mouth of two witnesses or at the mouth of three witnesses, must a matter be establish.
text.KJV: 19:15 ¶ One witnesse shall not rise vp against a man for any iniquitie, or for any sinne, in any sinne that he sinneth: at the mouth of two witnesses, or at the mouth of three witnesses, shall the matter be stablished.
text.NABRE: 19:15 False Witnesses. One witness alone shall not stand against someone in regard to any crime or any offense that may have been committed; a charge shall stand only on the testimony of two or three witnesses.

text.NRSV: If a malicious witness comes forward to accuse someone of wrongdoing,
text.Rotherham: When there shall rise up a wrongful witness against a man,—to answer against him perversely,
text.KJV: 19:16 ¶ If a false witnes rise vp against any man to testifie against him that which is wrong:
text.NABRE: 19:16 If a hostile witness rises against someone to accuse that person of wrongdoing,

text.NRSV: then both parties to the dispute shall appear before the Lord, before the priests and the judges who are in office in those days,
text.Rotherham: then shall the two men who are at variance stand before Yahweh,—before the priests and the judges who shall be in those days;
text.KJV: 19:17 Then both the men betweene whom the controuersie is, shall stand before the Lord, before the Priests, and the Iudges, which shall be in those dayes.
text.NABRE: 19:17 the two parties in the dispute shall appear in the presence of the LORD, in the presence of the priests and judges in office at that time,

text.NRSV: and the judges shall make a thorough inquiry. If the witness is a false witness, having testified falsely against another,
text.Rotherham: and the judges shall make diligent inquisition,—and lo! if the witness be, a false witness and, falsely, have answered against his brother,
text.KJV: 19:18 And the Iudges shall make diligent inquisition: and behold, if the witnesse be a false witnesse, and hath testified falsly against his brother:
text.NABRE: 19:18 and the judges must investigate it thoroughly. If the witness is a false witness and has falsely accused the other,

text.NRSV: then you shall do to the false witness just as the false witness had meant to do to the other. So you shall purge the evil from your midst.
text.Rotherham: then shall ye do unto him as he had thought to de unto his brother,—so shalt thou consume the wicked thing out of thy midst;
text.KJV: 19:19 Then shall ye doe vnto him, as he had thought to haue done vnto his brother: so shalt thou put the euil away from among you.
text.NABRE: 19:19 you shall do to the false witness just as that false witness planned to do to the other. Thus shall you purge the evil from your midst.

text.NRSV: The rest shall hear and be afraid, and a crime such as this shall never again be committed among you.
text.Rotherham: and, they who remain shall hear and fear,—and not go on to do again according to this wicked thing, in thy midst:
text.KJV: 19:20 And those which remaine shall heare, and feare, and shall hencefoorth commit no more any such euill among you.
text.NABRE: 19:20 The rest shall hear and be afraid, and never again do such an evil thing as this in your midst.

text.NRSV: Show no pity: life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.
text.Rotherham: neither shall thine eye pity,—life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.
text.KJV: 19:21 And thine eye shall not pitie, but life shall goe for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot.
text.NABRE: 19:21 Do not show pity. Life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, and foot for foot!

text.NRSV: When you go out to war against your enemies, and see horses and chariots, an army larger than your own, you shall not be afraid of them; for the Lord your God is with you, who brought you up from the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: When thou shalt go out to wage war against thine enemy, and shalt see the horses and chariots of a people more in number than thou, thou shalt not be afraid of them,—for, Yahweh thy God, is with thee, who brought thee up out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: 20:1 When thou goest out to battell against thine enemies, and seest horses and charets, and a people more then thou, be not afraid of them: for the Lord thy God is with thee, which brought thee vp out of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: 20:1 Chapter 20 - Courage in War. When you go out to war against your enemies and you see horses and chariots and an army greater than your own, you shall not be afraid of them, for the LORD, your God, who brought you up from the land of Egypt, will be with you.

text.NRSV: Before you engage in battle, the priest shall come forward and speak to the troops,
text.Rotherham: And it shall be, when ye are coming nigh unto the battle, that the priest shall approach and speak unto the people;
text.KJV: 20:2 And it shall bee when ye are come nigh vnto the battell, that the Priest shall approach and speake vnto the people,
text.NABRE: 20:2 When you are drawing near to battle, the priest shall come forward and speak to the army,

text.NRSV: and shall say to them: “Hear, O Israel! Today you are drawing near to do battle against your enemies. Do not lose heart, or be afraid, or panic, or be in dread of them;
text.Rotherham: and shall say unto them,—Hear, O Israel, ye are coming near to-day, unto battle against your enemies,—let not your heart faint do not fear nor start nor quake because of them.
text.KJV: 20:3 And shall say vnto them, Heare O Israel, you approach this day vnto battell against your enemies: let not your hearts faint, feare not, and doe not tremble, neither be ye terrified because of them.
text.NABRE: 20:3 and say to them, “Hear, O Israel! Today you are drawing near for battle against your enemies. Do not be weakhearted or afraid, alarmed or frightened by them.

text.NRSV: for it is the Lord your God who goes with you, to fight for you against your enemies, to give you victory.”
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh your God, is he that is going with you,—to fight for you against your enemies to save you.
text.KJV: 20:4 For the Lord your God is hee that goeth with you, to fight for you against your enemies, to saue you.
text.NABRE: 20:4 For it is the LORD, your God, who goes with you to fight for you against your enemies and give you victory.”

text.NRSV: Then the officials shall address the troops, saying, “Has anyone built a new house but not dedicated it? He should go back to his house, or he might die in the battle and another dedicate it.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the officers speak unto the people saying, Who is the man that hath built a new house and hath not dedicated it? let him go and return unto his house,—lest he die in the battle, and, another man dedicate it.
text.KJV: 20:5 ¶ And the Officers shall speake vnto the people, saying, What man is there that hath built a new house, and hath not dedicated it? let him goe and returne to his house, lest hee die in the battell, and an other man dedicate it.
text.NABRE: 20:5 Then the officials shall speak to the army: “Is there anyone who has built a new house and not yet dedicated it? Let him return home, lest he die in battle and another dedicate it.

text.NRSV: Has anyone planted a vineyard but not yet enjoyed its fruit? He should go back to his house, or he might die in the battle and another be first to enjoy its fruit.
text.Rotherham: And who is the man that hath planted a vineyard and hath not thrown it open? let him go and return unto his house,—lest he die in the battle, and another man, throw it open.
text.KJV: 20:6 And what man is hee that hath planted a Uineyard, and hath not yet eaten of it? let him also go and returne vnto his house, lest he die in the battell, and an other man eate of it.
text.NABRE: 20:6 Is there anyone who has planted a vineyard and not yet plucked its fruit? Let him return home, lest he die in battle and another pluck its fruit.

text.NRSV: Has anyone become engaged to a woman but not yet married her? He should go back to his house, or he might die in the battle and another marry her.”
text.Rotherham: And who is the man that hath betrothed a wife and hath not taken her? let him go and return unto his house,—lest he die in the battle, and, another man, take her.
text.KJV: 20:7 And what man is there that hath betrothed a wife, and hath not taken her? let him goe and returne vnto his house, lest he die in battell, and another man take her.
text.NABRE: 20:7 Is there anyone who has betrothed a woman and not yet married her? Let him return home, lest he die in battle and another marry her.”

text.NRSV: The officials shall continue to address the troops, saying, “Is anyone afraid or disheartened? He should go back to his house, or he might cause the heart of his comrades to melt like his own.”
text.Rotherham: Then shall the officers further speak unto the people, and say—Who is the man that is fearful and faint-hearted? let him go and return unto his house,—lest his brethren’s heart melt as well as his heart.
text.KJV: 20:8 And the Officers shall speake further vnto the people: and they shall say, What man is there that is fearefull and faint hearted? let him goe and returne vnto his house, lest his brethrens heart faint as well as his heart.
text.NABRE: 20:8 The officials shall continue to speak to the army: “Is there anyone who is afraid and weakhearted? Let him return home, or else he might make the hearts of his fellows melt as his does.”

text.NRSV: When the officials have finished addressing the troops, then the commanders shall take charge of them.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be, when the officers have made an end of speaking unto the people, then shall they appoint captains of hosts at the head of the people.
text.KJV: 20:9 And it shall be when the Officers haue made an end of speaking vnto the people, that they shall make Captaines of the armies to leade the people.
text.NABRE: 20:9 When the officials have finished speaking to the army, military commanders shall be appointed over them.

text.NRSV: When you draw near to a town to fight against it, offer it terms of peace.
text.Rotherham: When thou shalt come nigh unto a city, to fight against it,—then shalt thou proclaim unto it—peace,
text.KJV: 20:10 ¶ When thou commest nigh vnto a City to fight against it, then proclaime peace vnto it.
text.NABRE: 20:10 Cities of the Enemy. When you draw near a city to attack it, offer it terms of peace.

text.NRSV: If it accepts your terms of peace and surrenders to you, then all the people in it shall serve you at forced labor.
text.Rotherham: and it shall be if peace, be the answer it giveth thee, and it open unto thee, then shall it be, that, all the people that are found therein, shall become thy tributaries and shall serve thee.
text.KJV: 20:11 And it shall be, if it make thee answere of peace, and open vnto thee, then it shalbe that all the people that is found therein, shall be tributaries vnto thee, and they shall serue thee.
text.NABRE: 20:11 If it agrees to your terms of peace and lets you in, all the people to be found in it shall serve you in forced labor.

text.NRSV: If it does not submit to you peacefully, but makes war against you, then you shall besiege it;
text.Rotherham: But if it will not make peace with thee, but will make war with thee, then shalt thou lay siege to it;
text.KJV: 20:12 And if it will make no peace with thee, but will make warre against thee, then thou shalt besiege it.
text.NABRE: 20:12 But if it refuses to make peace with you and instead joins battle with you, lay siege to it,

text.NRSV: and when the Lord your God gives it into your hand, you shall put all its males to the sword.
text.Rotherham: and Yahweh thy God will deliver it into thy hand,—and thou shalt smite every male thereof with the edge of the sword;
text.KJV: 20:13 And when the Lord thy God hath deliuered it into thine hands, thou shalt smite euery male thereof with the edge of the sword.
text.NABRE: 20:13 and when the LORD, your God, delivers it into your power, put every male in it to the sword;

text.NRSV: You may, however, take as your booty the women, the children, livestock, and everything else in the town, all its spoil. You may enjoy the spoil of your enemies, which the Lord your God has given you.
text.Rotherham: but the women and the little ones and the cattle and all that shall be in the city—all the spoil thereof, shalt thou take as thy prey,—so shalt thou eat the spoil of thine enemies, whom Yahweh thy God hath delivered unto thee.
text.KJV: 20:14 But the women, and the litle ones, and the cattell, and all that is in the citie, euen all the spoile thereof, shalt thou take vnto thy selfe, and thou shalt eate the spoile of thine enemies, which the Lord thy God hath giuen thee.
text.NABRE: 20:14 but the women and children and livestock and anything else in the city—all its spoil—you may take as plunder for yourselves, and you may enjoy this spoil of your enemies, which the LORD, your God, has given you.

text.NRSV: Thus you shall treat all the towns that are very far from you, which are not towns of the nations here.
text.Rotherham: Thus, shalt thou do unto all the cities that are very far away from thee,—which are not of the cities of those nations.
text.KJV: 20:15 Thus shalt thou doe vnto all the cities which are very far off from thee, which are not of the cities of these nations.
text.NABRE: 20:15 That is how you shall deal with any city at a considerable distance from you, which does not belong to these nations here.

text.NRSV: But as for the towns of these peoples that the Lord your God is giving you as an inheritance, you must not let anything that breathes remain alive.
text.Rotherham: But of the cities of these peoples which Yahweh thy God is giving unto thee as an inheritance, shalt thou not save alive, any breathing thing.
text.KJV: 20:16 But of the cities of these people which the Lord thy God doth giue thee for an inheritance, thou shalt saue aliue nothing that breatheth:
text.NABRE: 20:16 But in the cities of these peoples that the LORD, your God, is giving you as a heritage, you shall not leave a single soul alive.

text.NRSV: You shall annihilate them—the Hittites and the Amorites, the Canaanites and the Perizzites, the Hivites and the Jebusites—just as the Lord your God has commanded,
text.Rotherham: But thou shalt devote them to destruction—the Hittites and the Amorites the Canaanites and the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites,—as Yahweh thy God hath commanded thee:
text.KJV: 20:17 But thou shalt vtterly destroy them, namely, the Hittites, and the Amorites, the Canaanites, and the Perizzites, the Hiuites, and the Iebusites, as the Lord thy God hath commanded thee:
text.NABRE: 20:17 You must put them all under the ban—the Hittites, Amorites, Canaanites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites—just as the LORD, your God, has commanded you,

text.NRSV: so that they may not teach you to do all the abhorrent things that they do for their gods, and you thus sin against the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: lest they teach you to do, according to all their abominations, which they have done unto their gods,—and so ye sin against Yahweh your God.
text.KJV: 20:18 That they teach you not to do after all their abominations, which they haue done vnto their gods, so should ye sinne against the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 20:18 so that they do not teach you to do all the abominations that they do for their gods, and you thus sin against the LORD, your God.

text.NRSV: If you besiege a town for a long time, making war against it in order to take it, you must not destroy its trees by wielding an ax against them. Although you may take food from them, you must not cut them down. Are trees in the field human beings that they should come under siege from you?
text.Rotherham: When thou shalt besiege a city for many days to fight against it, to take it, thou shalt not destroy the trees thereof by wielding against them an axe, when of them, thou mightest eat, them, there-fore shalt thou not cut down,—for, Is the tree of the field, a man, that it should enter, because of thee into the siege?
text.KJV: 20:19 ¶ When thou shalt besiege a citie a long time, in making warre against it to take it, thou shalt not destroy the trees thereof, by forcing an axe against them: for thou mayest eate of them, and thou shalt not cut them downe ( for the tree of the field is mans life) to employ them in the siege.
text.NABRE: 20:19 Trees of a Besieged City. When you are at war with a city and have to lay siege to it for a long time before you capture it, you shall not destroy its trees by putting an ax to them. You may eat of them, but you must not cut them down. Are the trees of the field human beings, that they should be included in your siege?

text.NRSV: You may destroy only the trees that you know do not produce food; you may cut them down for use in building siegeworks against the town that makes war with you, until it falls.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, the tree of which thou knowest that it is, not a tree yielding food, the same, mayest thou destroy, and cut down,—and so build up a siege-work against the city which is making war with thee, until thou hast subdued it.
text.KJV: 20:20 Only the trees which thou knowest that they be not trees for meate, thou shalt destroy, and cut them downe, and thou shalt build bulwarkes against the city that maketh warre with thee, vntil it be subdued.
text.NABRE: 20:20 However, those trees which you know are not fruit trees you may destroy. You may cut them down to build siegeworks against the city that is waging war with you, until it falls.

text.NRSV: If, in the land that the Lord your God is giving you to possess, a body is found lying in open country, and it is not known who struck the person down,
text.Rotherham: When there shall be found one slain on the soil which Yahweh thy God is giving unto thee to possess, lying prostrate in the field,—it not being known who smote him,
text.KJV: 21:1 If one bee found slaine in the lande, which the Lord thy God giueth thee to possesse it, lying in the fielde, and it bee not knowen who hath slaine him:
text.NABRE: 21:1 Chapter 21 - Absolution of Untraced Murder. If the corpse of someone who has been slain is found lying in the open, in the land the LORD, your God, is giving you to possess, and it is not known who killed the person,

text.NRSV: then your elders and your judges shall come out to measure the distances to the towns that are near the body.
text.Rotherham: then shall thine elders and thy judges go forth,—and measure unto the cities that are round about the slain;
text.KJV: 21:2 Then thy Elders and thy Iudges shall come forth, and they shall measure vnto the cities which are round about him that is slaine.
text.NABRE: 21:2 your elders and judges shall go out and measure the distances to the cities that are in the neighborhood of the corpse.

text.NRSV: The elders of the town nearest the body shall take a heifer that has never been worked, one that has not pulled in the yoke;
text.Rotherham: and it shall be that the city that is nearest unto the slain, the elders of that city shall take a heifer of the herd which hath not been wrought with, which hath not drawn in a yoke;
text.KJV: 21:3 And it shall be that the citie which is next vnto the slaine man, euen the Elders of that citie shall take an heifer which hath not bene wrought with, and which hath not drawen in the yoke.
text.NABRE: 21:3 When it is established which city is nearest the corpse, the elders of that city shall take a heifer that has never been put to work or worn a yoke;

text.NRSV: the elders of that town shall bring the heifer down to a wadi with running water, which is neither plowed nor sown, and shall break the heifer’s neck there in the wadi.
text.Rotherham: and the elders of that city shall take down the heifer into a ravine with an everflowing stream, which is neither tilled nor sown,—and shall behead there the heifer in the ravine.
text.KJV: 21:4 And the Elders of that citie shall bring downe the heifer vnto a rough valley, which is neither eared nor sowen, and shall strike off the heifers necke there in the valley.
text.NABRE: 21:4 the elders of that city shall bring the heifer down to a wadi with an everflowing stream at a place that has not been plowed or sown, and shall break the heifer’s neck there in the wadi.

text.NRSV: Then the priests, the sons of Levi, shall come forward, for the Lord your God has chosen them to minister to him and to pronounce blessings in the name of the Lord, and by their decision all cases of dispute and assault shall be settled.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priests the sons of Levi come near, for of them, hath Yahweh thy God made choice to wait upon him, and to bless in the name of Yahweh,—and at their bidding, shall be settled every controversy and every punishment;
text.KJV: 21:5 And the Priests the sonnes of Leui shall come neere (for them the Lord thy God hath chosen to minister vnto him, and to blesse in the Name of the Lord: ) and by their worde shall euery controuersie and euery stroke bee tried.
text.NABRE: 21:5 The priests, the descendants of Levi, shall come forward, for the LORD, your God, has chosen them to minister to him and to bless in the name of the LORD, and every case of dispute or assault shall be for them to decide.

text.NRSV: All the elders of that town nearest the body shall wash their hands over the heifer whose neck was broken in the wadi,
text.Rotherham: and, all the elders of that city who are nearest unto the slain, shall bathe their hands over the heifer that hath been beheaded in the ravine,
text.KJV: 21:6 And all the Elders of that city that are next vnto the slaine man, shal wash their hands ouer the heifer that is beheaded in the valley.
text.NABRE: 21:6 Then all the elders of that city nearest the corpse shall wash their hands over the heifer whose neck was broken in the wadi,

text.NRSV: and they shall declare: “Our hands did not shed this blood, nor were we witnesses to it.
text.Rotherham: and shall respond, and say,—Our hands, shed not this blood, neither did, our eyes, see [the deed] .
text.KJV: 21:7 And they shall answere, and say, Our hands haue not shedde this blood, neither haue our eyes seene it.
text.NABRE: 21:7 and shall declare, “Our hands did not shed this blood, and our eyes did not see the deed.

text.NRSV: Absolve, O Lord, your people Israel, whom you redeemed; do not let the guilt of innocent blood remain in the midst of your people Israel.” Then they will be absolved of bloodguilt.
text.Rotherham: Be propitious unto thy people Israel whom thou hast redeemed, O Yahweh, and do not impute innocent blood in the midst of thy people Israel. So shall they obtain propitiation for the guilt of shedding blood.
text.KJV: 21:8 Be merciful, O Lord, vnto thy people Israel, whom thou hast redeemed, and lay not innocent blood vnto thy people of Israels charge, and the blood shall be forgiuen them.
text.NABRE: 21:8 Absolve, O LORD, your people Israel, whom you have redeemed, and do not let the guilt of shedding innocent blood remain in the midst of your people Israel.” Thus they shall be absolved from the guilt of bloodshed,

text.NRSV: So you shall purge the guilt of innocent blood from your midst, because you must do what is right in the sight of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore shalt consume the guilt of shedding innocent blood out of thy midst,—when thou shalt do that which is right in the eyes of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 21:9 So shalt thou put away the guilt of innocent blood from among you, when thou shalt do that which is right in the sight of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 21:9 and you shall purge the innocent blood from your midst, and do what is right in the eyes of the LORD.

text.NRSV: When you go out to war against your enemies, and the Lord your God hands them over to you and you take them captive,
text.Rotherham: When thou shalt go forth to war against thine enemies,—and Yahweh thy God shall deliver them into thy hand and thou shalt take them captive;
text.KJV: 21:10 ¶ When thou goest forth to warre against thine enemies, and the Lord thy God hath deliuered them into thine hands, & thou hast taken them captiue,
text.NABRE: 21:10 Marriage with a Female Captive. When you go out to war against your enemies and the LORD, your God, delivers them into your power, so that you take captives,

text.NRSV: suppose you see among the captives a beautiful woman whom you desire and want to marry,
text.Rotherham: and shalt see among the captives a woman of beautiful figure,—and shalt have a desire unto her, and wouldest take her to thee to wife,
text.KJV: 21:11 And seest among the captiues a beautifull woman, and hast a desire vnto her, that thou wouldest haue her to thy wife:
text.NABRE: 21:11 if you see a beautiful woman among the captives and become so enamored of her that you wish to have her as a wife,

text.NRSV: and so you bring her home to your house: she shall shave her head, pare her nails,
text.Rotherham: then shalt thou bring her into the midst of thy house,—and she shall shave her head and pare her nails;
text.KJV: 21:12 Then thou shalt bring her home to thine house, and shee shall shaue her head, and pare her nailes.
text.NABRE: 21:12 and so you take her home to your house, she must shave her head, cut her nails,

text.NRSV: discard her captive’s garb, and shall remain in your house a full month, mourning for her father and mother; after that you may go in to her and be her husband, and she shall be your wife.
text.Rotherham: and put away the raiment of her captivity from off her, and shall remain in thy house, and bewail her father and her mother for the space of a month,—and after that, mayest thou go in unto her, and be her husband, and she shall be thy wife.
text.KJV: 21:13 And shee shall put the raiment of her captiuitie from off her, and shall remaine in thine house, and bewaile her father and her mother a full moneth: and after that, thou shalt go in vnto her and be her husband, and she shall be thy wife.
text.NABRE: 21:13 lay aside her captive’s garb, and stay in your house, mourning her father and mother for a full month. After that, you may come to her, and you shall be her husband and she shall be your wife.

text.NRSV: But if you are not satisfied with her, you shall let her go free and not sell her for money. You must not treat her as a slave, since you have dishonored her.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be, if thou hast no pleasure in her, then shalt thou let her go whither she will but thou shalt not sell, her for silver,—thou shalt not make merchandise of her, because thou hast humbled her.
text.KJV: 21:14 And it shall be if thou haue no delight in her, then thou shalt let her goe whither she will, but thou shalt not sell her at al for money, thou shalt not make merchandize of her, because thou hast humbled her.
text.NABRE: 21:14 If later on you lose your liking for her, you shall give her her freedom, if she wishes it; you must not sell her for money. Do not enslave her, since you have violated her.

text.NRSV: If a man has two wives, one of them loved and the other disliked, and if both the loved and the disliked have borne him sons, the firstborn being the son of the one who is disliked,
text.Rotherham: When a man shall have two wives—the one beloved and, the other hated, and they have borne him sons, both she that is beloved, and she that is hated,—and it shall be that the firstborn son belongeth to her that is hated,
text.KJV: 21:15 ¶ If a man haue two wiues, one beloued and another hated, and they haue borne him children, both the beloned, and the hated: and if the first borne sonne be hers that was hated:
text.NABRE: 21:15 Rights of the Firstborn. If a man has two wives, one loved and the other unloved, and if both the loved and the unloved bear him sons, but the firstborn is the son of the unloved wife:

text.NRSV: then on the day when he wills his possessions to his sons, he is not permitted to treat the son of the loved as the firstborn in preference to the son of the disliked, who is the firstborn.
text.Rotherham: then shall it be, that in the day when he maketh his sons to inherit that which he hath, he may not treat the son of the beloved, instead of the son of the hated, as the firstborn;
text.KJV: 21:16 Then it shall be, when he maketh his sonnes to inherite that which hee hath, that he may not make the sonne of the beloued, first borne, before the sonne of the hated, which is indeed the first borne:
text.NABRE: 21:16 when he comes to bequeath his property to his sons he may not consider as his firstborn the son of the wife he loves, in preference to the son of the wife he does not love, the firstborn.

text.NRSV: He must acknowledge as firstborn the son of the one who is disliked, giving him a double portion of all that he has; since he is the first issue of his virility, the right of the firstborn is his.
text.Rotherham: but the true firstborn the son of her that is hated, shall he treat as firstborn by giving him double out of all that is found to be his,—for, he, is the beginning of his strength, his, is the right of the firstborn.
text.KJV: 21:17 But hee shall acknowledge the sonne of the hated for the first borne, by giuing him a double portion of all that hee hath: for hee is the beginning of his strength; the right of the first borne is his.
text.NABRE: 21:17 On the contrary, he shall recognize as his firstborn the son of the unloved wife, giving him a double share of whatever he happens to own, since he is the first fruits of his manhood, and to him belong the rights of the firstborn.

text.NRSV: If someone has a stubborn and rebellious son who will not obey his father and mother, who does not heed them when they discipline him,
text.Rotherham: When a man hath a son, rebellious and insulting, who will not hearken to the voice of his father, and to the voice of his mother,—though they chastise him, yet will not hearken unto them,
text.KJV: 21:18 ¶ If a man haue a stubborne and rebellious sonne, which will not obey the voice of his father, or the voice of his mother, and that when they haue chastened him, wil not hearken vnto them:
text.NABRE: 21:18 The Stubborn and Rebellious Son. If someone has a stubborn and rebellious son who will not listen to his father or mother, and will not listen to them even though they discipline him,

text.NRSV: then his father and his mother shall take hold of him and bring him out to the elders of his town at the gate of that place.
text.Rotherham: then shall his father and his mother, lay hold of him,—and bring him forth unto the elders of his city, and unto the gate of his place;
text.KJV: 21:19 Then shall his father and his mother lay hold on him, and bring him out vnto the Elders of his citie, and vnto the gate of his place:
text.NABRE: 21:19 his father and mother shall take hold of him and bring him out to the elders at the gate of his home city,

text.NRSV: They shall say to the elders of his town, “This son of ours is stubborn and rebellious. He will not obey us. He is a glutton and a drunkard.”
text.Rotherham: and shall say unto the elders of his city: This our son, is rebellious and insulting,—he will not hearken unto our voice,—a glutton and a tippler.
text.KJV: 21:20 And they shall say vnto the Elders of his citie, This our sonne is stubborne, and rebellious, hee will not obey our voice: he is a glutton, & a drunkard.
text.NABRE: 21:20 where they shall say to the elders of the city, “This son of ours is a stubborn and rebellious fellow who will not listen to us; he is a glutton and a drunkard.”

text.NRSV: Then all the men of the town shall stone him to death. So you shall purge the evil from your midst; and all Israel will hear, and be afraid.
text.Rotherham: Then shall all the men of his city stone him with stones that he die, so shalt thou consume the wicked thing out of thy midst,—and, all Israel, shall hear and fear.
text.KJV: 21:21 And all the men of his city shall stone him with stones, that hee die: so shalt thou put euill away from among you, and all Israel shall heare, & feare.
text.NABRE: 21:21 Then all his fellow citizens shall stone him to death. Thus shall you purge the evil from your midst, and all Israel will hear and be afraid.

text.NRSV: When someone is convicted of a crime punishable by death and is executed, and you hang him on a tree,
text.Rotherham: But when there shall be in any man a sin worthy of death and he is to be put to death,—and thou shalt hang him on a tree,
text.KJV: 21:22 ¶ And if a man haue committed a sinne worthy of death, and he be to be put to death, and thou hang him on a tree:
text.NABRE: 21:22 Corpse of a Criminal. If a man guilty of a capital offense is put to death and you hang him on a tree,

text.NRSV: his corpse must not remain all night upon the tree; you shall bury him that same day, for anyone hung on a tree is under God’s curse. You must not defile the land that the Lord your God is giving you for possession.
text.Rotherham: his dead body shall not remain all night upon the tree but thou shalt bury, him on the same day, for a reproach unto God, is he that is hanged,—so shalt thou not make unclean thy soil which Yahweh thy God is giving unto thee for an inheritance.
text.KJV: 21:23 His body shall not remaine all night vpon the tree, but thou shalt in any wise bury him that day: for he that is hanged, is accursed of God: that thy land be not defiled, which the Lord thy God giueth thee for an inheritance.
text.NABRE: 21:23 his corpse shall not remain on the tree overnight. You must bury it the same day; anyone who is hanged is a curse of God. You shall not defile the land which the LORD, your God, is giving you as a heritage.

text.NRSV: You shall not watch your neighbor’s ox or sheep straying away and ignore them; you shall take them back to their owner.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not see the ox of thy brother or his sheep, going astray, and turn away from them,—thou shalt, bring them back unto thy brother.
text.KJV: 22:1 Thou shalt not see thy brothers oxe, or his sheepe go astray, and hide thy selfe from them: thou shalt in any case bring them againe vnto thy brother.
text.NABRE: 22:1 Chapter 22 - Concern for the Neighbor. You shall not see your neighbor’s ox or sheep going astray and ignore it; you must bring it back.

text.NRSV: If the owner does not reside near you or you do not know who the owner is, you shall bring it to your own house, and it shall remain with you until the owner claims it; then you shall return it.
text.Rotherham: And, if thy brother be not nigh unto thee or thou know him not, then shalt thou make room for it within thee own shed and it shall be with thee, until thy brother seek after it, when thou shalt return it unto him.
text.KJV: 22:2 And if thy brother be not nigh vnto thee, or if thou know him not, then thou shalt bring it vnto thine owne house, and it shall be with thee, vntil thy brother seeke after it, and thou shalt restore it to him againe.
text.NABRE: 22:2 If this neighbor does not live near you, or you do not know who the owner may be, take it to your own house and keep it with you until your neighbor claims it; then return it.

text.NRSV: You shall do the same with a neighbor’s donkey; you shall do the same with a neighbor’s garment; and you shall do the same with anything else that your neighbor loses and you find. You may not withhold your help.
text.Rotherham: And, so, shalt thou do with his ass, and, so, shalt thou do with his mantle and, so, shalt thou do with any lost thing of thy brother’s, which shall go astray from him and thou shalt find,—thou mayest not turn away.
text.KJV: 22:3 In like maner shalt thou do with his asse, and so shalt thou doe with his raiment: and with all lost thing of thy brothers which he hath lost, and thou hast found, shalt thou do likewise: thou mayest not hide thy selfe.
text.NABRE: 22:3 You shall do the same with a donkey; you shall do the same with a garment; and you shall do the same with anything else which your neighbor loses and you happen to find. You may not ignore them.

text.NRSV: You shall not see your neighbor’s donkey or ox fallen on the road and ignore it; you shall help to lift it up.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not see the ass of thy brother or his ox fallen in the way, and turn away from them,—thou shalt, raise, them with him.
text.KJV: 22:4 ¶ Thou shalt not see thy brothers asse or his oxe fall downe by the way, and hide thy selfe from them: thou shalt surely helpe him to lift them vp againe.
text.NABRE: 22:4 You shall not see your neighbor’s donkey or ox fallen on the road and ignore it; you must help in lifting it up.

text.NRSV: A woman shall not wear a man’s apparel, nor shall a man put on a woman’s garment; for whoever does such things is abhorrent to the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: A woman shall not have on the wearing apparel of a man, nor shall a man put on the mantle of a woman; for an abomination to Yahweh thy God, is any one who doeth these things.
text.KJV: 22:5 ¶ The woman shall not weare that which pertaineth vnto a man, neither shall a man put on a womans garment: for all that doe so, are abomination vnto the Lord thy God.
text.NABRE: 22:5 Various Precepts. A woman shall not wear a man’s garment, nor shall a man put on a woman’s clothing; for anyone who does such things is an abomination to the LORD, your God.

text.NRSV: If you come on a bird’s nest, in any tree or on the ground, with fledglings or eggs, with the mother sitting on the fledglings or on the eggs, you shall not take the mother with the young.
text.Rotherham: When a bird’s-nest chanceth to be before thee,—in the way, in any tree or upon the ground, whether with nestlings or eggs, and, the mother, be sitting upon the nestlings or upon the eggs, thou shalt not take the mother upon the young;
text.KJV: 22:6 ¶ If a birds nest chance to be before thee in the way in any tree, or on the ground, whether they be yong ones, or egges, and the damme sitting vpon the yong, or vpon the egges, thou shalt not take the damme with the yong.
text.NABRE: 22:6 If, while walking along, you come across a bird’s nest with young birds or eggs in it, in any tree or on the ground, and the mother bird is sitting on them, you shall not take away the mother bird along with her brood.

text.NRSV: Let the mother go, taking only the young for yourself, in order that it may go well with you and you may live long.
text.Rotherham: thou shalt, let go, the mother, and then, her young, mayest thou take for thyself,—that it may go well with thee, and that thou mayest prolong thy days.
text.KJV: 22:7 But thou shalt in any wise let the damme goe, and take the yong to thee, that it may be well with thee, and that thou mayest prolong thy dayes.
text.NABRE: 22:7 You must let the mother go, taking only her brood, in order that you shall prosper and have a long life.

text.NRSV: When you build a new house, you shall make a parapet for your roof; otherwise you might have bloodguilt on your house, if anyone should fall from it.
text.Rotherham: When thou buildest a new house, then shalt thou make a parapet to thy roof, so shalt thou not treasure up blood-guiltiness against thy house, for he that is in danger of falling might fall therefrom.
text.KJV: 22:8 ¶ When thou buildest a new house, then thou shalt make a battlement for thy roofe, that thou bring not blood vpon thine house, if any man fall from thence.
text.NABRE: 22:8 When you build a new house, put a parapet around the roof, so that you do not bring bloodguilt upon your house if someone falls off.

text.NRSV: You shall not sow your vineyard with a second kind of seed, or the whole yield will have to be forfeited, both the crop that you have sown and the yield of the vineyard itself.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not sow thy vineyard with two sorts of seed,—lest the fulness of the seed which thou sowest, and the increase of thy vineyard be profaned.
text.KJV: 22:9 ¶ Thou shalt not sow thy vineyard with diuers seeds: lest the fruit of thy seed which thou hast sowen, and the fruit of thy Uineyard be defiled.
text.NABRE: 22:9 You shall not sow your vineyard with two different kinds of seed, or else its produce shall become forfeit, both the crop you have sown and the yield of the vineyard.

text.NRSV: You shall not plow with an ox and a donkey yoked together.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not plough with an ox and an ass together,
text.KJV: 22:10 ¶ Thou shalt not plow with an oxe and an asse together.
text.NABRE: 22:10 You shall not plow with an ox and a donkey harnessed together.

text.NRSV: You shall not wear clothes made of wool and linen woven together.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not put on linsey-woolsey, of wool and flax together.
text.KJV: 22:11 ¶ Thou shalt not weare a garment of diuers sorts, as of woollen, and linnen together.
text.NABRE: 22:11 You shall not wear cloth made from wool and linen woven together.

text.NRSV: You shall make tassels on the four corners of the cloak with which you cover yourself.
text.Rotherham: Tassels, shalt thou make thee,—on the four corners of thy vesture, wherewith thou dost cover thyself.
text.KJV: 22:12 ¶ Thou shalt make thee fringes vpon the foure quarters of thy vesture, wherewith thou couerest thy selfe.
text.NABRE: 22:12 You shall put tassels on the four corners of the cloak that you wrap around yourself.

text.NRSV: Suppose a man marries a woman, but after going in to her, he dislikes her
text.Rotherham: When a man taketh a wife,—and goeth in unto her and hateth her;
text.KJV: 22:13 ¶ If any man take a wife, and go in vnto her, and hate her,
text.NABRE: 22:13 Marriage Legislation. If a man, after marrying a woman and having relations with her, comes to dislike her,

text.NRSV: and makes up charges against her, slandering her by saying, “I married this woman; but when I lay with her, I did not find evidence of her virginity.”
text.Rotherham: and raiseth against her occasions of speech, and bringeth upon her an evil name, and saith—This woman, I took, and approached her, and found not that she had the tokens of virginity,
text.KJV: 22:14 And giue occasions of speach against her, and bring vp an euill name vpon her, and say, I tooke this woman, and when I came to her, I found her not a mayd:
text.NABRE: 22:14 and accuses her of misconduct and slanders her by saying, “I married this woman, but when I approached her I did not find evidence of her virginity,”

text.NRSV: The father of the young woman and her mother shall then submit the evidence of the young woman’s virginity to the elders of the city at the gate.
text.Rotherham: then shall the father of the damsel and her mother take and bring forth the tokens of the virginity of the damsel, unto the elders of the city, in the gate;
text.KJV: 22:15 Then shal the father of the damosell, and her mother take, and bring forth the tokens of the damosels virginitie, vnto the Elders of the citie in the gate.
text.NABRE: 22:15 the father and mother of the young woman shall take the evidence of her virginity and bring it to the elders at the city gate.

text.NRSV: The father of the young woman shall say to the elders: “I gave my daughter in marriage to this man but he dislikes her;
text.Rotherham: and the father of the damsel shall say unto the elders,—My daughter, gave I unto this man to wife and he hated her;
text.KJV: 22:16 And the damosels father shall say vnto the Elders, I gaue my daughter vnto this man to wife, & he hateth her:
text.NABRE: 22:16 There the father of the young woman shall say to the elders, “I gave my daughter to this man in marriage, but he has come to dislike her,

text.NRSV: now he has made up charges against her, saying, ‘I did not find evidence of your daughter’s virginity.’ But here is the evidence of my daughter’s virginity.” Then they shall spread out the cloth before the elders of the town.
text.Rotherham: and lo! he, hath raised occasions of speech, saying—I found not that thy daughter had the tokens of virginity and yet, these, are the tokens of the virginity of my daughter. And they shall spread out the garment before the elders of the city.
text.KJV: 22:17 And loe, he hath giuen occasions of speech against her, saying, I found not thy daughter a maid: and yet these are the tokens of my daughters virginity; and they shall spread the cloth before the Elders of the citie.
text.NABRE: 22:17 and now accuses her of misconduct, saying: ‘I did not find evidence of your daughter’s virginity.’ But here is the evidence of my daughter’s virginity!” And they shall spread out the cloth before the elders of the city.

text.NRSV: The elders of that town shall take the man and punish him;
text.Rotherham: Then shall the elders of that city take the man, and chastise him;
text.KJV: 22:18 And the Elders of that citie shall take that man, and chastise him.
text.NABRE: 22:18 Then these city elders shall take the man and discipline him,

text.NRSV: they shall fine him one hundred shekels of silver (which they shall give to the young woman’s father) because he has slandered a virgin of Israel. She shall remain his wife; he shall not be permitted to divorce her as long as he lives.
text.Rotherham: and fine him a hundred [shekels] of silver and give unto the father of the damsel, because he hath brought an evil name upon a virgin of Israel, and she shall remain his wife, he may not put her away, all his days.
text.KJV: 22:19 And they shall amearse him in an hundred shekels of siluer, and giue them vnto the father of the damosell, because he hath brought vp an euill name vpon a virgine of Israel: and she shall be his wife, hee may not put her away all his dayes.
text.NABRE: 22:19 and fine him one hundred silver shekels, which they shall give to the young woman’s father, because the man slandered a virgin in Israel. She shall remain his wife, and he may not divorce her as long as he lives.

text.NRSV: If, however, this charge is true, that evidence of the young woman’s virginity was not found,
text.Rotherham: But if this thing be true, and the tokens of virginity be not found with the damsel,
text.KJV: 22:20 But if this thing be true, and the tokens of virginitie be not found for the damosel:
text.NABRE: 22:20 But if this charge is true, and evidence of the young woman’s virginity is not found,

text.NRSV: then they shall bring the young woman out to the entrance of her father’s house and the men of her town shall stone her to death, because she committed a disgraceful act in Israel by prostituting herself in her father’s house. So you shall purge the evil from your midst.
text.Rotherham: then shall they bring forth the damsel into the entrance of her father’s house, and the men of her city shall stone her with stones that she die, because she hath wrought wickedness in Israel, by committing unchastity in her father’s house,—so shalt thou consume the wicked thing out of thy midst.
text.KJV: 22:21 Then they shall bring out the damosell to the doore of her fathers house, and the men of her city shal stone her with stones that she die, because she hath wrought folly in Israel, to play the whore in her fathers house: so shalt thou put euill away from among you.
