text.Rotherham: and all these blessings shall come in upon thee, and reach thee,—because thou dost hearken unto the voice of Yahweh thy God:—
text.KJV: 28:2 And all these blessings shall come on thee, and ouertake thee, if thou shalt hearken vnto the voice of the Lord thy God.
text.NABRE: 28:2 All these blessings will come upon you and overwhelm you when you obey the voice of the LORD, your God:

text.NRSV: Blessed shall you be in the city, and blessed shall you be in the field.
text.Rotherham: Blessed shalt, thou be in the city,—and blessed shalt thou be in the field:
text.KJV: 28:3 Blessed shalt thou bee in the citie, and blessed shalt thou be in the field.
text.NABRE: 28:3 May you be blessed in the city, and blessed in the country!

text.NRSV: Blessed shall be the fruit of your womb, the fruit of your ground, and the fruit of your livestock, both the increase of your cattle and the issue of your flock.
text.Rotherham: Blessed shall be the fruit of thy body, and the fruit of thy ground and the fruit of thy cattle,—the young of thy kine, and the ewes of thy flock:
text.KJV: 28:4 Blessed shall be the fruit of thy body, and the fruit of thy ground, and the fruit of thy cattell, the increase of thy kine, and the flocks of thy sheepe.
text.NABRE: 28:4 Blessed be the fruit of your womb, the produce of your soil and the offspring of your livestock, the issue of your herds and the young of your flocks!

text.NRSV: Blessed shall be your basket and your kneading bowl.
text.Rotherham: Blessed, shall be thy basket and thy kneading-trough:
text.KJV: 28:5 Blessed shall be thy basket and thy store.
text.NABRE: 28:5 Blessed be your grain basket and your kneading bowl!

text.NRSV: Blessed shall you be when you come in, and blessed shall you be when you go out.
text.Rotherham: Blessed, shalt thou be when thou comest in,—and, blessed, shalt thou be when thou goest out:
text.KJV: 28:6 Blessed shalt thou bee when thou commest in, and blessed shalt thou bee when thou goest out.
text.NABRE: 28:6 May you be blessed in your coming in, and blessed in your going out!

text.NRSV: The Lord will cause your enemies who rise against you to be defeated before you; they shall come out against you one way, and flee before you seven ways.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will deliver thine enemies who rise up against thee, to be routed before thee,—one way, shall they come out against thee, and seven ways, shall they flee before thee:
text.KJV: 28:7 The Lord shall cause thine enemies that rise vp against thee, to bee smitten before thy face: they shall come out against thee one way, and flee before thee seuen wayes.
text.NABRE: 28:7 Victory and Prosperity. The LORD will beat down before you the enemies that rise up against you; they will come out against you from one direction, and flee before you in seven.

text.NRSV: The Lord will command the blessing upon you in your barns, and in all that you undertake; he will bless you in the land that the Lord your God is giving you.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will command to be with thee the blessing, in thy storehouses and in all whereunto thou settest thy hand,—and will bless thee in the land which Yahweh thy God is giving unto thee:
text.KJV: 28:8 The Lord shall command the blessing vpon thee in thy store-houses, and in all that thou settest thine hand vnto, and he shall blesse thee in the land which the Lord thy God giueth thee.
text.NABRE: 28:8 The LORD will affirm the blessing upon you, on your barns and on all your undertakings; he will bless you in the land that the LORD, your God, is giving you.

text.NRSV: The Lord will establish you as his holy people, as he has sworn to you, if you keep the commandments of the Lord your God and walk in his ways.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will confirm thee unto himself for a holy people, as he sware unto thee,—because thou dost keep the commandments of Yahweh thy God, and dost walk in his ways.
text.KJV: 28:9 The Lord shall establish thee an holy people vnto himselfe, as hee hath sworne vnto thee, if thou shalt keepe the Commaundements of the Lord thy God, and walke in his wayes.
text.NABRE: 28:9 The LORD will establish you as a holy people, as he swore to you, if you keep the commandments of the LORD, your God, and walk in his ways.

text.NRSV: All the peoples of the earth shall see that you are called by the name of the Lord, and they shall be afraid of you.
text.Rotherham: And all the peoples of the earth shall see that, the name of Yahweh, hath been called upon thee,—and shall be afraid of thee.
text.KJV: 28:10 And all people of the earth shall see, that thou art called by the Name of the Lord, and they shall bee afraid of thee.
text.NABRE: 28:10 All the peoples of the earth will see that the name of the LORD is proclaimed over you, and they will be afraid of you.

text.NRSV: The Lord will make you abound in prosperity, in the fruit of your womb, in the fruit of your livestock, and in the fruit of your ground in the land that the Lord swore to your ancestors to give you.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh will cause thee to abound in that which is good, in the fruit of thy body, and in the fruit of thy cattle and in the fruit of thy soil,—upon the soil which Yahweh sware unto thy fathers, to give unto thee:
text.KJV: 28:11 And the Lord shal make thee plenteous in goods, in the fruit of thy body, and in the fruit of thy cattell, and in the fruit of thy ground, in the land which the Lord sware vnto thy fathers to giue thee.
text.NABRE: 28:11 The LORD will generously increase the fruit of your womb, the offspring of your livestock, and the produce of your soil, upon the land which the LORD swore to your ancestors he would give you.

text.NRSV: The Lord will open for you his rich storehouse, the heavens, to give the rain of your land in its season and to bless all your undertakings. You will lend to many nations, but you will not borrow.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will open unto thee his rich storehouse—the heavens, to give the rain of thy land in its season, and to bless every work of thy hand, so shalt thou lend unto many nations, but thou, shalt not borrow:
text.KJV: 28:12 The Lord shal open vnto thee his good treasure, the heauen to giue the raine vnto thy land in his season, and to blesse all the worke of thine hand: and thou shalt lend vnto many nations, and thou shalt not borrow.
text.NABRE: 28:12 The LORD will open up for you his rich storehouse, the heavens, to give your land rain in due season and to bless all the works of your hands. You will lend to many nations but borrow from none.

text.NRSV: The Lord will make you the head, and not the tail; you shall be only at the top, and not at the bottom—if you obey the commandments of the Lord your God, which I am commanding you today, by diligently observing them,
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh, will give thee, to be the head, and not the tail, and thou shalt be only above, and shalt not be beneath,—because thou dost hearken unto the commandments of Yahweh thy God, which I am commanding thee, to-day, to observe and to do;
text.KJV: 28:13 And the Lord shall make thee the head, and not the taile, and thou shalt be aboue onely, and thou shalt not be beneath: if that thou hearken vnto the Commandements of the Lord thy God, which I command thee this day, to obserue, and to doe them:
text.NABRE: 28:13 The LORD will make you the head not the tail, the top not the bottom, if you obey the commandments of the LORD, your God, which I am giving you today, observing them carefully,

text.NRSV: and if you do not turn aside from any of the words that I am commanding you today, either to the right or to the left, following other gods to serve them.
text.Rotherham: and dost not turn side from any of the words which I am commanding you to-day, to the right hand or to the left,—to go after other gods, to serve them,
text.KJV: 28:14 And thou shalt not go aside from any of the wordes which I command thee this day, to the right hand, or to the left, to goe after other gods, to serue them.
text.NABRE: 28:14 not turning aside, either to the right or to the left, from any of the words which I am giving you today, following other gods and serving them.

text.NRSV: But if you will not obey the Lord your God by diligently observing all his commandments and decrees, which I am commanding you today, then all these curses shall come upon you and overtake you:
text.Rotherham: But it shall be, if thou do not hearken unto the voice of Yahweh thy God, to observe to do all his commandments and his statutes which I am commanding thee to-day, then shall come in upon thee all these curses and shall reach thee:—
text.KJV: 28:15 ¶ But it shal come to passe, if thou wilt not hearken vnto the voyce of the Lord thy God, to obserue to doe all his Commandements and his Statutes, which I command thee this day, that all these curses shall come vpon thee, and ouertake thee.
text.NABRE: 28:15 Curses for Disobedience. But if you do not obey the voice of the LORD, your God, carefully observing all his commandments and statutes which I give you today, all these curses shall come upon you and overwhelm you:

text.NRSV: Cursed shall you be in the city, and cursed shall you be in the field.
text.Rotherham: Cursed, shalt thou be in the city,—and cursed, shalt thou be in the field:
text.KJV: 28:16 Cursed shalt thou be in the city, and cursed shalt thou be in the field.
text.NABRE: 28:16 May you be cursed in the city, and cursed in the country!

text.NRSV: Cursed shall be your basket and your kneading bowl.
text.Rotherham: Cursed, shall be thy basket, and thy kneading-trough:
text.KJV: 28:17 Cursed shall be thy basket and thy store.
text.NABRE: 28:17 Cursed be your grain basket and your kneading bowl!

text.NRSV: Cursed shall be the fruit of your womb, the fruit of your ground, the increase of your cattle and the issue of your flock.
text.Rotherham: Cursed, shall be the fruit of thy body, and the fruit of thy ground—the young of thy kine and the ewes of thy flock:
text.KJV: 28:18 Cursed shalbe the fruit of thy body, and the fruit of thy land, the increase of thy kine, and the flocks of thy sheepe.
text.NABRE: 28:18 Cursed be the fruit of your womb, the produce of your soil and the offspring of your livestock, the issue of your herds and the young of your flocks!

text.NRSV: Cursed shall you be when you come in, and cursed shall you be when you go out.
text.Rotherham: Cursed, shalt thou be, when thou comest in,—and, cursed, shalt thou be, when thou goest out:
text.KJV: 28:19 Cursed shalt thou bee when thou commest in, and cursed shalt thou bee when thou goest out.
text.NABRE: 28:19 May you be cursed in your coming in, and cursed in your going out!

text.NRSV: The Lord will send upon you disaster, panic, and frustration in everything you attempt to do, until you are destroyed and perish quickly, on account of the evil of your deeds, because you have forsaken me.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will send upon thee cursing, confusion and rebuke, in all whereunto thou settest thy hand that thou mayest do it,—until thou be destroyed and until thou perish quickly, because of the wickedness of thy doings, whereby thou hast forsaken me.
text.KJV: 28:20 The Lord shall send vpon thee cursing, vexation, and rebuke, in all that thou settest thine hand vnto, for to doe, vntill thou be destroyed, and vntill thou perish quickely, because of the wickednesse of thy doings, whereby thou hast forsaken me.
text.NABRE: 28:20 Sickness and Defeat. The LORD will send on you a curse, panic, and frustration in everything you set your hand to, until you are speedily destroyed and perish for the evil you have done in forsaking me.

text.NRSV: The Lord will make the pestilence cling to you until it has consumed you off the land that you are entering to possess.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will cause to cleave unto thee—the pestilence,—until he hath consumed thee from off the soil which thou art entering to possess.
text.KJV: 28:21 The Lord shall make the pestilence cleaue vnto thee, vntill he haue consumed thee from off the land, whither thou goest to possesse it.
text.NABRE: 28:21 The LORD will make disease cling to you until he has made an end of you from the land you are entering to possess.

text.NRSV: The Lord will afflict you with consumption, fever, inflammation, with fiery heat and drought, and with blight and mildew; they shall pursue you until you perish.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, will smite thee, with consumption and with fever, and with inflammation and with violent heat and with the sword, and with blight and with mildew,—and they shall pursue thee, until thou perish.
text.KJV: 28:22 The Lord shall smite thee with a consumption, and with a feuer, and with an inflammation, & with an extreme burning, and with the sword, and with blasting, and with mildewe: and they shall pursue thee vntill thou perish.
text.NABRE: 28:22 The LORD will strike you with consumption, fever, and inflammation, with fiery heat and drought, with blight and mildew, that will pursue you until you perish.

text.NRSV: The sky over your head shall be bronze, and the earth under you iron.
text.Rotherham: And thy heavens which are over thy head shall become bronze,—and the earth which is under thee iron.
text.KJV: 28:23 And the heauen that is ouer thy head shall be brasse, and the earth that is vnder thee shall be yron.
text.NABRE: 28:23 The heavens over your heads will be like bronze and the earth under your feet like iron.

text.NRSV: The Lord will change the rain of your land into powder, and only dust shall come down upon you from the sky until you are destroyed.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will cause the rain of thy land to be powder and dust,—out of the heavens, shall it come down upon thee, until thou be destroyed.
text.KJV: 28:24 The Lord shall make the raine of thy land powder & dust: from heauen shall it come downe vpon thee, vntill thou be destroyed.
text.NABRE: 28:24 For rain the LORD will give your land powdery dust, which will come down upon you from the heavens until you are destroyed.

text.NRSV: The Lord will cause you to be defeated before your enemies; you shall go out against them one way and flee before them seven ways. You shall become an object of horror to all the kingdoms of the earth.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will give thee up to be routed before thine enemies, one way, shalt thou go out against them, and, seven ways, shalt thou flee before them,—and thou shalt become a terror unto all the kingdoms of the earth.
text.KJV: 28:25 The Lord shall cause thee to be smitten before thine enemies: thou shalt go out one way against them, and flee seuen wayes before them, and shalt be remoued into all the kingdomes of the earth.
text.NABRE: 28:25 The LORD will let you be beaten down before your enemies; though you advance against them from one direction, you will flee before them in seven, so that you will become an object of horror to all the kingdoms of the earth.

text.NRSV: Your corpses shall be food for every bird of the air and animal of the earth, and there shall be no one to frighten them away.
text.Rotherham: And thy dead body shall become food for every bird of the heavens, and for the beast of the earth,—with none to fright them away.
text.KJV: 28:26 And thy carkeise shalbe meat vnto all foules of the aire, and vnto the beasts of the earth, and no man shall fray them away.
text.NABRE: 28:26 Your corpses will become food for all the birds of the air and for the beasts of the field, with no one to frighten them off.

text.NRSV: The Lord will afflict you with the boils of Egypt, with ulcers, scurvy, and itch, of which you cannot be healed.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, will smite thee, with the burning sores of Egypt and with the hemorrhoids, and with scab, and with itch,—of which thou canst not be healed.
text.KJV: 28:27 The Lord wil smite thee with the botch of Egypt, and with the emerods, and with the scabbe, and with the itch, whereof thou canst not bee healed.
text.NABRE: 28:27 The LORD will strike you with Egyptian boils and with tumors, skin diseases and the itch, from none of which you can be cured.

text.NRSV: The Lord will afflict you with madness, blindness, and confusion of mind;
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, will smite thee, with madness and with blindness,—and with terror of heart;
text.KJV: 28:28 The Lord shall smite thee with madnesse, and blindnesse, and astonishment of heart.
text.NABRE: 28:28 And the LORD will strike you with madness, blindness and panic,

text.NRSV: you shall grope about at noon as blind people grope in darkness, but you shall be unable to find your way; and you shall be continually abused and robbed, without anyone to help.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt he groping about in noonday brightness, as the blind man gropeth in thick darkness, and thou shalt not make thy ways prosper,—but shalt be only oppressed and spoiled all the days with none to save.
text.KJV: 28:29 And thou shalt grope at noone dayes, as the blind gropeth in darknes, and thou shalt not prosper in thy waies: and thou shalt be onely oppressed, and spoiled euermore, and no man shal saue thee.
text.NABRE: 28:29 so that even at midday you will grope in the dark as though blind, unable to find your way. Despoilment. You will be oppressed and robbed continually, with no one to come to your aid.

text.NRSV: You shall become engaged to a woman, but another man shall lie with her. You shall build a house, but not live in it. You shall plant a vineyard, but not enjoy its fruit.
text.Rotherham: A wife, shalt thou betroth, and, another man shall lie with her. A house, shalt thou build, and shalt not dwell therein,—A vineyard, shalt thou plant, and shalt not throw it open;
text.KJV: 28:30 Thou shalt betrothe a wife, and another man shall lie with her: thou shalt build an house, and thou shalt not dwell therein: thou shalt plant a vineyard, and shalt not gather the grapes thereof.
text.NABRE: 28:30 Though you betroth a wife, another will have her. Though you build a house, you will not live in it. Though you plant a vineyard, you will not pluck its fruits.

text.NRSV: Your ox shall be butchered before your eyes, but you shall not eat of it. Your donkey shall be stolen in front of you, and shall not be restored to you. Your sheep shall be given to your enemies, without anyone to help you.
text.Rotherham: Thine ox slaughtered before thine eyes, and thou shalt not eat thereof, Thine ass stolen from before thee, and shall not be restored to thee,—Thy flock given to thine enemies, and thou shalt have none to save.
text.KJV: 28:31 Thine oxe shall be slaine before thine eyes, and thou shalt not eat thereof: thine asse shall be violently taken away from before thy face, and shal not be restored to thee: thy sheepe shall bee giuen vnto thine enemies, and thou shalt haue none to rescue them.
text.NABRE: 28:31 Your ox will be slaughtered before your eyes, but you will not eat its flesh. Your donkey will be stolen in your presence, but you will never get it back. Your flocks will be given to your enemies, with no one to come to your aid.

text.NRSV: Your sons and daughters shall be given to another people, while you look on; you will strain your eyes looking for them all day but be powerless to do anything.
text.Rotherham: Thy sons and thy daughters given to another people, thine eyes looking on, and failing for them all the day, thine own hand being powerless.
text.KJV: 28:32 Thy sonnes, and thy daughters shall be giuen vnto another people, and thine eyes shal looke, and faile with longing for them al the day long: and there shall be no might in thine hand.
text.NABRE: 28:32 Your sons and daughters will be given to another people while you strain your eyes looking for them every day, having no power to do anything.

text.NRSV: A people whom you do not know shall eat up the fruit of your ground and of all your labors; you shall be continually abused and crushed,
text.Rotherham: The fruit of thy soil, and all thy toilsome produce, shall a people whom thou knowest not, eat up,—and thou shalt be only oppressed and crushed, all the days;
text.KJV: 28:33 The fruit of thy land, and all thy labours, shall a nation which thou knowest not, eate vp: and thou shalt be onely oppressed and crushed alway:
text.NABRE: 28:33 A people you do not know will consume the fruit of your soil and of all your labor, and you will be thoroughly oppressed and continually crushed,

text.NRSV: and driven mad by the sight that your eyes shall see.
text.Rotherham: so that thou shalt be mad,—for the sight of thine eyes which thou shalt see.
text.KJV: 28:34 So that thou shalt bee mad, for the sight of thine eyes which thou shalt see.
text.NABRE: 28:34 until you are driven mad by what your eyes must look upon.

text.NRSV: The Lord will strike you on the knees and on the legs with grievous boils of which you cannot be healed, from the sole of your foot to the crown of your head.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, will smite thee, with a grievous boil, upon the knees and upon the legs, of which thou canst not be healed,—from the sole of thy foot even unto the crown of thy head.
text.KJV: 28:35 The Lord shall smite thee in the knees, and in the legges with a sore botch that cannot bee healed, from the sole of thy foot, vnto the top of thy head.
text.NABRE: 28:35 The LORD will strike you with malignant boils of which you cannot be cured, on your knees and legs, and from the soles of your feet to the crown of your head.

text.NRSV: The Lord will bring you, and the king whom you set over you, to a nation that neither you nor your ancestors have known, where you shall serve other gods, of wood and stone.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will bring thee and thy king whom thou wilt set up over thee, unto a nation which thou hast not known, thou nor thy fathers,—and thou shalt serve there other gods of wood and of stone.
text.KJV: 28:36 The Lord shal bring thee, and thy king which thou shalt set ouer thee, vnto a nation which neither thou, nor thy fathers haue knowen, and there shalt thou serue other gods, wood and stone.
text.NABRE: 28:36 Exile. The LORD will bring you, and your king whom you have set over you, to a nation which you and your ancestors have not known, and there you will serve other gods, of wood and stone,

text.NRSV: You shall become an object of horror, a proverb, and a byword among all the peoples where the Lord will lead you.
text.Rotherham: Thus shalt thou become a horror, a byword, and a mockery, among all the peoples whither Yahweh thy God will drive thee.
text.KJV: 28:37 And thou shalt become an astonishment, a prouerbe, and a by-worde, among all nations whither the Lord shall leade thee.
text.NABRE: 28:37 and you will be a horror, a byword, a taunt among all the peoples to which the LORD will drive you.

text.NRSV: You shall carry much seed into the field but shall gather little in, for the locust shall consume it.
text.Rotherham: Much seed, shalt thou take out into the field,—and little, shalt thou gather in, for the locust shall consume it.
text.KJV: 28:38 Thou shalt carie much seede out into the field, and shalt gather but litle in: for the locust shall consume it.
text.NABRE: 28:38 Fruitless Labors. Though you take out seed to your field, you will harvest but little, for the locusts will devour it.

text.NRSV: You shall plant vineyards and dress them, but you shall neither drink the wine nor gather the grapes, for the worm shall eat them.
text.Rotherham: Vineyards, shalt thou plant, and dress,—but wine, shalt thou not drink, neither shalt thou gather the grapes, for the worm shall eat them.
text.KJV: 28:39 Thou shalt plant vineyards and dresse them, but shalt neither drinke of the wine, nor gather the grapes: for the wormes shall eate them.
text.NABRE: 28:39 Though you plant and cultivate vineyards, you will not drink or store up the wine, for the worms will eat them.

text.NRSV: You shall have olive trees throughout all your territory, but you shall not anoint yourself with the oil, for your olives shall drop off.
text.Rotherham: Olive trees, shalt thou have in all thy bounds,—but with oil, shalt thou not anoint thyself, for thine olives, shall drop off.
text.KJV: 28:40 Thou shalt haue Oliue trees throughout al thy coasts, but thou shalt not anoint thy selfe with the oyle: for thine Oliue shall cast his fruit.
text.NABRE: 28:40 Though you have olive trees throughout your country, you will have no oil for ointment, for your olives will drop off.

text.NRSV: You shall have sons and daughters, but they shall not remain yours, for they shall go into captivity.
text.Rotherham: Sons and daughters, shalt thou beget, and they shall not be thine, for they shall go into captivity.
text.KJV: 28:41 Thou shalt beget sonnes and daughters, but thou shalt not enioy them: for they shall goe into captiuitie.
text.NABRE: 28:41 Though you beget sons and daughters, they will not remain with you, for they will go into captivity.

text.NRSV: All your trees and the fruit of your ground the cicada shall take over.
text.Rotherham: All thy trees, and the fruit of thy ground, shall the grasshopper, devour.
text.KJV: 28:42 All thy trees and fruit of thy land shall the locusts consume.
text.NABRE: 28:42 Buzzing insects will take possession of all your trees and the crops of your soil.

text.NRSV: Aliens residing among you shall ascend above you higher and higher, while you shall descend lower and lower.
text.Rotherham: The sojourner who is in thy midst, shall mount up above thee higher and higher,—whereas, thou, shalt come down lower and lower:
text.KJV: 28:43 The stranger that is within thee shall get vp aboue thee very high: and thou shalt come downe very low.
text.NABRE: 28:43 The resident aliens among you will rise above you higher and higher, while you sink lower and lower.

text.NRSV: They shall lend to you but you shall not lend to them; they shall be the head and you shall be the tail.
text.Rotherham: he, shall lend to thee, but, thou, shalt not lend to him,—he, shall become head, and, thou, shalt become tail.
text.KJV: 28:44 He shall lend to thee, and thou shalt not lend to him: he shall bee the head, and thou shalt be the taile.
text.NABRE: 28:44 They will lend to you, not you to them. They will become the head, you the tail.

text.NRSV: All these curses shall come upon you, pursuing and overtaking you until you are destroyed, because you did not obey the Lord your God, by observing the commandments and the decrees that he commanded you.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, all these curses, shall come in upon thee, and pursue thee and overtake thee, until thou be destroyed,—because thou didst not hearken unto the voice of Yahweh thy God, to keep his commandments and his statutes which he hath commanded thee;
text.KJV: 28:45 Moreouer, all these curses shall come vpon thee, and shall pursue thee, and ouertake thee, til thou be destroied: because thou hearkenedst not vnto the voice of the Lord thy God, to keepe his Commandements, and his Statutes which he commanded thee.
text.NABRE: 28:45 All these curses will come upon you, pursuing you and overwhelming you, until you are destroyed, because you would not obey the voice of the LORD, your God, by keeping his commandments and statutes which he gave you.

text.NRSV: They shall be among you and your descendants as a sign and a portent forever.
text.Rotherham: and they shall be upon thee, for a sign, and for a wonder, and upon thy seed, unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: 28:46 And they shall be vpon thee for a signe, and for a wonder, and vpon thy seed for euer:
text.NABRE: 28:46 They will be a sign and a wonder for you and your descendants for all time.

text.NRSV: Because you did not serve the Lord your God joyfully and with gladness of heart for the abundance of everything,
text.Rotherham: Because thou servedst not Yahweh thy God, with rejoicing, and with gladness of heart, for abundance of all things,
text.KJV: 28:47 Because thou seruedst not the Lord thy God with ioyfulnesse, and with gladnesse of heart, for the aboundance of all things.
text.NABRE: 28:47 Since you would not serve the LORD, your God, with heartfelt joy for abundance of every kind,

text.NRSV: therefore you shall serve your enemies whom the Lord will send against you, in hunger and thirst, in nakedness and lack of everything. He will put an iron yoke on your neck until he has destroyed you.
text.Rotherham: therefore shalt thou serve thine enemies, whom Yahweh will send against thee, with hunger and with thirst and with nakedness, and with want of all things,—and he, will put a yoke of iron upon thy neck, until he hath destroyed thee.
text.KJV: 28:48 Therefore shalt thou serue thine enemies, which the Lord shall send against thee, in hunger, and in thirst, and in nakednesse, and in want of all things: and he shall put a yoke of iron vpon thy necke, vntill he haue destroyed thee.
text.NABRE: 28:48 in hunger and thirst, in nakedness and utter want, you will serve the enemies whom the LORD will send against you. He will put an iron yoke on your neck, until he destroys you.

text.NRSV: The Lord will bring a nation from far away, from the end of the earth, to swoop down on you like an eagle, a nation whose language you do not understand,
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will bring against thee a nation from afar, from the end of the earth, as darteth a bird of prey,—a nation whose tongue thou canst not understand;
text.KJV: 28:49 The Lord shall bring a nation against thee from farre, from the end of the earth, as swift as the Eagle fleeth, a nation whose tongue thou shalt not vnderstand:
text.NABRE: 28:49 Invasion and Siege. The LORD will raise up against you a nation from afar, from the end of the earth, that swoops down like an eagle, a nation whose language you do not understand,

text.NRSV: a grim-faced nation showing no respect to the old or favor to the young.
text.Rotherham: a nation of fierce countenance,—who wilt not respect an elder nor to the young, show favour;
text.KJV: 28:50 A nation of fierce countenance, which shal not regard the person of the old, nor shew fauour to the yong:
text.NABRE: 28:50 a nation of fierce appearance, that shows neither respect for the aged nor mercy for the young.

text.NRSV: It shall consume the fruit of your livestock and the fruit of your ground until you are destroyed, leaving you neither grain, wine, and oil, nor the increase of your cattle and the issue of your flock, until it has made you perish.
text.Rotherham: then shall he eat the young of thy cattle and the fruit of thy ground until thou art destroyed, who will not leave for thee corn, new wine or oil, the young of thy kine, or the ewes of thy flock,—until he hath caused thee to perish.
text.KJV: 28:51 And hee shall eat the fruit of thy cattell, and the fruit of thy land, vntill thou be destroyed: which also shall not leaue thee either corne, wine, or oyle, or the increase of thy kine, or flockes of thy sheepe, vntill he haue destroyed thee.
text.NABRE: 28:51 They will consume the offspring of your livestock and the produce of your soil, until you are destroyed; they will leave you no grain or wine or oil, no issue of herd, no young of flock, until they have brought about your ruin.

text.NRSV: It shall besiege you in all your towns until your high and fortified walls, in which you trusted, come down throughout your land; it shall besiege you in all your towns throughout the land that the Lord your God has given you.
text.Rotherham: And he shall lay siege to thee in all thy gates, until thy high and fortified walls come down wherein thou wast trusting, in all thy land,—yea he will lay siege to thee in all thy gates, in all thy land which Yahweh thy God, hath given, unto thee.
text.KJV: 28:52 And he shall besiege thee in all thy gates, vntill thy high and fenced walles come downe wherein thou trustedst throughout all thy land: and hee shall besiege thee in all thy gates, throughout all thy land which the Lord thy God hath giuen thee.
text.NABRE: 28:52 They will besiege you in each of your communities, until the great, fortified walls, in which you trust, come tumbling down all over your land. They will besiege you in every community throughout the land which the LORD, your God, has given you,

text.NRSV: In the desperate straits to which the enemy siege reduces you, you will eat the fruit of your womb, the flesh of your own sons and daughters whom the Lord your God has given you.
text.Rotherham: And thou wilt eat the fruit of thy body, the flesh of thy sons and of thy daughters, whom Yahweh thy God hath given unto thee—in the siege and in the straitness wherewith thine enemy will straiten thee.
text.KJV: 28:53 And thou shalt eate the fruit of thine owne body, the flesh of thy sonnes, and of thy daughters (which the Lord thy God hath giuen thee) in the siege, and in the straitnesse wherewith thine enemies shall distresse thee.
text.NABRE: 28:53 and because of the siege and the distress to which your enemy subjects you, you will eat the fruit of your womb, the flesh of your own sons and daughters whom the LORD, your God, has given you.

text.NRSV: Even the most refined and gentle of men among you will begrudge food to his own brother, to the wife whom he embraces, and to the last of his remaining children,
text.Rotherham: The man that is tender among you, and exceedingly delicate, his eye will be jealous of his brother and of the wife of his bosom, and of the remnant of his sons, whom be might leave behind;
text.KJV: 28:54 So that the man that is tender among you, and very delicate, his eye shalbe euill toward his brother, and toward the wife of his bosome, and towards the remnant of his children which he shall leaue:
text.NABRE: 28:54 The most refined and fastidious man among you will begrudge his brother and his beloved wife and his surviving children,

text.NRSV: giving to none of them any of the flesh of his children whom he is eating, because nothing else remains to him, in the desperate straits to which the enemy siege will reduce you in all your towns.
text.Rotherham: so that he will not give to any one of them, of the flesh of his sons which he will eat, because he hath nothing at all left him,—in the siege and in the straitness wherewith thine enemy will straiten thee within all thy gates.
text.KJV: 28:55 So that he wil not giue to any of them of the flesh of his children whom he shall eate: because hee hath nothing left him in the siege, and in the straitnesse wherewith thine enemies shal distresse thee, in all thy gates.
text.NABRE: 28:55 any share in the flesh of his children that he himself is using for food because nothing else is left him—such the siege and distress to which your enemy will subject you in all your communities.

text.NRSV: She who is the most refined and gentle among you, so gentle and refined that she does not venture to set the sole of her foot on the ground, will begrudge food to the husband whom she embraces, to her own son, and to her own daughter,
text.Rotherham: The tender and delicate woman among you who hath never adventured the sole of her foot to set it upon the ground, through delicateness and through tenderness, her eye shall be jealous of the husband of her bosom, and of her own son, and of her own daughter;
text.KJV: 28:56 The tender and delicate woman among you, which would not aduenture to set the sole of her foote vpon the ground, for delicatenesse and tendernesse, her eye shall be euill towards the husband of her bosome, and towards her sonne, and towards her daughter,
text.NABRE: 28:56 The most fastidious woman among you, who would not venture to set the sole of her foot on the ground, so refined and fastidious is she, will begrudge her beloved husband and her son and daughter

text.NRSV: begrudging even the afterbirth that comes out from between her thighs, and the children that she bears, because she is eating them in secret for lack of anything else, in the desperate straits to which the enemy siege will reduce you in your towns.
text.Rotherham: both as to her afterbirth that cometh forth from between her feet and as to her children which she shall bear, for she will eat them for want of all things secretly,—in the siege and in the straitness wherewith thine enemy will straiten thee within thine own gates.
text.KJV: 28:57 And towards her yong one that commeth out from betweene her feete, and towards her children which shee shall beare: for shee shall eate them for want of all things secretly in the siege and straitnes, wherewith thine enemie shall distresse thee in thy gates.
text.NABRE: 28:57 the afterbirth that issues from her womb and the infants she brings forth because she secretly eats them for want of anything else—such the siege and distress to which your enemy will subject you in your communities.

text.NRSV: If you do not diligently observe all the words of this law that are written in this book, fearing this glorious and awesome name, the Lord your God,
text.Rotherham: If thou wilt not take heed to do all the words of this law, which are written in this scroll,—to revere this glorious and reverend name, Yahweh thy God,
text.KJV: 28:58 If thou wilt not obserue to doe all the wordes of this Law that are written in this booke, that thou mayest feare this glorious and fearefull Name, The Lord thy God:
text.NABRE: 28:58 Plagues. If you are not careful to observe all the words of this law which is written in this book, and to fear this glorious and awesome name, the LORD, your God,

text.NRSV: then the Lord will overwhelm both you and your offspring with severe and lasting afflictions and grievous and lasting maladies.
text.Rotherham: then will Yahweh make thy plagues wonderful, and the plagues of thy seed,—plagues great and lasting, and diseases grievous and lasting;
text.KJV: 28:59 Then the Lord wil make thy plagues wonderfull, and the plagues of thy seed, euen great plagues, and of long continuance, and sore sicknesses, and of long continuance.
text.NABRE: 28:59 the LORD will bring upon you and your descendants wondrous calamities, severe and constant calamities, and malignant and constant sicknesses.

text.NRSV: He will bring back upon you all the diseases of Egypt, of which you were in dread, and they shall cling to you.
text.Rotherham: and he will bring back on thee all the sickness of Egypt, because of which thou wast afraid,—and they shall cleave unto thee;
text.KJV: 28:60 Moreouer, hee will bring vpon thee all the diseases of Egypt, which thou wast afraid of, and they shal cleaue vnto thee.
text.NABRE: 28:60 He will bring back upon you all the diseases of Egypt which you dread, and they will cling to you.

text.NRSV: Every other malady and affliction, even though not recorded in the book of this law, the Lord will inflict on you until you are destroyed.
text.Rotherham: even every disease and, every plague, which are not written in this scroll of the law, will Yahweh bring up against thee, until thou art destroyed,
text.KJV: 28:61 Also euery sickenesse, and euery plague which is not written in the booke of this Law, them will the Lord bring vpon thee, vntill thou be destroyed.
text.NABRE: 28:61 Even any sickness or calamity not written in this book of the law, that too the LORD will bring upon you until you are destroyed.

text.NRSV: Although once you were as numerous as the stars in heaven, you shall be left few in number, because you did not obey the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: And ye shall be left men few in number, whereas ye had become as the stars of the heavens for multitude,—because thou hast not hearkened unto the voice of Yahweh thy God.
text.KJV: 28:62 And ye shall be left few in number, whereas ye were as the starres of heauen for multitude: because thou wouldest not obey the voyce of the Lord thy God.
text.NABRE: 28:62 You who were numerous as the stars of the heavens will be left few in number, because you would not obey the voice of the LORD, your God.

text.NRSV: And just as the Lord took delight in making you prosperous and numerous, so the Lord will take delight in bringing you to ruin and destruction; you shall be plucked off the land that you are entering to possess.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass that as Yahweh rejoiced over you to do you good and to multiply you, so, will Yahweh rejoice over you, to cause you to perish and to destroy you,—and ye shall be torn away, from off the soil, whither thou art going in to possess it;
text.KJV: 28:63 And it shall come to passe, that as the Lord reioyced ouer you to doe you good, and to multiply you; so the Lord will reioyce ouer you to destroy you, and to bring you to nought; and ye shalbe plucked from off the land whither thou goest to possesse it.
text.NABRE: 28:63 Exile. Just as the LORD once took delight in making you prosper and grow, so will the LORD now take delight in ruining and destroying you, and you will be plucked out of the land you are now entering to possess.

text.NRSV: The Lord will scatter you among all peoples, from one end of the earth to the other; and there you shall serve other gods, of wood and stone, which neither you nor your ancestors have known.
text.Rotherham: and Yahweh will scatter thee among all the peoples, from one end of the earth even unto the other end of the earth,—and thou wilt serve there other gods whom thou hast not known—thou nor thy fathers,—of wood and of stone.
text.KJV: 28:64 And the Lord shall scatter thee among all people, from the one end of the earth, euen vnto the other: and there thou shalt serue other gods, which neither thou nor thy fathers haue knowen, euen wood and stone.
text.NABRE: 28:64 The LORD will scatter you among all the peoples from one end of the earth to the other, and there you will serve other gods, of wood and stone, which you and your ancestors have not known.

text.NRSV: Among those nations you shall find no ease, no resting place for the sole of your foot. There the Lord will give you a trembling heart, failing eyes, and a languishing spirit.
text.Rotherham: And among those nations, shalt thou find no ease, neither shall there be a place of rest for the sole of thy foot,—but Yahweh will give unto thee there a trembling heart, and a failing of eyes and faintness of soul.
text.KJV: 28:65 And among these nations shalt thou finde no ease, neither shall the sole of thy foote haue rest: but the Lord shall giue thee there a trembling heart, and failing of eyes, & sorrow of minde.
text.NABRE: 28:65 Among these nations you will find no rest, not even a resting place for the sole of your foot, for there the LORD will give you an anguished heart and wearied eyes and a trembling spirit.

text.NRSV: Your life shall hang in doubt before you; night and day you shall be in dread, with no assurance of your life.
text.Rotherham: And thy life will be hung up for thee in front,—and thou wilt be in dread by night and by day, and wilt not trust in thy life.
text.KJV: 28:66 And thy life shall hang in doubt before thee, and thou shalt feare day and night, and shalt haue none assurance of thy life.
text.NABRE: 28:66 Your life will hang in suspense and you will stand in dread both day and night, never sure of your life.

text.NRSV: In the morning you shall say, “If only it were evening!” and at evening you shall say, “If only it were morning!” —because of the dread that your heart shall feel and the sights that your eyes shall see.
text.Rotherham: In the morning, thou wilt say—Oh that it were evening! and in the evening, thou wilt say—Oh that it were morning! because of the dread of thy heart which thou wilt dread, and because of the sight of thine eyes which thou wilt see.
text.KJV: 28:67 In the morning thou shalt say, Would God it were Euen: and at Euen thou shalt say, Would God it were morning, for the feare of thine heart wherewith thou shalt feare, and for the sight of thine eyes which thou shalt see.
text.NABRE: 28:67 In the morning you will say, “Would that it were evening!” and in the evening you will say, “Would that it were morning!” because of the dread that your heart must feel and the sight that your eyes must see.

text.NRSV: The Lord will bring you back in ships to Egypt, by a route that I promised you would never see again; and there you shall offer yourselves for sale to your enemies as male and female slaves, but there will be no buyer.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh will take thee back again to Egypt in ships, by the way whereof I said unto thee, Thou shalt not again any more see it. And ye will offer yourselves there for sale unto thine enemies as servants and as handmaids with no one to buy.
text.KJV: 28:68 And the Lord shall bring thee into Egypt againe, with ships, by the way whereof I spake vnto thee, Thou shalt see it no more againe: and there ye shall bee sold vnto your enemies for bondmen, and bondwomen, and no man shall buy you.
text.NABRE: 28:68 The LORD will send you back in ships to Egypt, by a route which I told you that you would never see again; and there you will offer yourselves for sale to your enemies as male and female slaves, but there will be no buyer.

text.NRSV: These are the words of the covenant that the Lord commanded Moses to make with the Israelites in the land of Moab, in addition to the covenant that he had made with them at Horeb.
text.Rotherham: These, are the words of the covenant which Yahweh commanded Moses to solemnise with the sons of Israel in the land of Moab,—besides the covenant, which he solemnised with them in Horeb.
text.KJV: 29:1 These are the woordes of the Couenant which the Lord commanded Moses to make with the children of Israel in the land of Moab, beside the Couenant which he made with them in Horeb.
text.NABRE: 29:1 III. Third Address Chapter 29 - Past Favors Recalled. Moses summoned all Israel and said to them, You have seen with your own eyes all that the LORD did in the land of Egypt to Pharaoh and all his servants and to all his land,

text.NRSV: Moses summoned all Israel and said to them: You have seen all that the Lord did before your eyes in the land of Egypt, to Pharaoh and to all his servants and to all his land,
text.Rotherham: And Moses called unto all Israel and said unto them,—Ye yourselves, saw all that Yahweh did before your eyes in the land of Egypt, unto Pharaoh and unto all his servants and unto all his land:
text.KJV: 29:2 ¶ And Moses called vnto all Israel, and said vnto them, Yee haue seene all that the Lord did before your eyes in the land of Egypt vnto Pharaoh, and vnto all his seruants, and vnto all his land;
text.NABRE: 29:2 the great testings your own eyes have seen, and those great signs and wonders.

text.NRSV: the great trials that your eyes saw, the signs, and those great wonders.
text.Rotherham: the great provings which thine own eyes saw,—those great signs and wonders:
text.KJV: 29:3 The great temptations which thine eyes haue seene, the signes and those great miracles:
text.NABRE: 29:3 But the LORD has not given you a heart to understand, or eyes to see, or ears to hear until this day.

text.NRSV: But to this day the Lord has not given you a mind to understand, or eyes to see, or ears to hear.
text.Rotherham: yet hath Yahweh not given onto you a heart to know, or eyes to see or ears to hear,—until this day.
text.KJV: 29:4 Yet the Lord hath not giuen you an heart to perceiue, and eyes to see, and eares to heare, vnto this day.
text.NABRE: 29:4 I led you for forty years in the wilderness. Your clothes did not fall from you in tatters nor your sandals from your feet;

text.NRSV: I have led you forty years in the wilderness. The clothes on your back have not worn out, and the sandals on your feet have not worn out;
text.Rotherham: I led you, therefore forty years in the desert,—your mantles fell not with age, from off you, even thy sandal, fell not with age from off thy foot:
text.KJV: 29:5 And I haue led you fourtie yeres in the wildernes: your clothes are not waxen old vpon you, and thy shooe is not waxen old vpon thy foot.
text.NABRE: 29:5 it was not bread that you ate, nor wine or beer that you drank—so that you might know that I, the LORD, am your God.

text.NRSV: you have not eaten bread, and you have not drunk wine or strong drink—so that you may know that I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Bread, ye did not eat, and wine and strong drink, ye did not drink,—that ye might know that, I, Yahweh, was your God.
text.KJV: 29:6 Ye haue not eaten bread, neither haue you drunke wine, or strong drink: that yee might knowe that I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 29:6 When you came to this place, Sihon, king of Heshbon, and Og, king of Bashan, came out to engage us in battle, but we defeated them

text.NRSV: When you came to this place, King Sihon of Heshbon and King Og of Bashan came out against us for battle, but we defeated them.
text.Rotherham: And when ye entered into this place, then came forth Sihon king of Heshbon and Og king of Bashan to meet us in battle and we smote them;
text.KJV: 29:7 And when yee came vnto this place, Sihon the king of Heshbon, and Og the King of Bashan, came out against vs vnto battell, and wee smote them.
text.NABRE: 29:7 and took their land, and gave it as a heritage to the Reubenites, Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh.

text.NRSV: We took their land and gave it as an inheritance to the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh.
text.Rotherham: and we took their land, and gave it for an inheritance unto the Reubenites and unto the Gadites,—and unto the half tribe of Manasseh.
text.KJV: 29:8 And wee tooke their lande, and gaue it for an inheritance vnto the Reubenites, and to the Gadites, and to the halfe tribe of Manasseh.
text.NABRE: 29:8 Observe carefully the words of this covenant, therefore, in order that you may succeed in whatever you do.

text.NRSV: Therefore diligently observe the words of this covenant, in order that you may succeed in everything that you do.
text.Rotherham: Ye must, therefore, keep the words of this covenant, and do them,—that ye may prosper in all that ye do.
text.KJV: 29:9 Keepe therefore the wordes of this Couenant and doe them, that yee may prosper in all that ye doe.
text.NABRE: 29:9 All Israel Bound by Covenant. You are standing today, all of you, in the presence of the LORD, your God—your tribal heads, elders, and officials, all of the men of Israel,

text.NRSV: You stand assembled today, all of you, before the Lord your God—the leaders of your tribes, your elders, and your officials, all the men of Israel,
text.Rotherham: Ye are stationed, to-day, all of you, before Yahweh your God,—your heads your tribes, and your elders and your officers, every man of Israel;
text.KJV: 29:10 ¶ Ye stand this day all of you before the Lord your God: your captaines of your tribes, your Elders, and your officers, with all the men of Israel,
text.NABRE: 29:10 your children, your wives, and the resident alien who lives in your camp, from those who cut wood to those who draw water for you—

text.NRSV: your children, your women, and the aliens who are in your camp, both those who cut your wood and those who draw your water—
text.Rotherham: your little ones your wives, and thy sojourner that is in the midst of thy camps,—from him that heweth thy wood unto him that draweth thy water:
text.KJV: 29:11 Your litle ones, your wiues, and thy stranger that is in thy campe, from the hewer of thy wood, vnto the drawer of thy water:
text.NABRE: 29:11 to enter into the covenant of the LORD, your God, which the LORD, your God, is making with you today, with its curse,

text.NRSV: to enter into the covenant of the Lord your God, sworn by an oath, which the Lord your God is making with you today;
text.Rotherham: that thou mayest pass through into the covenant of Yahweh thy God and into his oath,—which Yahweh thy God is solemnising with thee, to-day:
text.KJV: 29:12 That thou shouldest enter into Couenant with the Lord thy God, and into his othe which the Lord thy God maketh with thee this day:
text.NABRE: 29:12 so that he may establish you today as his people and he may be your God, as he promised you and as he swore to your ancestors, to Abraham, Isaac and Jacob.

text.NRSV: in order that he may establish you today as his people, and that he may be your God, as he promised you and as he swore to your ancestors, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.
text.Rotherham: that he may confirm thee to-day as his own people, while, he, becometh thine own God, as he spake unto thee,—and as he sware unto thy fathers, unto Abraham unto Isaac and unto Jacob.
text.KJV: 29:13 That he may establish thee to day for a people vnto himselfe, and that hee may be vnto thee a God, as he hath said vnto thee, and as he hath sworne vnto thy fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Iacob.
text.NABRE: 29:13 But it is not with you alone that I am making this covenant, with its curse,

text.NRSV: I am making this covenant, sworn by an oath, not only with you who stand here with us today before the Lord our God,
text.Rotherham: And not with you alone, am I solemnising this covenant and this oath;
text.KJV: 29:14 Neither with you onely doe I make this couenant and this othe:
text.NABRE: 29:14 but with those who are standing here with us today in the presence of the LORD, our God, and with those who are not here with us today.

text.NRSV: but also with those who are not here with us today.
text.Rotherham: but with him who is here with us, standing to-day before Yahweh our God,—and with him who is not here with us to-day.
text.KJV: 29:15 But with him that standeth here with vs this day before the Lord our God, and also with him that is not here with vs this day:
text.NABRE: 29:15 Warning Against Idolatry. You know that we lived in the land of Egypt and that we passed through the nations, that you too passed through

text.NRSV: You know how we lived in the land of Egypt, and how we came through the midst of the nations through which you passed.
text.Rotherham: (For, ye, know, how we dwelt in the land of Egypt,—and how we passed through the midst of the nations which ye did pass through;
text.KJV: 29:16 (For ye know how we haue dwelt in the land of Egypt, and how we came thorow the nations which ye passed by.
text.NABRE: 29:16 and saw the loathsome things and idols of wood and stone, of gold and silver, that they possess.

text.NRSV: You have seen their detestable things, the filthy idols of wood and stone, of silver and gold, that were among them.
text.Rotherham: and ye saw their disgusting images, and their manufactured gods,—the wood and the stone, the silver and the gold that are with them.)
text.KJV: 29:17 And ye haue seene their abominations, and their idoles, wood, and stone, siluer, and gold, which were among them.)
text.NABRE: 29:17 There may be among you a man or woman, or a clan or tribe, whose heart is now turning away from the LORD, our God, to go and serve the gods of these nations; there may be among you a root bearing poison and wormwood;

text.NRSV: It may be that there is among you a man or woman, or a family or tribe, whose heart is already turning away from the Lord our God to serve the gods of those nations. It may be that there is among you a root sprouting poisonous and bitter growth.
text.Rotherham: Lest there be among you—a man or a woman or a family or a tribe, whose heart is turning to-day, from Yahweh our God, to go and serve the gods of those nations,—lest there be among you a root fruitful of poison and wormwood;
text.KJV: 29:18 Lest there should be among you man or woman, or familie, or tribe, whose heart turneth away this day frō the Lord our God, to goe and serue the gods of these nations: lest there should bee among you a root that beareth gall and wormewood,
text.NABRE: 29:18 if any such persons, after hearing the words of this curse, should congratulate themselves, saying in their hearts, “I am safe, even though I walk in stubbornness of heart,” thereby sweeping away moist and dry alike,

text.NRSV: All who hear the words of this oath and bless themselves, thinking in their hearts, “We are safe even though we go our own stubborn ways” (thus bringing disaster on moist and dry alike)—
text.Rotherham: and so it come to pass while he is hearing the words of this oath, that he will bless himself in his heart—saying, Prosperity, shall I have, although in the stubbornness of my heart, I go on,—so that the drunkenness addeth to the thirst:
text.KJV: 29:19 And it come to passe when he heareth the wordes of this curse, that hee blesse himselfe in his heart, saying, I shall haue peace, though I walke in the imagination of mine heart, to adde drunkennesse to thirst:
text.NABRE: 29:19 the LORD will never consent to pardon them. Instead, the LORD’s burning wrath will flare up against them; every curse written in this book will pounce on them, and the LORD will blot out their names from under the heavens.

text.NRSV: the Lord will be unwilling to pardon them, for the Lord’s anger and passion will smoke against them. All the curses written in this book will descend on them, and the Lord will blot out their names from under heaven.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh will not be willing to forgive him, but, then, will the anger of Yahweh and his jealousy smoke against that man, and all the oath that is written in this scroll, shall settle down upon him,—and Yahweh wipe out his name from under the heavens;
text.KJV: 29:20 The Lord wil not spare him, but then the anger of the Lord, and his ielousie shall smoke against that man, and all the curses that are written in this booke shall lie vpon him, and the Lord shall blot out his name from vnder heauen.
text.NABRE: 29:20 The LORD will single them out from all the tribes of Israel for doom, in keeping with all the curses of the covenant written in this book of the law.

text.NRSV: The Lord will single them out from all the tribes of Israel for calamity, in accordance with all the curses of the covenant written in this book of the law.
text.Rotherham: and Yahweh single him out for calamity, out of all the tribes of Israel,—according to all the oaths of the covenant that is written in this scroll of the law.
text.KJV: 29:21 And the Lord shall separate him vnto euill, out of all the tribes of Israel, according to all the curses of the Couenant, that are written in this booke of the Law:
text.NABRE: 29:21 Punishment for Idolatry. Future generations, your descendants who will rise up after you, as well as the foreigners who will come here from distant lands, when they see the calamities of this land and the ills the LORD has inflicted upon it—

text.NRSV: The next generation, your children who rise up after you, as well as the foreigner who comes from a distant country, will see the devastation of that land and the afflictions with which the Lord has afflicted it—
text.Rotherham: So that the generation coming after—your sons who shall rise up after you, and the foreigner who shall come in from a far-off land, will say—when they see the plagues of that land and the sicknesses thereof wherewith Yahweh hath made it sick:
text.KJV: 29:22 So that the generation to come of your children, that shall rise vp after you, and the stranger that shall come from a farre land, shall say, when they see the plagues of that land, and the sicknesses which the Lord hath layd vpon it;
text.NABRE: 29:22 all its soil burned out by sulphur and salt, unsown and unfruitful, without a blade of grass, like the catastrophe of Sodom and Gomorrah, Admah and Zeboiim, which the LORD overthrew in his furious wrath—

text.NRSV: all its soil burned out by sulfur and salt, nothing planted, nothing sprouting, unable to support any vegetation, like the destruction of Sodom and Gomorrah, Admah and Zeboiim, which the Lord destroyed in his fierce anger—
text.Rotherham: brimstone and salt burning up all the land thereof, it shall not be sown neither shall it shoot forth neither shall there come up thereon any herbage,—like the overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah, Admah and Zeboiim, which Yahweh overthrow in his anger and in his wrath:
text.KJV: 29:23 And that the whole land thereof is brimstone and salt, and burning, that it is not sowen, nor beareth, nor any grasse groweth therein, like the ouerthrow of Sodome, and Gomorah, Admah, and Zeboim, which the Lord ouerthrew in his anger, and in his wrath:
text.NABRE: 29:23 they and all the nations will ask, “Why has the LORD dealt thus with this land? Why this great outburst of wrath?”

text.NRSV: they and indeed all the nations will wonder, “Why has the Lord done thus to this land? What caused this great display of anger?”
text.Rotherham: yea all the nations will say, For what cause, hath Yahweh done, thus, unto this land? what meaneth this great heat of anger?
text.KJV: 29:24 Euen al nations shal say, Wherefore hath the Lord done thus vnto this land? what meaneth the heat of this great anger?
text.NABRE: 29:24 And they will say, “Because they abandoned the covenant of the LORD, the God of their ancestors, which he had made with them when he brought them out of the land of Egypt,

text.NRSV: They will conclude, “It is because they abandoned the covenant of the Lord, the God of your ancestors, which he made with them when he brought them out of the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Then will men say, Because they forsook the covenant of Yahweh the God of their fathers,—which he solemnised with them, when he brought them forth out of the land of Egypt;
text.KJV: 29:25 Then men shall say, Because they haue forsaken the Couenant of the Lord God of their fathers, which he made with them when he brought them foorth out of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: 29:25 and they went and served other gods and bowed down to them, gods whom they did not know and whom he had not apportioned to them.

text.NRSV: They turned and served other gods, worshiping them, gods whom they had not known and whom he had not allotted to them;
text.Rotherham: and went and served other gods, and bowed down to them,—gods which they had not known, and in which he had given them no share:
text.KJV: 29:26 For they went and serued other gods, & worshipped them, gods whom they knew not, and whom he had not giuen vnto them.
text.NABRE: 29:26 So the anger of the LORD flared up against this land and brought on it every curse written in this book.

text.NRSV: so the anger of the Lord was kindled against that land, bringing on it every curse written in this book.
text.Rotherham: therefore did the anger of Yahweh kindle upon that land,—to bring in upon it all the curse that is written in this scroll;
text.KJV: 29:27 And the anger of the Lord was kindled against this land, to bring vpon it all the curses, that are written in this booke.
text.NABRE: 29:27 The LORD uprooted them from their soil in anger, fury, and great wrath, and cast them out into another land, as they are today.”

text.NRSV: The Lord uprooted them from their land in anger, fury, and great wrath, and cast them into another land, as is now the case.”
text.Rotherham: and therefore hath Yahweh rooted them out, from off their own soil, in anger and in wrath and in great indignation,—and cast them into another land as at this day.
text.KJV: 29:28 And the Lord rooted them out of their land, in anger and in wrath, and in great indignation, and cast them into another land, as it is this day.
text.NABRE: 29:28 The hidden things belong to the LORD our God, but the revealed things are for us and for our children forever, to observe all the words of this law.

text.NRSV: The secret things belong to the Lord our God, but the revealed things belong to us and to our children forever, to observe all the words of this law.
text.Rotherham: The secret things, [belong unto Yahweh our God] and, the things that are revealed, belong to us and to our children unto times age-abiding, that we may do all the words of this law.
text.KJV: 29:29 The secret things belong vnto the Lord our God: but those things which are reuealed belong vnto vs, and to our children for euer, that wee may doe all the words of this Law.

text.NRSV: When all these things have happened to you, the blessings and the curses that I have set before you, if you call them to mind among all the nations where the Lord your God has driven you,
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass, when all these things shall come in upon thee—the blessing and the curse, which I have set before thee,—and thou shalt bring them back unto thy heart among all the nations whither Yahweh thy God hath driven thee;
text.KJV: 30:1 And it shall come to passe when all these things are come vpon thee, the blessing, and the curse, which I haue set before thee, and thou shalt call them to minde among all the nations whither the Lord thy God hath driuen thee,
text.NABRE: 30:1 Chapter 30 - Compassion for the Repentant. When all these things, the blessing and the curse which I have set before you, come upon you, and you take them to heart in any of the nations where the LORD, your God, has dispersed you,

text.NRSV: and return to the Lord your God, and you and your children obey him with all your heart and with all your soul, just as I am commanding you today,
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt return unto Yahweh thy God and shalt hearken unto his voice, according to all that I am commanding thee to-day,—thou and thy sons, with all thy heart, and with all thy soul,
text.KJV: 30:2 And shalt returne vnto the Lord thy God, and shalt obey his voyce according to all that I command thee this day, thou and thy children with al thine heart, and with all thy soule:
text.NABRE: 30:2 and return to the LORD, your God, obeying his voice, according to all that I am commanding you today, you and your children, with your whole heart and your whole being,

text.NRSV: then the Lord your God will restore your fortunes and have compassion on you, gathering you again from all the peoples among whom the Lord your God has scattered you.
text.Rotherham: then will Yahweh thy God bring back thy captivity and have compassion upon thee,—and return and gather thee from among all the peoples whither Yahweh thy God hath scattered thee.
text.KJV: 30:3 That then the Lord thy God will turne thy captiuitie, and haue compassion vpon thee, and wil returne and gather thee from all the nations whither the Lord thy God hath scattered thee.
text.NABRE: 30:3 the LORD, your God, will restore your fortunes and will have compassion on you; he will again gather you from all the peoples where the LORD, your God, has scattered you.

text.NRSV: Even if you are exiled to the ends of the world, from there the Lord your God will gather you, and from there he will bring you back.
text.Rotherham: Though thou be driven out unto the utmost part of the heavens, from thence, will Yahweh thy God gather thee, and, from thence will he fetch thee;
text.KJV: 30:4 If any of thine be driuen out vnto the outmost parts of heauen, from thence will the Lord thy God gather thee, and from thence will he fetch thee.
text.NABRE: 30:4 Though you may have been dispersed to the farthest corner of the heavens, even from there will the LORD, your God, gather you; even from there will he bring you back.

text.NRSV: The Lord your God will bring you into the land that your ancestors possessed, and you will possess it; he will make you more prosperous and numerous than your ancestors.
text.Rotherham: and Yahweh thy God will bring thee into the land which thy fathers possessed, and thou shalt possess it,—and he will do thee good and multiply thee beyond thy fathers.
text.KJV: 30:5 And the Lord thy God will bring thee into the land which thy fathers possessed, and thou shalt possesse it: and he will doe thee good, and multiply thee aboue thy fathers.
text.NABRE: 30:5 The LORD, your God, will then bring you into the land your ancestors once possessed, that you may possess it; and he will make you more prosperous and numerous than your ancestors.

text.NRSV: Moreover, the Lord your God will circumcise your heart and the heart of your descendants, so that you will love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul, in order that you may live.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh thy God will circumcise thy heart, and the heart of thy seed,—to love Yahweh thy God with all thy heart and with all thy soul, that thou mayest live.
text.KJV: 30:6 And the Lord thy God will circumcise thine heart, and the heart of thy seed, to loue the Lord thy God with all thine heart, and with all thy soule, that thou mayest liue.
text.NABRE: 30:6 The LORD, your God, will circumcise your hearts and the hearts of your descendants, so that you will love the LORD, your God, with your whole heart and your whole being, in order that you may live.

text.NRSV: The Lord your God will put all these curses on your enemies and on the adversaries who took advantage of you.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh thy God will put all these oaths upon thine enemies and upon them who hated thee who persecuted thee.
text.KJV: 30:7 And the Lord thy God will put all these curses vpon thine enemies, and on them that hate thee, which persecuted thee.
text.NABRE: 30:7 The LORD, your God, will put all those curses on your enemies and the foes who pursued you.

text.NRSV: Then you shall again obey the Lord, observing all his commandments that I am commanding you today,
text.Rotherham: But, thou, wilt return, and hearken unto the voice of Yahweh,—and wilt do all his commandments, which I am commanding thee, to-day,
text.KJV: 30:8 And thou shalt returne and obey the voice of the Lord, and doe all his Commandements which I command thee this day.
text.NABRE: 30:8 You, however, shall again obey the voice of the LORD and observe all his commandments which I am giving you today.

text.NRSV: and the Lord your God will make you abundantly prosperous in all your undertakings, in the fruit of your body, in the fruit of your livestock, and in the fruit of your soil. For the Lord will again take delight in prospering you, just as he delighted in prospering your ancestors,
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh thy God will make thee pre-eminent, in every work of thy hand—in the fruit of thy body, and in the fruit of thy cattle and in the fruit of thy ground, for good,—for Yahweh will again rejoice over thee for good, as he rejoiced over thy fathers:
text.KJV: 30:9 And the Lord thy God will make thee plenteous in euery worke of thine hand, in the fruit of thy body, and in the fruit of thy cattell, and in the fruit of thy land, for good: for the Lord will againe reioyce ouer thee for good, as he reioyced ouer thy fathers:
text.NABRE: 30:9 Then the LORD, your God, will generously increase your undertakings, the fruit of your womb, the offspring of your livestock, and the produce of your soil; for the LORD, your God, will again take delight in your prosperity, just as he took delight in your ancestors’,

text.NRSV: when you obey the Lord your God by observing his commandments and decrees that are written in this book of the law, because you turn to the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul.
text.Rotherham: if thou hearken unto the voice of Yahweh thy God, to keep his commandments, and his statutes, which are written in this scroll of the law,—if thou return unto Yahweh thy God, with all thy heart and with all thy soul.
text.KJV: 30:10 If thou shalt hearken vnto the voyce of the Lord thy God to keepe his Commandements, and his Statutes which are written in this booke of the Law, and if thou turne vnto the Lord thy God with all thine heart, and with all thy soule.
text.NABRE: 30:10 because you will obey the voice of the LORD, your God, keeping the commandments and statutes that are written in this book of the law, when you return to the LORD, your God, with your whole heart and your whole being.

text.NRSV: Surely, this commandment that I am commanding you today is not too hard for you, nor is it too far away.
text.Rotherham: For as touching this commandment, which I am commanding thee to-day, it is not, too wonderful, for thee, neither is it far off.
text.KJV: 30:11 ¶ For this Commaundement which I command thee this day, it is not hidden from thee, neither is it farre off.
text.NABRE: 30:11 For this command which I am giving you today is not too wondrous or remote for you.

text.NRSV: It is not in heaven, that you should say, “Who will go up to heaven for us, and get it for us so that we may hear it and observe it?”
text.Rotherham: It is, not in the heavens,—that thou shouldest say—Who shall ascend for us into the heavens that he may fetch it for us, that we may hear it, and do it?
text.KJV: 30:12 It is not in heauen, that thou shouldest say, Who shal goe vp for vs to heauen, and bring it vnto vs, that wee may heare it, and doe it?
text.NABRE: 30:12 It is not in the heavens, that you should say, “Who will go up to the heavens to get it for us and tell us of it, that we may do it?”

text.NRSV: Neither is it beyond the sea, that you should say, “Who will cross to the other side of the sea for us, and get it for us so that we may hear it and observe it?”
text.Rotherham: Neither is it, over the sea,—that thou shouldest say, Who will pass over for us to the other side of the sea, that he may fetch it for us, that we may hear it, and do it?
text.KJV: 30:13 Neither is it beyond the sea, that thou shouldest say, Who shall goe ouer the sea for vs, and bring it vnto vs, that we may heare it, and doe it?
text.NABRE: 30:13 Nor is it across the sea, that you should say, “Who will cross the sea to get it for us and tell us of it, that we may do it?”

text.NRSV: No, the word is very near to you; it is in your mouth and in your heart for you to observe.
text.Rotherham: But, near unto thee, is the word, exceedingly,—in thy mouth and in thy heart, that thou mayest do it.
text.KJV: 30:14 But the word is very nigh vnto thee, in thy mouth, and in thy heart, that thou mayest doe it.
text.NABRE: 30:14 No, it is something very near to you, in your mouth and in your heart, to do it.

text.NRSV: See, I have set before you today life and prosperity, death and adversity.
text.Rotherham: See! I have set before thee, to-day, life and prosperity,—and death and calamity;
text.KJV: 30:15 ¶ See, I haue set before thee this day, life and good, and death, and euill:
text.NABRE: 30:15 The Choice Before Israel. See, I have today set before you life and good, death and evil.

text.NRSV: If you obey the commandments of the Lord your God that I am commanding you today, by loving the Lord your God, walking in his ways, and observing his commandments, decrees, and ordinances, then you shall live and become numerous, and the Lord your God will bless you in the land that you are entering to possess.
text.Rotherham: for thou must keep the commandments of Yahweh thy God which I am commanding thee to-day, to love Yahweh thy God, to walk in his ways, and to keep his commandments and his statutes, and his regulations,—so shalt thou live and multiply, and Yahweh thy God will bless thee, in the land which thou art entering to possess.
text.KJV: 30:16 In that I command thee this day to loue the Lord thy God, to walke in his wayes, and to keepe his Commandements, and his Statutes, and his Iudgements, that thou maiest liue and multiply: and the Lord thy God shall blesse thee in the land, whither thou goest to possesse it.
text.NABRE: 30:16 If you obey the commandments of the LORD, your God, which I am giving you today, loving the LORD, your God, and walking in his ways, and keeping his commandments, statutes and ordinances, you will live and grow numerous, and the LORD, your God, will bless you in the land you are entering to possess.

text.NRSV: But if your heart turns away and you do not hear, but are led astray to bow down to other gods and serve them,
text.Rotherham: But, if thy heart shall turn away, and thou wilt, not hearken,—but shalt be drawn away, and shalt bow thyself down to other gods and serve them,
text.KJV: 30:17 But if thine heart turne away, so that thou wilt not heare, but shalt bee drawen away, and worship other gods and serue them:
text.NABRE: 30:17 If, however, your heart turns away and you do not obey, but are led astray and bow down to other gods and serve them,

text.NRSV: I declare to you today that you shall perish; you shall not live long in the land that you are crossing the Jordan to enter and possess.
text.Rotherham: I declare unto you, to-day, that ye shall perish,—ye shall not prolong your days, upon the soil which thou art passing over the Jordan to enter and possess.
text.KJV: 30:18 I denounce vnto you this day, that ye shall surely perish, and that yee shall not prolong your dayes vpon the land, whither thou passest ouer Iordan, to goe to possesse it.
text.NABRE: 30:18 I tell you today that you will certainly perish; you will not have a long life on the land which you are crossing the Jordan to enter and possess.

text.NRSV: I call heaven and earth to witness against you today that I have set before you life and death, blessings and curses. Choose life so that you and your descendants may live,
text.Rotherham: I call to witness against you to-day, the heavens and the earth, that life and death, have I set before thee, the blessing and the curse,—therefore shouldest thou choose life, that thou mayest live, thou and thy seed;
text.KJV: 30:19 I call heauen and earth to record this day against you, that I haue set before you life and death, blessing and cursing: therefore choose life, that both thou and thy seed may liue:
text.NABRE: 30:19 I call heaven and earth today to witness against you: I have set before you life and death, the blessing and the curse. Choose life, then, that you and your descendants may live,

text.NRSV: loving the Lord your God, obeying him, and holding fast to him; for that means life to you and length of days, so that you may live in the land that the Lord swore to give to your ancestors, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.
text.Rotherham: to love Yahweh thy God, to hearken unto his voice, and to cleave unto him,—for, he, is thy life, and thy length of days, that thou mayest abide upon the soil which Yahweh sware to thy fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac and to Jacob, to give unto them.
text.KJV: 30:20 That thou maiest loue the Lord thy God, and that thou mayest obey his voyce, and that thou mayest cleaue vnto him: for he is thy life, and the length of thy dayes, that thou mayest dwell in the land, which the Lord sware vnto thy fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Iacob, to giue them.
text.NABRE: 30:20 by loving the LORD, your God, obeying his voice, and holding fast to him. For that will mean life for you, a long life for you to live on the land which the LORD swore to your ancestors, to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, to give to them.

text.NRSV: When Moses had finished speaking all these words to all Israel,
text.Rotherham: And Moses went, and spake these words unto all Israel;
text.KJV: 31:1 And Moses went & spake these wordes vnto all Israel.
text.NABRE: 31:1 Chapter 31 - The Lord’s Leadership. When Moses had finished speaking these words to all Israel,

text.NRSV: he said to them: “I am now one hundred twenty years old. I am no longer able to get about, and the Lord has told me, ‘You shall not cross over this Jordan.’
text.Rotherham: and said unto them—A hundred and twenty years old, am I to-day, I can no more go out and come in,—Yahweh indeed hath said unto me, Thou shall not pass over this Jordan.
text.KJV: 31:2 And hee saide vnto them, I am an hundred and twentie yeeres old this day; I can no more goe out and come in: also the Lord hath said vnto mee, Thou shalt not goe ouer this Iordan.
text.NABRE: 31:2 he said to them, I am now one hundred and twenty years old and am no longer able to go out and come in; besides, the LORD has said to me, Do not cross this Jordan.

text.NRSV: The Lord your God himself will cross over before you. He will destroy these nations before you, and you shall dispossess them. Joshua also will cross over before you, as the Lord promised.
